name: Aberrant Maxine
locations: Novus: La Ville Rose - Maxine's Office

name: Aberrant Paradox
cheats: If a Wizard joins the duel late, Aberrant Paradox will cast a single-target version of Power Nova for that Wizard's maximum Health, ignoring all Resist and all Wards. This occurs at the beginning of the Round.

At the beginning of the first Round, the Divine Paradox in the background will cast a random School Global Spell. These Global Spells boost the Damage of their School and weaken the Damage of the opposite School. The only exception is the Balance School, which weakens the damage of all other Schools. The first Global Spell lasts two Rounds. All others last three Rounds.

When this Global Spell is cast, all Wizards will receive a card related to the School of the Global Spell in their hand that cannot be discarded. Reshuffle does not remove them. These cards either help the enemy team or hurt the Wizard's team.
Changing the Global Spell will make the Abberant Paradox recast the previous Global Spell that was in effect.
If the Global spell is changed by an enemy spell, such as Gaze of Fate, the Global will not be recast.
Fire Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Smoke Screen][Anti-Steal Charm][Anti-Scimitars]
Ice Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Anti-Tower Shield][Cursed Taunt][Anti-Climaclysm]
Storm Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Anti-Cleanse Charm][Anti-Disarm][Anti-Iron Sultan]
Myth Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Cleanse Ward][Cursed Stun][Anti-Snake Charmer]
Life Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Armor][Cursed Pigsie][Anti-Lamassu]
Death Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Infection][Cursed Juju][Qismah's Curse (2 Pip)]
Balance Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Balanceblade][Cursed Mass Hex][Anti-Sand Wurm]

If all Aberrations are not defeated before the Abberant Paradox, it will respawn with 5,775 Health. This also applies if the Wizard defeats the last Aberration on the turn the Abberant Paradox is defeated.
Defeating any of the initial Minion Aberrations in the specified slot will summon the following Minions in the same slot.
[Key] Aberration (Myth Elite) [Myth]
[Gem] Aberration (Death Elite) [Death]
[Spiral] Aberration (Life Elite) [Life]
Defeating any of the second Minion Aberrations in the specified slot will summon the following Minions in the same slot. No more will spawn after this.
[Key] Aberration (Shadow Elite) [Shadow]
[Gem] Aberration (Balance Elite) [Balance]
[Spiral] Aberration (Balance Elite) [Balance]
spellNotes: Casts Feint (Shadow) that applies a +70% Trap to all enemies and does not apply a Trap on Self.
Casts an All Enemies version of Poison (Shadow).
Casts a Tatzlewurm Terror (Shadow) that has a chance to Confuse Wizards for three Rounds.

name: Admiral Bontau
cheats: "Midnight, cloud their vision!" - During Round 1 (and every 3rd or 4th Round thereafter), Admiral Bontau's Pet will cast a -50% Death version of Black Mantle on a random Wizard.
"Midnight, bring them to their knees!" - Sometimes, if a Wizard casts a single-target attack at Admiral Bontau, then he will cast Virulent Plague (-45%).
"Midnight, weaken that one!" - Alternating with the first cheat, Admiral Bontau's Pet casts a Death version of Weakness on a random Wizard.

name: Admiral Dynt
locations: Empyrea: Nimbus Citadel - Control Room (Nimbus Citadel)
cheats: "Yar! What in the Seventy Skies Do You Scallywags Think You're Doing?!" - Boss will cast Grand Phoenix on Wizards who are late to join the duel.
"Good heads!" - At the end of each Round, Boss will say this and he casts one of the following Spells on the Wizard(s):
Bladestorm, Legion Shield (-30%), Precision, Rebirth, Spirit Armor (1,000), Sprite Swarm, Stunblock, Unicorn.
"Ye Barnacle-Brained Buffoons, Look at What Ye've Done!" At the end of the fifth Round, he will cast Wild Bolt on a random Wizard.
After casting Wild Bolt, Admiral Dynt uses Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps placed upon him.
After casting his Natural Attack, Admiral Dynt will receive a Blade based on which Natural Attack he used:
Admiral Dynt receives a +40% version of Fireblade after using his Fire Natural Attack.
Admiral Dynt receives a +30% version of Shadowblade after using his Shadow Natural Attack.
spellNotes: Casts an all-enemies version of Hex
Deals 120 Fire Damage per Pip for a maximum of 4 Pips
Deals 105 Shadow Damage per Pip for a maximum of 4 Pips

name: Adrok
locations: Empyrea: Aeriel Shores - Beastman Fight Club
cheats: Adrok
[Late] "I'm gonna set you straight for bein' late" - If a Wizard enters the battle late, Adrok will cheat-cast Rocklops that Stuns for two Rounds, and places a -100% Accuracy Charm on the Wizard.
[Accuracy] "Check your head!" - If Adrok or an ally fizzles, he will cast Lightning Strike on the fizzler.
[Curse] "Leave Adrok alone!" - If a harmful Charm is cast on Yowck, Adrok will cast Cleanse Charm (Star) to remove it.
[Damage] After casting his Natural Attack, Adrok will cheat-cast Healing Current (325) on a random ally.
Adrok's Interrupt Cycle
[Minion] "Feel the Beet!" - At the end of every Round that the Boss does not have a Minion, Adrok will use a Moon Spell to summon a Beets Minion.

[Round] "This gonna hurt!" - If a Wizard has not been defeated two Rounds after the Beets Minion is summoned, and no Trap has been placed on Adrok in the last two Rounds, Adrok casts a Spell to instantly defeat the Beets Minion and a random Wizard.
"Eh, the beets are mmm.... stopped" - This cycle can be reset by casting a Trap on Adrok.
Prisms will not reset this cycle.
Beets

name: Aethyr Elemental
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Northeast Aero Plains Stone Key Room
cheats: "You're Late" - Casts Lord of Winter (Shadow) on any Wizard who joins after the second round of the duel. Each Boss that is present will cast this cheat.
Casting a Feint Spell will trigger casting of a double Cleanse Ward, which removes the last two Traps placed on the Boss.
Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Casting a Heal Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Snow Drift.
Casting a Damage Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Mass Triage.
Phase 1

Casting a Charm or Global Spell triggers casting of a Mass version of Enfeeble (Moon). This is also triggered by casting a negative Charm on the Boss. This cheat only applies for the Phase 1 Boss. Charms are allowed in Phase 2 and Phase 3.
Aegis can protect one Blade Spell.
Phase 2
After being defeated, this Boss splits into two Aethyr Elemental (16,000) Bosses.
Phase 3
The Boss will split again into two Aethyr Elemental (8,000) Bosses per each Aethyr Elemental (16,000) that is defeated.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Tower Shield (-55%) and Icy Shadowblade.

Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
[Heal over Time] Casting a Heal Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Snow Drift.

name: Aethyr Titan
cheats: The Divine Paradox is in a Star School Polymorph throughout this duel, with the given Spells:
Fire Cat (0 Pip)
Headless Horseman (0 Pip)
Ice Wyvern (0 Pip)
Loremaster (0 Pip)
Minotaur (0 Pip)
Sacred Charge (0 Pip)
Stormzilla (99,999)
At the beginning of the duel, the Paradox Wizard casts a Star Spell which places an Aura that gives the Wizard masteries of all 7 schools (Balance, Fire, Ice, Storm, Death, Myth, and Life).
The Paradox Wizard and the Boss gain seven Power Pips every Round.
Henchmen summoned in this duel will also gain seven Power Pips every Round.
Global Spells will be removed if they are cast by Henchmen.
If the Wizard starts with or has low hit points, they will cast Dryad that restores them back to full Health.

When the Boss goes down to about 15% of his current Health, he restores his Health back to 100%, and invisible polymorphs the Paradox Wizard that lasts 99 Rounds, allowing to use either of the following Spells to finish the Boss:
Scion of Elements
Scion of Spirits
spellNotes: All Aethyr Titan's spells deal 9,999 Damage to target and cost 14 Pips.
These Shadow spells deal damage based on their School and are not Shadow Damage.
Frost Giant and Medusa only can be cast on the first turn.

name: Ali Baboon
locations: Mirage: Thieves' Den - Ali Baboon's Private Lounge
cheats: "You are late. How embarrassing" - If a Wizard is late to combat, he will interrupt-cast a Cloaked version of a negative Charm on all Wizards.
"Now, you made Ali Baboon angry" - At the beginning of Round 1, he will cast a Sun Spell that summons one of the following Gobblorean Guard Minions:
After defeating his first minion, he will cast a +45% in / +35% out version of Berserk. Berserk is only cast the first time a minion is defeated.
At the start of every Round that Ali doesn't have a minion he will resummon another one of a random type.
"Pick on Ali Baboon, not his men!" - If a Gobblorean Guard is attacked, he will cast an Aegis-protected Mander Armor and Healing Current on the Minion.
Hitting Ali with Spells that have an all enemies secondary effect, such as Winged Sorrow, will still activate this cheat.
"Here, consider this a gift" - After casting his Natural Attack, he will interrupt-cast a Cloaked version of a negative Charm on all Wizards.
Spell used to be a -55% version of Smoke Screen, but now the spell may be bugged.
"Such tricks won't work on me" - If a Feint is placed on Ali Baboon, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps from himself .
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent removal.

name: Alicane Swiftarrow
locations: Wizard City: Firecat Alley - Fireglobe Theatre

name: Alorna
locations: Polaris: River of Frozen Tears - Alorna's Cave
spellNotes: This Boss uses an advanced version of Cyclops Minion that summons a Rank 5 "Cyclops Guardian" with 800 Health.

name: Alpha Wolf
cheats: [Aura] "Don't even try to wear me down!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Alpha Wolf will cast a Domination Aura on himself for 99 Rounds that reduces all incoming Damage to 1.
To remove the Aura, Wizards must pass through three phases by placing the following hanging effects on Alpha Wolf:
Phase 1: Four Damage over Time effects
Phase 2: Four harmful Wards
Phase 3: Four harmful Charms

To move to the next phase, all four hanging effects must be active at the same time. If any of the necessary hanging effects are removed before all four are placed (ie.: the first Damage over Time expiring before a fourth can be placed), the phase will not change.
Attempting to remove this Aura without passing through the phases will cause Alpha Wolf to cast Exit Combat on the caster.
[Heal over Time] Phase 1:
"Ha! I supplement my Health!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Alpha Wolf will restore 11 Health to himself, and apply three Heal over Time effects that each restore 111 Health over 50 Rounds.
"You know, these little bites are annoying." - Whenever a Damage over Time effect is applied on Alpha Wolf during this phase, he will say this once.

"No... not my supplements!" - Once four or more Damage over Time effects are on Alpha Wolf at the same time, he will remove all hanging effects on himself and deal 25% of his max Health (4,051 Damage) to himself.
Removing the Heal over Time effects other than by placing four or more Damage over Times on Alpha Wolf will cause him to recast his Heal over Times.
[Shield] Phase 2:

"You won't trick me again! I'm too smart for any trap!" - After Alpha Wolf removes the three Heal over Time effects from himself, he will restore 11 Health to himself and apply three -50% Damage Wards on himself.
"Gah! Who put that there?" - Whenever a harmful Ward is applied on Alpha Wolf during this phase, he will say this once.

"Enough games! I'll just dominate you" - Once four or more harmful Wards are on Alpha Wolf at the same time, he will remove all hanging effects on himself and deal 25% of his max Health (4,051 Damage) to himself.
Removing the -50% Damage Wards other than by placing four or more harmful Wards on Alpha Wolf will cause him to recast his -50% Damage Wards.
[Blade] Phase 3:
"Weakling." - After Alpha Wolf removes the three -50% Damage Wards from himself, he will restore 11 Health to himself and apply three +50% Damage Charms on himself.
Despite their appearance, these Charms will not be used to increase the Damage of Alpha Wolf's attacks.
"Whoa! Where'd my muscles go?" - Whenever a harmful Charm is placed on Alpha Wolf during this phase, he will say this once.

"No! You found my only weakness! That's cheating!" - Once four or more harmful Charms are on Alpha Wolf at the same time, he will remove all hanging effects on himself and deal 25% of his max Health (4,051 Damage) to himself.
After the third phase is completed, Alpha Wolf will cast a Dominated Aura on himself which will allow Wizards to deal more than 1 Damage.

name: Alyssa Crowly
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor (Location) - Castle Darkmoor Dungeon
cheats: [Dispel] "Behold! The Mighty Magic of Beyond!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Alyssa Crowly will cast a Dispel Charm of the Wizard's School on each Wizard.
It has the visual appearance of a +100% Damage Charm of the Wizard's School.
"Why... isn't this working?" - Alyssa Crowly will say this if one of her Dispels are triggered.

name: Ammit the Devourer
locations: Krokotopia: Upper Zigazag - House of Scales
cheats: "Why don't we redirect all that potential?" - At the end of every Round, Ammit the Devourer will cast a Spell that deals 300 Death Damage to herself or one of her Pit Vipers, then Heal herself for 100% of the Damage dealt.
Negative Healing Charms do not affect this healing.
If all of the Pit Vipers have been defeated, this cheat will not be triggered.
"Ammit is the Judge of Souls now!" - At the beginning of every second Round, she will cast a 0-Pip Judgement on a random Wizard, which deals 1,000 Balance Damage.

"The Condemned Serve the Mighty Ammit! / Behold the Mighty Ranks of the Army of the Condemned!" -When a Pit Viper has low Health, it may cheat-cast Brilliant Light, followed by Ammit cheat-casting Sap Health on the Minion, which deals 2,735 Balance Damage.

"Ammit's Rebellion Will Never Fail! / Soldiers of the Condemned, Protect Your Queen!" -If a Pit Viper has low Health, it may cheat-cast Donate Power, followed by Ammit cheat-casting Take Power on the Minion, which deals 2,375 Fire Damage.
If a Wizard places a negative Charm on Ammit, she will cheat-cast Sacrifice on herself.
Unbalance does not trigger this cheat.
Target All Enemy Spells such as Plague and Virulent Plague do not trigger this cheat.
This cheat can also occur when a Spirit Shield is cast on Ammit. It's unclear what exact conditions are required. Death Prism and / or Feint seem to be related.

name: Anacaona Black Snake
locations: Azteca: Mangrove Marsh - Anacaona Black Snake's Tomb

name: Angrus Hollowsoul
locations: Wizard City: Colossus Boulevard - Briskbreeze Tower
cheats: At the start of Round 2, Angrus will interrupt to use Weakness on a random Wizard.
Although this Weakness is not cast, it can be dispelled.
At the end of every fourth Round, an Exploding Ember will join the duel and become active two Rounds later.
If Wizards flee and return, then the Exploding Ember(s) will remain.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Fire Damage per Pip.

name: Annoushka
cheats: [Damage] "Little doll fights you all. In the end you all will fall!" - At the end of the first Round, Annoushka will use a Moon school version of Poison that deals 50% max Health Damage to all Wizards and does not apply a Damage over Time.
[Round] "I have something for you. Try some!" - At the end of the second Round, and every Round after, Annoushka will cast a version of Poison that does regular Damage to all Wizards.

[Damage] [Polymorph] "Don't leave. Be like me. It's not so fun... is it?" - If two or more Wizards are present in combat and a Wizard is defeated in battle, Annoushka will revive, beguile, and polymorph that Wizard into a Doll for 99 Rounds.
If one Wizard is present in battle and has an active Minion when they are defeated, this cheat will trigger.
Minions cannot be Polymorphed by this cheat.

[Round] "All I've ever wanted was to destroy you!" - If a Wizard is currently polymorphed, Annoushka will activate this cheat. It is unclear what this cheat does and further testing will be necessary.

name: Ant Griffin
locations: Khrysalis: Crescent Beach - Ant Lion's Warren

name: Ant Lion
locations: Khrysalis: Crescent Beach - Ant Lion's Warren

name: Anubis
cheats: "Offer your Soul unto Death." - At the beginning of the first Round, if a Wizard has less than 100% Max Health, Anubis will cast a version of Blessing, restoring the Wizard to 100% max Health.
"Clench tight your fist with MAGIC!" - Anubis will say this at the end of the second Round.
"I shall feast on your final breath." - Each time a Wizard attacks Anubis after the second Round, he will give all Wizards a "Hunger of Anubis".
[Hunger of Anubis]
Power Cycle: Anubis will gain an Aura that decreases incoming Damage and decreases in power as Wizard's Health is lowered.
"Death demands tribute!" - If Anubis' Aura is removed, he will cast a version of Anubis on the Wizard that heals them for 4,800 Health, then recast the Aura.
"Let your spirit fade and feed my power." - If a Wizard is above 70% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -100%" -100% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
"Yes, embrace death." - If a Wizard is between 50% and 70% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -70%" -70% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
"Good. Your spirit will be delicious." - If a Wizard is between 20% and 50% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -50%" -50% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.

"Yes, I see your spirit is true.": After this cheat has been triggered, Anubis can now be defeated. If he is defeated before this, he will respawn with 114 Health. Only one Wizard must be below this threshold to be able to defeat Anubis.
If a Wizard is between 10% and 20% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -20%" -20% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
If a Wizard is below 10% max Health, Anubis will cast a +100% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.

name: Apep the Snaky One
locations: Krokotopia: Upper Zigazag - House of Scales
cheats: "Lets make this interesting." - At the beginning of the first Round, Apep will interrupt cast a Balance of Power.
"You'll have to do better than that!" - If a Wizard casts a Global Spell that gives a Damage bonus, such as Balefrost, he will immediately replace it back with Power Play.
Doom and Gloom, and Sanctuary will trigger this Cheat.
Power Play will not trigger this Cheat.
Damage Spells that cast a Global effect, such as White Rat Magician, will not trigger this Cheat.
Global Spells that give Critical and Armor Piercing effects, such as Katabatic Wind, will not trigger this Cheat.
Apep's Pit Viper Minions have 1,870 Health, instead of their normal 2,190 Health.They Boost to Fire, Ice, and Storm Damage, rather than their original Life, Myth, and Death Damage.

If a Pit Viper is defeated (or is missing at the start of the battle due to being defeated in an earlier attempt), Apep will summon a Minion with 1,150 Health. This Minion will attack for two Rounds, before Apep sacrifices it with Draw Health, Sap Power, Siphon Health, or Take Power.
When Apep uses one of his Minion Sacrifice Spells, he casts the Spell from first to last once. He then hidden casts the Spells two more times on each Minion.
When Dispelled once, it skips over the first Minion, but after others are sacrificed, the Minion he skipped over will still be sacrificed.
When with all his Minions, it takes 7 Dispels to save one, 8 to save 2, and 9 to save all 3.
When one Minion is in combat with two other Minions, it takes 3 Dispels to save him.
When two Minions are in combat with one other Minion, it takes 6 Dispels to save them.

The healing Spells will heal Apep even if the Minion he is trying to sacrifice is already dead (so if all three Pit Vipers have been defeated then he will be healed nine times). This may be a bug. The Pip-gaining Spells will only give Apep Pips once per Minion.
After one more Round, Apep will summon one of the following Minions (to be replaced in order):

cheats: At the beginning of Round 1 (and every fourth Round thereafter), Ares will interrupt-cast Amplify on himself.

DoT spells (such as Fire Elf) will not trigger this cheat constantly each round, and will only trigger it during the initial hit.
Frostbite triggers his cheat. (Needs further research.)
Steal spells will not trigger this cheat.
Pet spells will not trigger this cheat.
Drain spells will not trigger this cheat.

cheats: "The constellations form again!" - At the end of the first Round, Astraeus will summon three Ptolemos (Rank 6 Minion) Minions, which become active two Rounds later.
During the first Round, Astraeus will use his turn to cast a -90% Tower Shield on himself.

On occasion, he will not re-summon the Minions.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Pierce or Steal Ward can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield (-90%).

name: Balaur (Balance, Standard)
cheats: [Late] "Fool! You dare to sneak in late?" - If a wizard joins the battle late, Balaur (Ice-Storm) will cheat-cast a version of Mana Burn that removes all of the wizard's pips.
[Round] "The world turns to my will!" - At the start of combat, Balaur (Balance) designates himself as "active" and all other Balaurs as "inactive".
All spells can only target the "active" Balaur, including AOE spells.
"Inactive" Balaurs will always pass until they are able to use a 7 pip or stronger spell.
Once the "active" Balaur is defeated, the next Balaur in sequence will become "active". The order of Balaurs is as follows:
[Balance] Balaur (Balance, Standard)
[Life] [Myth] Balaur (Life-Myth, Standard)
[Death] [Fire] Balaur (Death-Fire, Standard)
[Ice] [Storm] Balaur (Ice-Storm, Standard)

[Afterlife] "Even death cannot fell me!" "The wheel of fortune turns without you" - When the "active" Balaur dies, the next Balaur in sequence will become "active" and begin its cheats. All cheats from the previous defeated Balaur will be removed.
[Feint] At the start of the round, if Balaur has a feint on it, it will remove the feint. Indemnity does not stop this cheat. Other traps are not affected.
[Global] "I am the emperor of this realm. I decide its state." - If a wizard casts a Global spell, the "active" Balaur will replace it with its own Global.
"Let Spring rejuvenate our strength" - Casts Balance of Power upon becoming "active".
If the Order Idol has been placed, this head will also cast Quicken on all Balaur heads, which will be removed once this head is defeated.
"The star itself will replenish my pips!" - At the end of the first Round and every 4 Rounds thereafter while it is "active", Balaur (Balance) gains seven Power Pips.
"Let Summer sear with the sun" - Casts Circle of Thorns upon becoming "active".
At the end of each Round while it is "active", Balaur (Life-Myth) places a -20% Damage Ward on itself. This shield increases by 20% potency after each cast. Once it reaches -100%, this cheat stops.
"Let Autumn balance the scales of justice" - Removes all helpful Charms and helpful Wards from all Wizards and casts Deadzone upon becoming "active".
"Let Winter's temperance wither you all" - Casts Mana Burn on all Wizards and casts Balefrost upon becoming "active".
"My power is towering, now for the destruction" - At the end of each Round while it is "active", Balaur (Ice-Storm) gains two Power Pips and a Pip.
spellNotes: Casts a Natural Attack for 7 Pips that deals 780 Balance Damage to all Wizards

name: Barbarous Van Der Borst (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: On the second Round, this Boss spawns his Minions.

name: Barbarous Van Der Borst (Tier 3)

The number of Minions summoned is equal to the number of Wizards engaged in the duel.

name: Bartleby Quietus
cheats: Cheat Cycle

[Round] "I won't let you destroy me!" - Every turn, Bartleby Quietus will cast a Spell that applies hanging effects corresponding to one of the six non-Balance Schools of Magic. Whenever there are enough of any hanging effect to trigger a Scion Gambit, he will cheat-cast the corresponding Scion Spell.
This cheat can trigger multiple times in the same Round.
Hanging Effect Counters
[Charm] Helpful Charms
If the Wizard casts a helpful Charm, Bartleby Quietus will remove all helpful Charms from the Wizard and do one of the following:
Applies two +30% Death Damage Charms on himself
Applies two -20% Damage Charms on the Wizard
Applies two +35% Ice Damage Wards on the Wizard
Applies two -40% Damage Wards on himself
[Curse] Harmful Charms
If the Wizard casts a harmful Charm, Bartleby Quietus will remove all harmful Charms from himself and do one of the following:
Applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 291 Myth Damage after 3 Rounds
Applies two +35% Myth Damage Wards on the Wizard
[Jinx] Harmful Wards
If the Wizard casts a harmful Ward, including Prisms, Bartleby Quietus will remove all harmful Wards from himself and do one of the following:
Applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 312 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds
Applies two +70% Damage over Time Wards on the Wizard
Applies two +30% Life Damage Charms on the Wizard (bugged)
Applies a Heal over Time effect on the Wizard that restores 321 Health over 3 Rounds (bugged)
[Ward] Helpful Wards
If the Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Bartleby Quietus will do one of the following:
Removes all helpful Wards from himself and applies a Heal over Time effect on himself that restores 432 Health over 3 Rounds (bugged)
Removes all helpful Wards from the Wizard and applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 436 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds
Removes all helpful Wards from the Wizard and applies two +35% Myth Damage Wards on the Wizard
[Damage over Time] Damage Over Time
If the Wizard casts a Damage Over Time Spell, Bartleby Quietus will remove all Damage Over Time effects from the himself and do one of the following:
Applies a -25% Damage Charm on the Wizard.
Applies a +30% Storm Damage Charms on the Wizard (bugged)
Applies a Heal over Time effect on the Wizard that restores 432 Health over 3 Rounds (bugged)
Applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 374 Life Damage after 3 Rounds
[Aura] Auras
"Your future is my memory." - If the Wizard casts a helpful Aura on themself, it will immediately be removed.
Bartleby Quietus will also say this at the beginning of the first Round with no effect.
[Global] Global
"Your future is my memory." - If the Wizard casts a Global spell, it will immediately be removed.
Helpful Cheats
[Round] "He's all-seeing, Wiz-Kid. He knows what you're going to do." - At the end of every third Round, Miranda Briar warns the Wizard.
[Spell] "Here, take this, just something I dreamed up." - At the end of the sixth Round, Miranda's Dream is added to the Wizard's Hand.
[Miranda's Dream]
This Spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat Bartleby Quietus.
spellNotes: Any Spells that Bartleby Quietus casts do not consume any Pips.
Casts a Fire Spell that applies two Damage over Time effects that each deal 275 Fire Damage over 10 Rounds.

locations: Housing Instance: Night Mire Gauntlet - Night Mire Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: [Late] "Just what we needed- time to bask under the sun!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.
[Round] "Moon and Stars keep Sun eternal!" - At the start of the battle, Bearded Dragon (Sun) says this.

[Afterlife] "You cannot defeat us at full strength!" - If Bearded Dragon (Sun) is defeated before or at the same time as Moon and Star Bearded Dragons, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Ra, dealing 75% of Wizard's max Health, and use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.

[Afterlife] "As long as two remain we shall survive!" - If Bearded Dragon (Sun) is defeated before or at the same time as one other Boss, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Power Nova, dealing 50% of Wizard's max Health, and use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.
Cheat Cycle:
Beginning of Round 2:
[Trap] "The Sun Rises" - Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast Sun Trap (+25%).
[Blade] "The Stars Twinkle" - Either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast Star Blade on a random ally.
[Shield] "The Moon Glows" - Either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast Moon Shield on a random ally.
[Spell] End of Round 2:
"The Radiant Sun Glows" - Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast one of the following Spells:
Blinding Light, Guardian Spirit, Smoke Screen
(Note: Guardian Spirit will only target either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon)
"Star Light Shine Bright" - Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast one of the following Spells:
Unicorn, Restoring Rain 2, Virulent Plague
"We Dance in the Moonlight" - Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast one of the following Spells:
Legion Shield, Grendel's Amends (Tier 3b), Mass Infection

name: Beast With A Million Eyes
locations: Darkmoor: Mortal Plain - House of a Million Eyes
cheats: Note: These Cheats do not start until the Second Round.
[Polymorph] Immediately after Beast with a Million Eyes casts a Polymorph Spell, one of the following Cheats will trigger, depending on the Spell that was cast:
[Ice] Shift Dread Paladin: "I see with my million eyes... Ice!" - Casts Sleet Storm.
[Life] Shift GreenOak: "I see with my million eyes... Life!" - Casts Devotion.
[Fire] Shift Grendel: "I see with my million eyes... Fire!" - Casts Furnace.
[Myth] Shift Ogre: "I see with my million eyes... Myth!" - Casts Reliquary.
[Storm] Shift Piscean: "I see with my million eyes... Storm!" - Casts Galvanic Field.
[Death] Shift Sugar Glider "I see with my million eyes... Death!" - Casts Virulence.

[Afterlife] "Wrong!" - If Beast With A Million Eyes is killed while the cheat is active, he will revive himself to 100% HP. Depending on the Aura that Beast with a Million Eyes has, and what School's damage is dealt to it, Beast with a Million Eyes will retaliate with various Spells:
While Sleet Storm is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Fire and Myth Spells, or else it will cast Lord of Winter.
While Devotion is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Death and Storm Spells, or else it will cast Spinysaur.
While Furnace is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Life and Storm Spells, or else it will cast Sun Serpent.
While Reliquary is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Fire and Life Spells, or else it will cast Celestial Calendar.
While Galvanic Field is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Death and Ice Spells, or else it will cast Storm Owl.
While Virulence is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Ice and Myth Spells, or else it will cast Avenging Fossil.
For Spells that do multiple Schools of damage, such as Hydra, each school will be counted separately, and if any of them would cause this Cheat to trigger, it will trigger once.
[Round] "Hahahahahahahaha!" - At the end of any Round that Beast with a Million Eyes did not take damage during, it will cast one of the following at random:
Blizzard
Ratatoskr's Spin
Meteor Strike
Humongofrog
Tempest
Sandstorm
Ship of Fools

name: Billy the Cutter
locations: Marleybone: Barkingham Palace - State Wing Wooden Key Room
cheats: "I can use that!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Shield or single-target Absorb Spell, then Billy will interrupt-cast Steal Ward on the receiver.
If a multi-target universal Shield Spell is cast, such as Legion Shield, then Billy will only steal the Ward from the caster.
"That'll help!" - If a Wizard casts an Ice, Fire, or universal Blade, then Billy will interrupt-cast Steal Charm on the receiver.
If a multi-target universal Blade is cast, such as Bladestorm, then Billy will only steal the Blade from the caster.
Using Aegis will prevent this cheat.
Pet Spells and Maycasts will trigger this cheat.
"This will cool you off!" - At the end of the fourth Round, and at the end of every eighth Round after, Billy will interrupt-cast Frost Giant.

"This will warm you up!" - At the end of the eighth Round, and at the end of every eighth Round after, Billy will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Fire Dragon that deals 495 Fire Damage plus 395 Fire Damage over three Rounds.

"You'll pay for that!" - After casting Fire Serpent, Fire Dragon, Heck Hound, using his cool off or warm up cheat, sometimes after using his Natural Attack and Scald, and rarely after Meteor Strike, Billy will interrupt-cast a Fire Prism on a random Wizard, and then use his Natural Attack on that Wizard.
If this cheat triggers while Billy has no Pips, then his Natural Attack will act as though he has one Pip.
Billy will only use this cheat after his Natural Attack if the sound that accompanies his Natural Attack is more of a growl than a grunt.
Although his Natural Attack is not cast when used through this cheat, it can still be Dispelled.

name: Billy the Cutter (Challenge)
cheats: [Fire][Jinx] "Some salt for your wounds" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, Billy will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (125 Fire Damage when including Billy's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.

[Fire][Ward] "Let me cut right past that" - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Billy will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (125 Fire Damage when including Billy's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.
[Shield] "Smocks on, we don't want to get dirty" - At the beginning of the first Round, Legion Shield will be cast three times. And every 6rds after that this cheat will activate again.
[Trap] "Who wants traps then?" - At the beginning of the third Round, a +25% version of Mass Fire Trap will be cast.

cheats: Broken Branch Queen:

[Aura] "The Queen protects her King!" - At the beginning of the first Round, and every sixth Round threafter, Broken Branch Queen casts a -100% incoming Damage Aura for 6 Rounds on Broken Branch King.

"Not while I still stand!" - If a Spell is cast to remove the Aura, such as Supernova, Broken Branch Queen will restore Broken Branch King to 100% Health, and recast the Aura. If the Aura is replaced with a harmful Aura, Broken Branch Queen will recast it without healing Broken Branch King.
This does not remove the harmful Aura.
The Broken Branch King can be defeated on the Round that the Aura expires, if the Bosses have the first turn in the battle.
Broken Branch King:
[Ward] "Are you the king?" - At the beginning of the first Round, Broken Branch King marks a random Wizard with a Stun Shield labeled "King."
[Trap] "Check!" - At the end of every third Round, Broken Branch King places a +100% Damage Ward on the marked Wizard.
[Trap] "Checkmate!" - After the marked Wizard reaches below 10% Health, Broken Branch King casts a +200% Damage Ward instead.
[Afterlife] "As goes the King, so the kingdom..." - When the marked Wizard is defeated, Broken Branch King will cast a Death version of Meteor Strike that deals 100% max Health Damage to all Wizards.
[Afterlife] "My strongest piece!" - When the Broken Branch Queen is defeated, Broken Branch King will remove his Aura.
"The King has fallen, so fall we all!" - When the Broken Branch King is defeated, the Broken Branch Queen will cast a Spell that defeats herself.

cheats: Cerberus (Balance) and Cerberus (Myth)
Every 5 rounds, the Balance and Myth heads will cast three -40% Tower Shields each around themselves.
Attacking any of the heads while they have Shields will trigger both of the other heads to retaliate, dealing ~750 Damage each.
Dispels can be used to prevent this cheat from activating.
Shadow Creature spells will trigger this cheat.
Multi-target spells and Beguile will activate this cheat twice.
Call of Khrulhu doesn't trigger this cheat.
The Balance and Myth heads will remove any Traps cast upon them.
This cheat can be prevented by using Vaporize.
Multi-Trap spells will result in only the final Trap (e.g. Storm from Elemental, Life from Spirit) being removed.
Pet spells will not activate this cheat.
Spells such as Windstorm will not activate this cheat.
Indemnity enchantment on any single or multi trap spells will prevent any traps from being removed.
Cerberus (Fire)
The Fire head does not cast any Tower Shields.
Sometimes a Cerberus with shields trades to one without tower shields.
Traps can be placed on this head without triggering any cheats.
Defeating the Fire head will prevent the Balance and Myth head from cheat-casting their Tower Shields.

[Trap] "*HAPPY BARK!*" - If a harmful Ward is cast on the Fire head, it will cast a Spell to remove all Traps from itself.

cheats: "Chip up!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage Spell, there is a chance that the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Spirit Armor on the target of the attack.
If a multi-target Damage Spell triggers this cheat, then the Spirit Armor will be cast on whoever is in the first spot.

[Minion][Afterlife] "Hold the line!" - The first time that the Bulldog Sergeant Major is defeated before the Chief Whip, the Chief Whip will cast a Spell that restores 15% max Health to all allies, reviving the Bulldog Sergeant Major.

name: Chilly
cheats: [Late] "Steal Pips!" - If a Wizard joins the duel late (third Round or later), all of the Germ Ghosts will remove one Pip from the Wizard, and gain one Pip.
[Polymorph] "Medicine mode!" - At the end of every five Rounds, the Germ Ghosts will cheat-cast a Polymorph for two Rounds, at which time they are vulnerable to Damage.
All Ghosts will pass while polymorphed.
This Polymorph changes all of the Germ Ghosts' Schools to Shadow for that Round.
A crafted Animus Summon Card of these Creatures will have their original School instead of Shadow.
At the end of the next turn, they will cheat-cast a reverse Polymorph into their original forms.
The Polymorph cheats will not fizzle, even with multiple negative Accuracy Charms (such as Smoke Screen).
[Round] "Next level!" - At the end of every six Rounds, they will remove all Traps from themselves and all Blades from each Wizard.
[Afterlife] If Chilly is defeated on the fifth Round, then the Blades/Traps will not be removed on the sixth Round, and the Germ Ghosts will stay Polymorphed for the full two turns.

The Germ Ghost in the next position will take over the Blade/Trap removal and Polymorph cheats when the previous one is defeated, the order being: Chilly > Achey > Sneezy > Cough. "May need further confirmation for later Rounds."
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Ice Damage for 3 Pips maximum.

name: Chronius
locations: Dragonspyre: The Crystal Grove - Chronius' Tower

name: Chronomaster Devlin
cheats: "Bad time to be late!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the first Round, Chronomaster Devlin will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
Time is on my side!" - At the beginning of Round 1 (and every 10th Round thereafter), he will cast a +45% version of Power Play.
If a Wizard casts any type of global spell while this is active, he will recast Power Play.

"Time is not on your side!" - At the end of Round 5 (and every 10th Round thereafter), he will replace the Power Play with a Balance global with -45% Power Pip gain that limits the maximum pip limit to 2 if all pips gained before are spent, similar to Dampen Magic.
Shadow pips can still be earned while the global is active.
Wizards who have more than 3 pips total are unaffected until they spend their pips.
If a Wizard casts any type of global spell while this is active, he will recast this spell.
"You can't trap the Cabal!" - If a trap is placed on Chronomaster Devlin or his Minion, he will place a Weakness (-30%) and a Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip) on the Wizard who cast the spell.

name: Cipactli
locations: Azteca: Tierra de Brea - Cipactli's Cave
cheats: Interrupt Cycles

"Can you follow the steps of my war dance?" - At the beginning of the first Round in each Dance cycle, Cipactli will place a Stun Shield "mark" on each Wizard. If the mark is not removed in the first or second Round of each Dance cycle, then he will cheat-cast a Dr. Von's Monster dealing 820 Steal Damage, and places a -25% Healing Charm at the Wizard in the beginning of the third Round.

"Those who cannot keep up must perish!" - At the beginning of the third Round in each Dance cycle, he will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster at any Wizard who did not get rid of the Stun Shield mark in the first or second Round.
Melt can be used to Dispel him from placing his mark on Wizards.
Ward Shields may only be cast on the Wizard in the first position (Sun spot). Shields cast on other Wizards are removed.
If a Wizard casts Legion Shield, the Shield will only be removed from the caster.
The self-trap portion of Feint will be immediately removed after casting.
Global Spells, such as Sanctuary and Wyldfire, will trigger him to remove the mark.
The Earthquake cast by his Minion(s) will remove the mark.
Aura Spells, such as Galvanic Field and Sleet Storm, will not trigger him to remove the mark.
Pet Spells will not trigger him to remove the mark.
Gear/Equipment Item Cards will not trigger him to remove the mark.

name: Clemente Moraga

name: Cleric Elezar
cheats: [Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Cleric Elezar or her minions are hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on themself:
If they are not defeated by the attack, they will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If they are defeated by the attack, they will cast a version of Minor Blessing on themself that heals for 100% of their max health.

[Spell] "Is that the sun?!" - If either Cleric Elezar or her minions are hit by a spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal dispel on themself.

name: Colonel Ivanenko
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Colonel Ivanenko will cheat-cast Wild Bolt on the Wizard.
"You can't fool me!" - If a Feint is placed on Ivanenko, he will cheat-cast Tower Shield on himself.
Melt can be used to Dispel this cheat.
"In the Gulag, we share such things!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cheat-cast Healing Current on himself.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
"Prepare for a storm of pain!" - At the beginning of Round 4, he will cheat-cast Darkwind.
"It's cold in the Gulag!" - At the beginning of Round 8, he will cheat-cast Balefrost.
"You will not weather this battle!" - At the beginning of Round 12, he will cheat-cast Darkwind.
The Pet, Ivanenko's Tiger. may randomly cast Storm Cat, Ice Cat (75%) (removes one Pip/Power Pip), or Snow Serpent.

name: Commander Ahk'To (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade][Trap][Aura][Global][Polymorph] If a wizard casts a damage-increasing blade, trap, aura, global, or shadow polymorph, Commander Ahk'to will cheat-cast one of the following spells in order. After 5 buffs, the cycle repeats itself:
Shadow Shepherd will not trigger this cheat.
"I can match any offense!" - Casts an all-allies version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 650 damage
"You do not learn! Shields!" - Casts Mass Tower Shield
"Learn this: No blade, no trap, nothing can harm me." - Casts an all-allies version of Brace that gives -40% incoming damage for 4 rounds
"Still, you fail to comprehend." Casts a global spell that decreases power pip chance by 25%. Needs confirmation on global effect
"So be it, let darkness consume!" Casts Shadow Sentinel
Interrupt Cycle (Resets every 2 [Round])
[Absorb] If the enemy creature is at full health, she will cast a version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 735 damage.
[Heal] If the enemy is below full health, she will cast Minor Blessing. This can revive enemies defeated on the same turn.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Coolest Centaur that only deals Damage.

name: Commander Ahk'To (Standard)
cheats: [Blade][Trap][Aura][Global][Polymorph] Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage-increasing Blade, Trap, Aura, Global, or Shadow Polymorph, Commander Ahk'to will cast one of the following Spells in order. After 5 buffs, the cycle repeats itself.
Shadow Shepherd will not trigger this cheat.
Effects that increase Armor Piercing will not trigger this cheat.
"I can match any offense!" - Casts an all-allies version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 650 Damage.
"You do not learn! Shields!" - Casts Mass Tower Shield
"Learn this: No blade, no trap, nothing can harm me." - Casts an Aura on all allies that reduces incoming Damage by 40% for 4 Rounds.
"Still, you fail to comprehend." Casts a Global that decreases all incoming Damage by 25%.
"So be it, let darkness consume!" Casts Shadow Sentinel
Interrupt Cycle (Resets every 2 [Round])
[Absorb] If the enemy creature is at full health, she will cast a version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 735 Damage.
[Heal] If the enemy is below full health, she will cast a version of Minor Blessing that restores 500 Health (735 Health when including Ahk'To's outgoing Healing boost).
spellNotes: Casts a version of Coolest Centaur that only deals Damage.

cheats: Copy Qhat [Star]
"More of you? That's my trick!" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Copy Qhat (Star) will interrupt-cast Mana Burn (Star) on them.
Responses towards Copy Qhat (Moon).
"No, Don't..." - Ally Copy Qhat (Moon), on the enemy side, will cast Bladestorm (+25%) on the Wizard's team.
"You fool!" - ??? (needs clarification)
"Stop that!" - Copy Qhat (Star) will say this after Copy Qhat (Moon) casts Major Snowburst.
Copy Qhat [Moon]
"I am ENDLESS!" - Copy Qhat (Moon) will summon a Copy Qhat (Moon) to any empty space on the enemy side.
"We gonna get you! Pow!" - Summons a Copy Qhat (Moon) on the Wizard's team in the first opening slot.
"There's more where she came from!" - If there isn't currently a Copy Qhat minion on the Wizard's team, a new one will be summoned if a slot is available.
Copy Qhat (Moon) will replace a Minion that was summoned by the Wizard, but won't if all duel slots are occupied.
Defeating the enemy Copy Qhat (Moon) will stop all these cheats.
spellNotes: These Spell notes apply to Copy Qhat (Moon) and not the Boss, Copy Qhat (Star).
Casts a Tower Shield that applies itself to all enemies.
Casts a Hex that applies itself to all allies.
Casts a Black Mantle that applies itself to all allies.
Casts a Balance Blade that applies itself to all enemies.
Casts a Weakness that applies itself to all allies.

name: Corporal Tenni'syn
locations: Empyrea: Zanadu - Corporal Tenni'syn Stone Key Room
cheats: "Reinforcements? Ha! You're late!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Tenni'syn will interrupt-cast his natural attack on the Wizard.
At the end of every Round, Tenni'syn will gain a number of additional power pips that is equal to the number of Wizards that is present in the duel minus one.
"Into the jaws of Death with you!" - Tenni'syn will say this at the beginning of the Round that he gains his seventh Shadow pip to cast Lord of the Squall (Shadow).
Dispel Cycle:
"Ah, clever! Don't do that again" - When the first Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow).
"Oh, no! - I'm ready for you this time!" - When the second Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow).
"I'm warning you! No more!" - When the third Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow).
"You can't hurt me! - You've made me stronger!" - When the fourth Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow) and Healing Current on himself.
"Volley and thunder! Shatter and Sunder!"' - After the cheat above triggers, he'll cast Cleanse Charm (Shadow), cast his Natural Attack then proceed with the cheat below.
"Lightning from the left of you! Lightning from the right!" - After casting his Natural Attack, Tenni'syn will repeatedly cast Wild Bolt on random Wizards up to seven times.
Feint Cycle:
"Ah, clever! Don't do that again" - Tenni'syn will say this when the first Feint per Round is cast on him.
"Oh, no! - I'm ready for you this time!" - For every second Feint per Round that is cast on Tenni'syn, he will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2) on himself removing 2 Traps.

"You can't hurt me! - You've made me stronger!" - When a Damage over Time is used after a Feint is cast on him on the same turn, then Tenni'syn will interrupt-cast a Windstorm, a Supercharge (+12%), a +12% version of Stormspear, and a version of Storm Hound (150) that attacks all Wizards, followed up by Shift on a random Wizard and Incindiate on all Wizards then use Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) on himself to remove all Traps.
"Time to finish this!" - Every four Rounds, Tenni'syn will use his Natural Attack dealing Storm Damage.
If Tenni'syn is damaged on the Round before this cheat occurs, he will use Wild Bolt for every trap that was consumed when he was damaged.
spellNotes: Casts both a protected and unprotected Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).
Casts both a protected and unprotected Windstorm (+25%).
Casts Lord of the Squall (Shadow) that removes all Charms, Wards, Pips, and Shadow Pips from all Wizards.
Casts a Shadow Cleanse Ward that removes all negative Wards.
Deals an unknown quantity of Storm Damage.

name: Count Strigoi (Challenge)
cheats: [Minion] "They... ascended" - At the beginning of Round 1, Strigoi will summon 3 Ascended Guards.
[Manipulation] "I told you child... I protect you from the dangers beyond" - At the beginning of the round, Count Strigoi will cast Confusion on on a random wizard and heal himself for 6600 HP.

[Afterlife] "And now my hunger returns!" - When Ascended Guard dies, Strigoi will cast Sacrifice to revive them with 20% of their max HP at the cost of 2000 of his HP. If multiple Ascended Guards are defeated in 1 spell, he will cast Sacrifice multiple times.
[Damage] "We turn our pain into the master's boon!" - When Ascended Guard is hit with an attack, if they are not defeated, they will heal Count Strigoi for 5000 HP.
[Health] "Eh, I'm feeling a little overstuffed" - Count Strigoi will say this when he is at full HP at the start of a turn, allowing the Wizards to defeat him.

[Health] "The only thing I feel is hunger pangs!" - Count Strigoi will say this when he is not at full HP at the start of a turn. Defeating him in this state will result in him casting Scarecrow and reviving.
[Steal] "Are you alone? Let me fix that!" - If Count Strigoi is not defeated from full HP in a single attack, he will cast Scarecrow and revive himself.
[Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Count Strigoi or an Ascended Guard is hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on himself:
If he is not defeated by the attack, he will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If he is defeated by the attack, he will cast a version of Minor Blessing on himself that heals for 100% of his max health.

[Spell] "Is that the sun?!" - If either Count Strigoi or an Ascended Guard is hit by a spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal dispel on themself.

name: Crash the Bandit Coot (Challenge)
cheats: "Crickey, too many power-ups!" - At the end of every Round, Crash the Bandit Coot casts a 0 Damage Shadow version of Empower that gives him a random School Pip.
"Crash Attack!" - At the beginning of every Round, if Crash the Bandit Coot has 2 or more of the same School Pip, he will cast the following Spells:
[Fire School Pip] 2 Fire Pips: Sun Serpent and a version of Backfire that clears all negative Wards from all allies.
[Ice School Pip] 2 Ice Pips: Lord of Winter and a version of Glacial Fortress that clears all negative Charms from all allies.
[Storm School Pip] 2 Storm Pips: Storm Owl and Disarm (Shadow, All Enemies), removing one Charm from all enemies.
[Myth School Pip] 2 Myth Pips: Celestial Calendar and Cleanse Ward (Shadow, All Allies), removing one Ward from all allies.
[Life School Pip] 2 Life Pips: Spinysaur and a version of Oni's Naturalism that clears all protected Wards from all allies.
[Death School Pip] 2 Death Pips: Avenging Fossil and a version of Putrefaction that clears all protected Charms from all enemies.
[Balance School Pip] 2 Balance Pips: Sabertooth and casts a 0 Damage Shadow version of Empower that gives him a random School Pip.
This cheat can repeat multiple times during the same Round.

"Crash Lives Again!" - After Crash the Bandit Coot is defeated once, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring himself to 100% max Health, then cast an Equalize Global, setting the Damage cap to 4,000
This Global will be recast if replaced.

name: Cronus (Tartarus)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus - Cave of Nyx
cheats: Every Round, Cronus will cast a Sun Spell that places a +40% Trap of the following Schools.
Balance, Death, Fire, Ice, Myth, Life, Storm.
Sometimes Cronus casts an invisible trap, probably due to being a Balance trap, that seems to act like a universal trap. This trap is not listed in effects.
Every four Rounds, he will interrupt cast Lord of Night on a random Wizard.
When a Wizard places a trap on him, he will place a Marked Stun Block on himself.
Cronus will only place up to one marked stun block on himself, regardless of how many traps are placed on him, until he is attacked with a non-DoT spell.
When attacked by a single-targeting spell, a Cleanse Ward will be cast on him removing all Traps, and the Marked Stun Block.
Vaporize can dispel this cheat.
Spells that only place a DoT on Cronus will not remove the mark.

"Attacks alone will never hurt me!" - If any Wizards attack without placing a trap on him, Cronus will interrupt cast his Natural Attack of the opposite School that a Wizard cast (Example: Storm damage spell = Myth Myth Natural Attack +40% to the next Myth damage ward spell).
Casting a Balance damage spell will result in Cronus using Natural Attack (Cronus Storm).
Multiple damage spells such as Minotaur will trigger him casting his natural twice.
All enemies damage spells will trigger this cheat.
DoT spells will trigger this cheat only on initial hit.
Any Astral spells trigger an unknown damage school.
Shadow spells trigger an unknown damage school.

name: Cronus (Tartarus, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Cave of Nyx (Challenge)
cheats: [Trap] At the beginning of every Round, Cronus will cast a Sun Spell that applies a +40% Trap of a random School on all Wizards.
[Shield] "For every shield, I have a trap!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Shield, Cronus will cast a Sun Spell on the Wizard that applies a +40% Trap of a random School.
[Jinx] If a harmful Ward is cast on Cronus, he will remove it.

If a Multi-Trap Spell such as Elemental Trap is cast on Cronus, he will only remove one Ward, and Cronus will place a Marked Stun Block on himself. After Cronus is attacked while he has a Marked effect, he will remove the Mark and all harmful Wards from himself.
All Enemies harmful Ward Spells such as Windstorm will cause the Ward to be removed from Cronus only.

Casting an attack on Cronus that places multiple Wards will trigger his below cheat (if not Marked), then cause him to remove one Ward and place a Mark. If he is Marked when hit with a spell that places multiple Wards, he will remove the Mark but not place another.

[Damage] "Attacks alone will never hurt me!" - If Cronus is attacked when he doesn't have a Marked effect, Cronus will cast a version of his Natural Attack based on the School of the Spell that hit him.
Fire Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Storm)
Ice Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Fire)
Storm Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Ice)
Life Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Death)
Myth Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Life)
Death Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Myth)
Balance or Shadow Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Balance)
Damage Spells that hit multiple times such as Minotaur will trigger this cheat multiple times.
Damage over Time Spells will only trigger this cheat on their initial hit.

Indemnity will not protect Traps from this cheat.

[Healing Current (The Death Card)]
This spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat The Death Card.

name: Death Tendril
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Graveyard Stone Key Room
cheats: Aphrodite II:
The following duel circle spots are considered to be across from each other, in regards to the Boss cheats:
If a Blade Damage Charm or Damage-Increasing Global Spell is cast (e.g. Darkwind), the Tendril across from the Wizard will cheat-cast the following Spells:
"Terrify the vermin!" and "Find their weak points!" - The Death Tendril will cheat-cast Scarecrow and Feint (+75%) on a random Wizard.
"Tear down the Blades!" and "Seek out the cracks in their foundation!" - The Life Tendril will cheat-cast Forest Lord and Guidance.
"Shake the earth!" and "Stun the strongest!" - The Myth Tendril will cheat-cast Earthquake, then Stun a random Wizard.
When the Tendril across from the Wizard has been defeated, that Wizard may safely cast Blades without triggering this cheat.
If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time Spell, the Tendril across from the Wizard will cheat-cast the following Spell:
"Summon the deadly hunt!" - The Death Tendril will cheat-cast Deer Knight.
"Shake it off!" - The Life Tendril will cheat-cast Mass Triage, which remains active for 3 Rounds.
"Steal the strength!" - The Myth Tendril will cheat-cast Shift at the Wizard.
When a Wizard is defeated, the Tendril across from the Wizard will cheat-cast the following Spell:
"Find their weak points!" - The Death Tendril will cheat-cast Feint (+75%).
"Seek out the cracks in their foundation!" - The Life Tendril will cheat-cast Guidance.
"Stun the strongest!" - The Myth Tendril will cheat-cast Stun on a random Wizard.
Interrupt Cycle:

"I am untouched! My spores will spread over everyone!" - Any Round that a Tendril is not attacked, Aphrodite II will cast a unique Spell on all Wizards, which deals 333 Shadow Damage and 666 Shadow Damage over 3 Rounds, and applies a -30% Damage Charm, a -40% Accuracy Charm, and a -50% Healing Charm.
A Wizard fleeing will trigger this cheat.
A Wizard joining late will not trigger this cheat.
"No! That stings!" - Attacking any of the Tendrils will interrupt this cheat.

name: Despair
cheats: *SIGH*:
At the beginning of the first Round, Despair will cast a -100% version of Doom and Gloom and will recast this Spell when replaced.
If Despair casts a Spell on a Wizard, it will follow it up with a Deathblade (+45%).
If Despair takes Damage, it will cast a Virulent Plague on itself.
Multi-Hit Spells will trigger this cheat multiple times.
Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
If a Wizard casts one of the following Spell Effects, Despair will cast the Spells:
[Caster][Damage] - Self-Damage Spells, like Empower, will trigger a protected Balanceblade (+100%) on Despair.
[Aura] - Auras will trigger Supernova (Shadow) on the caster.
If a negative Aura is cast on Despair, it will cast the Supernova on itself.
[Charm] - Positive Charms will trigger Enfeeble (Shadow) on the caster.
Aegis can protect against this cheat.
[Ward] - Positive Wards will trigger a Shadow All Enemies version of Shatter on the caster.
Feint or Mass Feint, including Pet Item Cards, will trigger an Ice version of Swap Ward on the caster.
Indemnity or Melt can protect against this cheat.

name: Desperation
cheats: "GAH!":
At the beginning of the first Round, Desperation will cast Katabatic Wind and will recast this spell when replaced.
If a Feint or Mass Feint is cast, Desperation will cast a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb version of Frozen Armor on itself.
Melt can be used to prevent this cheat.
Health Threshold Cheats: If Desperation's Health reaches the following percent health thresholds, the following cheats will occur.
Below 80% Health - If Desperation takes Damage, it casts an Ice version of Healing Current restoring 100, 400, or 1,000 Health to itself.
Below 50% Health - At the beginning of every Round, Desperation will cast a -80% Random School Shield or a Tower Shield on itself.
Below 25% Health:
Casts an Ice version of Life Feint that applies a +30% Damage Trap on caster and a +75% Ice Trap on target.
Casts Resuscitate.
spellNotes: Desperation's Brace has the appearance of Sleet Storm.
Casts an Ice version of Sabertooth.

name: Detolli the Destroyer
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Detolli will cheat-cast Fuel on the Wizard.
"Burn! Burn it all down!" - If a Wizard casts a blade, global, or an aura spell, then Detolli will cast one of the following on himself.
Firespear: +5%, +10% or +15%
Pet spells will trigger this cheat.
Steal Charm will not trigger this cheat.
"We'll reduce this tower to cinders if we must!"' - During Round 1 (and every 2 Rounds thereafter), Detolli will summon a Scorcher (Summon).
Scorcher (Summon) Cheat Cycle: - Detolli will order the Minion to cast Spells on each Round as follows.
Round 1: "How about a bit of fire and brimstone?" - Scorcher (Summon) will cast Brimstone Revenant.
(This cheat is bugged since Scorcher has no archmastery, so it will pass.)
Round 2: "You know nothing of fire!" - Scorcher (Summon) will cast Mass Fire Prism (Random), which converts Fire damage into a random School of damage, including Shadow damage.
Round 3: "Burn out, my friend, and give me a boost!" - Scorcher (Summon) will interrupt with a version of Take Power on Detolli, sacrificing itself to give Detolli the Destroyer two Power Pips.
spellNotes: Detolli's Dragon can cast Meteor Strike (355-385) or Fire Dragon (495+395).

name: Dewey (Portal of Peril, Tier 1)
locations: Portal of Peril: Fantastic Voyage Expedition (Portal of Peril) - Data Caverns (Portal of Peril)
cheats: [Late] "Tisk, tisk! You'll have to pay the late fees!" - If a Wizard joins the Duel after the first Round, then Dewey will interrupt-cast a Sun version of Mana Burn that deals no Damage and removes 2 Pips.
[Damage] "This racket will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage Spell on an enemy, then Dewey will interrupt-cast a Shield of the Damage's School on the recipient of the Damage.
Spells that deal Damage multiple times or to multiple targets will result in Dewey casting a Shield for each hit that occurred.
Balance Damage will result in Dewey interrupt-casting Tower Shield on the recipient.
[Damage over Time] "I have to do everything around here!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time Spell on an enemy, then Dewey will interrupt-cast Mass Triage.
[Blade] / [Global] "Defend the sanctuary!" - The first time that any Friend or Enemy casts a Blade or Damage Global, Dewey will interrupt-cast Tower Shield on himself.
"I'm sick of all this noise!" - The second time that any Friend or Enemy casts a Blade or Damage Global, Dewey will interrupt-cast Plague.
"Fight, or no dinner for you!" - The third time that any Friend or Enemy casts a Blade or Damage Global, Dewey will interrupt-cast Legion Shield.
"Maybe this will quiet you down..." - The fourth time that any Friend or Enemy casts a Blade or Damage Global, Dewey will interrupt-cast Virulent Plague.
If any Friend or Enemy casts a fifth Blade or Damage Global, then the cycle resets back to "Defend the sanctuary!"
categories: Fantastic Voyage Expedition (Portal of Peril) Creatures

name: Doctor Demented
locations: Empyrea: Southwest Aero Plains - Dark Cloud Base
cheats: "Time burns away all things, especially when you are late!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Doctor Demented will interrupt-cast Burning Rampage on the Wizard.

"Your fears protect me!" - At the beginning of Round 1, she will cheat-cast a permanent version of Fortify that resists -60%, and it will increase by 20% for each additional Wizard in the duel for up to a maximum of -120%.
The Aura cannot be removed.

"Fear still remains" - If a Wizard joins or flees the duel after it has already begun, she will update her aura accordingly. This also occurs if a Minion is Beguiled with 3 or less Wizards in the duel, treating the Minion as an additional Wizard.
The aura will reset back to its original percentage after the Beguile effect ends.
"You have overcome your fears!? No! Never!" - The turn after all Minions are defeated, she will cheat-cast Frenzy and immediately after cast Supernova on herself.
Universal Traps can amplify the damage of the Supernova.
"Aren't we all having a BLAST!?" - Boss will cheat-cast Incindiate.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Poison (45+450) that attacks all enemies.
Deals 420 Death Damage for 4 Pips maximum.

name: Doctor Jackall
cheats: "I invoke the power of Ice!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Doctor Jackall will interrupt-cast Balefrost.
"I must defend myself!" - If Doctor Jackall is damaged by a Spell, then he will interrupt-cast a -35% version of Legion Shield.
If Doctor Jackall is damaged more than once by a single Spell, then he will use this cheat for every time that he is damaged.
Ticks from Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
This cheat will not trigger if Doctor Jackall is damaged during the Round that his Polymorph ends.
"I'll take that!" - If Doctor Jackall uses his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then he will interrupt to use Steal Ward on the Wizard that he attacked.

"See my true face!" - At the end of the fourth Round, and at the end of every fourth Round after, Doctor Jackall will interrupt-cast a Spell that Polymorphs him into Mister Hound and applies a +35% Fire Damage Global.
"You dare strike me?" - If Mister Hound is damaged by a Spell, then he will interrupt-cast Fireblade on himself, then use his Natural Attack on the Wizard that attacked him.
If Mister Hound is damaged more than once by a single Spell, then he will use this cheat for every time that he is damaged.
If this cheat triggers while Mister Hound has no Pips, then his Natural Attack will act as though he has one Pip.
Although his Natural Attack is not cast when used through this cheat, it can still be Dispelled.
This cheat will trigger if Doctor Jackall is damaged during the Round that his Polymorph ends.
"I'll take that, thank you!" - If Mister Hound uses his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then he will interrupt to use Steal Charm on the Wizard that he attacked.
"The weakness returns!" - At the beginning of the eighth Round, and at the end of every fourth Round after, Doctor Jackall will interrupt-cast Balefrost.
"My rage boils over!" - If all of the Lanyon Scratchers are defeated, then Doctor Jackall or Mister Hound will interrupt-cast Lava Giant.
If there are no Lanyon Scratchers present at the beginning of the duel, then this cheat will trigger whenever the Wizard team casts their first Spell.
May-Cast Spells will trigger this cheat.
If Doctor Jackall or Mister Hound is Beguiled, then this cheat will trigger the first time that a Lanyon Scratcher casts a Spell.
This trigger can only occur once during the duel.

name: Dream Belloq
cheats: "One of me is not enough!" - Dream Belloq will cast a Shadow Spell to summon one of the following Minions per Wizard in the dueling circle, if there is an open slot.
Belloq Image (Fire), Belloq Image (Ice), Belloq Image (Myth), Belloq Image (Storm)
”A new arrival? The more the merrier!” - If a Wizard is late joining the duel, Dream Belloq will cast Ra on the Wizard, dealing 33% of the Wizard’s maximum Health, and summon a new Minion.
Dream Belloq will summon one Minion once every six to ten Rounds, if there is an open slot.

"Behold, the power of - Oh no!" If a Minion is defeated, Belloq will cast Ra on himself and take Damage for every Minion defeated. Damage varies depending on the number of Wizards in the dueling circle. Example: If there is one Wizard, Dream Belloq will take 300,000 Damage. If there are two Wizards, Dream Belloq will take 150,000 Damage per Minion. If there are more than two Wizards, Dream Belloq will take 99,000 Damage per Minion.
*POSSIBLE BUG* Dream Belloq will respawn and rejoin the duel if a Wizard joins the dueling circle after Dream Belloq has been defeated. "Needs verification."

name: Dream Freddie
cheats: Confusion
At the beginning of the first Round, Confusion will cast Saga of Heroes and will recast this spell when replaced.
Beguile Cheats - While Dream Freddie is alive, Confusion will cast Spells that benefit whichever side has Beguiled it, while it has an Aura up. This prevents attacks on allies and heals on enemies.
[Enemy] - If Dream Freddie casts Beguile (Sun) on Confusion, it will cast a "Freddie's Influence" Empowerment Aura on itself.
[Friend] - If a Wizard casts Beguile on Confusion, Confusion will cast a "Wizard's Influence" Infallible Aura on itself.
Spells with Beguile as a Secondary Effect, such as Crow, will trigger this cheat.
Confusion will target and heal either side when not Beguiled.
If Dream Freddie is defeated, Confusion will cast a Star version of Supernova, removing its Aura and dealing no Damage to itself, ending it's Beguile cheats.
Dream Freddie
"Well, when you say it like that..." - At the beginning of every fourth Round, Dream Freddie casts Beguile (Sun) on Confusion.
"I'm a BAD guy!" - If Dream Freddie is attacked, he will cast a Tower Shield (Sun -90%) on himself.
Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward can be used to remove this Shield.
Damage over Times will not trigger this cheat.

name: Drowned Dan (Tier 1)
locations: Celestia: Science Center - Drowned Dan's Chamber
cheats: [Aura] "Ye must sink the sams to learn me code" - Drowned Dan will cast an Invulnerability -300% Brace Aura making him Immune to Damage.

[Round] "The code is #-#-#-#" - At the start of the second Round, Drowned Dan will reveal the first number of the code. Each time a Minion is defeated, the next number of the code is revealed at the start of the following Round. The code represents four different Spell pip costs that Drowned Dan must be targeted with in order to deactivate the Invulnerability Aura.
List of Codes:
Drowned Dan will use one of the following nine codes:
Correct Responses:
"Blast! Ye be correct!" - is his response if answered correctly the first time.
"Yar! A lucky guess!" - is his response if answered correctly the second time.
"Blimey! They've figured it out!" - is his response if answered correctly the third time.
"Nooo! My protection!"' - is his response if answered correctly the fourth time, and casts a version of Supernova on himself that deals no Damage.
Incorrect Responses:
"Incorrect! Back to the start with ye!" - is his response if answered incorrectly, starting over the original sequence.

[Blade][Global] "A little curse, I think" - If a Wizard casts a Damage-type Blade Spell or Global Spell, she will place a protected Curse (Shadow) on the Wizard who cast the Spell, and a protected Weakness (Shadow) on the Wizard who received the Blade.

name: Elephant Spectral Guardian
cheats: At the beginning of the battle, the Elephant Spectral Guardian will cast a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself. He replaces this Tower Shield every time he is attacked, until all of the other Spectral Guardians are defeated.
Pierce, Steal Ward and Shatter can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield. (He will still replace it when attacked.)

"Only charms can save you now!" - The Elephant Spectral Guardian will mark a random Wizard for 2 Rounds. During that time, if a charm is not cast upon the marked Wizard, then the other Spectral Guardians will cheat-cast the following spells at the marked Wizard:
"Are you still marked?" - After these spells are cast or a charm is cast, the Elephant Spectral Guardian will remove the mark and all wards (including traps) from the marked Wizard.
If a charm is cast on the marked Wizard, this cheat will not activate.
Pet spells will not affect this cheat. Pet Charms will not remove the mark.
Stun shields can be removed by Shatter.
"We can't fail!" - If all the other Spectral Guardians have been defeated, the Elephant Spectral Guardian will remove all wards from himself (including his Tower Shield).
"Your pursuit ends here!" - After all the other Spectral Guardians have been defeated, he will cast Ominous Scarecrow (Death) every three Rounds.

cheats: [Round] At the beginning of the first Round, Entrance Exam will cast one of the following on itself:
[Shield] Seven -80% Damage Wards, one for each School including Balance.
[Heal over Time] Four Heal over Time effects that each restore 52 Health over 50 Rounds.

"You missed the lesson. We must drive home its effect!" - If Entrance Exam is defeated while any of these hanging effects still exist, it will revive itself with half Health, reapply the effects, and give each Wizard the corresponding Spell that can be used to remove the effects.
[Shatter (Battle Card)][Incapacitate (Death)]

[Charm][Trap] "A conventional technique. We are prepared." - Each time a Wizard casts a Damage Charm on themself or a Damage Ward on Entrance Exam, each Wizard will take Damage equal to 20% of their max Health.
"*Grim Laughter*" - This will become the new line of the previous cheat if triggered more than 2 times.

name: Ervin the Barbarian
locations: Wysteria: Pegasus Place - Tower of the Helephant
cheats: Ervin the Barbarian
At the start of the battle, Ervin cheat-casts a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself. If removed by Damage, he cheat-casts it again as long as at least one Minion is alive.
Spells such as Steal Ward or Pierce can be used to safely remove the shield, but he will still replace it when he takes Damage.
Ervin will still cast the shield on the first Round even if all of the Minions are missing due to being defeated in a prior attempt.
"Hah! Your miss leaves you open!" - Whenever a Wizard fizzles, Ervin cheat-casts a 0-Pip Skeletal Dragon on the Wizard who fizzled, dealing 800 + 1,998 Death Damage over 3 Rounds.

"I needed those thieves!" - After all of the Minions are defeated and repeating every second round, Ervin cheat-casts a 0-Pip Leviathan at the start of the round that deals 2,030 Storm Damage to the highest threat Wizard.
Although this cheat is not cast, it may still be dispelled with Dissipate.
If all of the Minions were defeated in a prior attempt, this cheat will first trigger on the third Round (although the line is still spoken without any associated spellcast on the first Round).
If a Trap is placed on Ervin while any of the Minions are alive, then he will cast Cleanse Ward on himself.
Vaporize can be used to dispel this cheat.
For Elemental Trap and Spirit Trap, only the last Trap of the three (Storm, Life) is removed.
Multi-Trap Spells (such as Windstorm or Mass Death Prism) do not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
Taurus the Clever
At the start of every third round, Taurus cheat-casts Smoke Screen (-50%), making it easier for Ervin to use Skeletal Dragon.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.

"This would look better on Ervin." - If a Wizard casts any Charm, Taurus will cheat-cast Disarm on the recipient and give a Deathblade to Ervin the Barbarian. If Ervin the Barbarian is defeated, Taurus will still cast Disarm without casting Deathblade.
Dissipate can be used to prevent this cheat. If Disarm is dispelled, Taurus will not give Ervin a Deathblade.
For Elemental Blade and Spirit Blade, only the first Blade of the three (Fire, Life) is removed.
For multi-Charms like Bladestorm, only the caster's Charm is removed.
Spells such as Plague and Smoke Screen activate this cheat and Taurus will cast Disarm on the caster.
Aegis can be used to avoid this cheat.
Charms cast by Shadow Creatures such as Shadowblade will trigger this cheat.
Pet spells do not trigger this cheat.

name: Estrakir Gloomthorn
cheats: If Estrakir is attacked (or defeated) before Knuckles McCloud, then Knuckles will give him a Helping Hands out of turn. Estrakir will then cast Fire Dragon out of turn.
Meteor Strike will trigger this cheat if Estrakir casts this Spell.
Estrakir will sometimes cast Fire Dragon even when he is not hit.
Morganthe's Requiem does not trigger either cheat.
Estrakir will cast Cleanse Ward on himself or any minion of his that receives a Shield.
Only the first Trap of a Tri-Trap Spell will be removed.
All-Enemy Trap Spells, such as Windstorm or Mass Fire Prism, will not trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat, though he may randomly remove the Traps later on.
Secondary Spell effects will also be removed.(e.g. Brimstone Revenant, Keeper of the Flame).

"Power Spell!" - If a Global Spell is not active around the battlefield (even if the Global Spell is active(?)), Estrakir will cast a Fire version of Power Nova dealing 770 Fire Damage every three turns.
If a Wizard casts a Global Spell, Estrakir or a Wizard will shout out, "Interrupt Charge!".
"Power Spell!" - Sometime after his Minions have been defeated, Estrakir will start using a version of Scarecrow (600) every other turn.
On their first turn, the Rat Illusionists will shield Knuckles McCloud and Estrakir with -90% Tower Shields, and will replace these out of turn whenever they are broken.

Each of the Rat Illusionists will choose either Knuckles or Estrakir, and cast the Tower Shield on that Boss. If only one of the Rats is defeated, then the remaining Rat will cast a Shield on Knuckles before Estrakir.
Defeating Estrakir or Knuckles will end this cheat.
"Yeah, we can Heal Too!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Knuckles will cast a 750 Healing Rebirth (without Absorb Shields).
Pet heals will not trigger this cheat.

[Minion] "Right Hook!" - At the end of Round 4, he shouts "Right Hook!" and summons Rocky RightHook (which isn't active until the next Round ).
[Minion] "Upper Cut!" - At the end of Round 6, he will summon Tyson UpperCut (which isn't active until the next Round).
