name: Krokodile Hunter
cheats: "Let the show begin!" - At the end of Round 1, Krokodile Hunter will cast a protected Balanceblade (+100%) on himself, a Feint (Shadow), a +200% outgoing Damage Berserk and a 0 Damage Tempest to all enemies.
"That's right- showed you!" - After the Tempest, Krokodile Hunter will cast a Star version of Supernova, removing his Aura and dealing no Damage to himself.

"You're caught in the jaws of the Krok mate!" - At the beginning of every Round, Krokodile Hunter will cast a version of Spinysaur that deals 100 Life Damage to a random enemy. (214 with Outgoing Damage)

name: Krokopatra (Challenge)
cheats: [Late] "Your Queen has no patience for tardiness!"' - If a Wizard is late to combat, Krokopatra casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.

[Damage] "You dare strike your Queen?!" - If Krokopatra is attacked, she will cast a version of Healing Current that heals for 110, 440, or 1,100 Health, before casting a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
[Damage over Time] "I shrug off such paltry magic"' - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect on Krokopatra, she will cast Stormzilla (Tier 5b).
[Blade] "You can't charm me!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global, Krokopatra will either cast an AoE Moon version of Enfeeble, or Healing Current on herself.

name: Krokopatra (Rank 14)
cheats: Krokopatra
"More fodder! Protect your Queen!" If a Minion is defeated, Krokopatra will summon another random School Minion which will become active after two Rounds.

"Time to ride the Lightning!" - Various triggers can occur: If no Minions are present, if she has been defeated and healed, if some Minions are defeated and healed, Krokopatra will shout this phrase and use her turn to cast Insane Bolt. Needs confirmation on triggers.
"Why don't we direct all that potential?" Krokopatra will randomly cast Beguile on the Wizard with the most Blades at the beginning of the Round.
"Loose the winds of destruction!" - Krokopatra will interupt-cast Windstorm (+25%).
"I'm crackling with rage!" - Krokopatra will interupt-cast a zero-Pip Supercharge for the amount of Pips she has. Needs confirmation on trigger.
"Beware! There's a storm brewing!" Krokopatra will cheat-cast Astraphobia.
Queen's Thrall (Death) (Rank 14)
"Anything for my Queen" If Krokopatra receives a Charm, this Minion will cheat-cast Dark Pact on her.
"No one threatens my queen!" - The Wizard with the most Blades may randomly get a Bad Juju by this Minion at the beginning of the Round.
Queen's Thrall (Life) (Rank 14)
'We must protect ourselves to protect the Queen!" If a DoT Spell is cast, this Minion will cheat-cast Mass Triage.
"Our Queen needs her army!" - If Krokopatra or a Minion is attacked, this Minion will cheat-cast Unicorn.
Queen's Thrall (Storm) (Rank 14)
"Protect the Queen!" - If Krokopatra is attacked, this Minion will cheat-cast Healing Current.
"Stand Strong for the Queen!" - If a Dispel is placed on Krokopatra or a Minion, this Minion will cheat-cast Cleanse Charm.

name: Krokopatra (Rank 8)
cheats: Krokopatra will cheat-cast Beguile before a Wizard attacks with an Attack-All-Enemies Spell, causing the Wizard to attack allies that Round.
This cheat does not activate if soloing.
"More fodder! Protect your queen!" If one of Krokopatra's Minions is defeated before her, she will randomly summon another Minion at the end of the next Round.
"Loose the winds of destruction!" She will randomly cast Windstorm.

"Why don't we redirect all that potential?" She will cheat-cast Beguile at the beginning of the Round if there is any Blade on a Wizard. This will repeat every other Round until there are no more Blades on the Wizard.
"I'm crackling with rage!" Krokopatra will randomly cheat-cast Supercharge (+12%). Its percentage is still based on her number of Pips, however.
"Stand strong for the Queen!"If a Wizard casts a negative Charm (e.g. Plague), this Queen's Thrall will cast Cleanse Charm on the receiver of the Charm.
"Our Queen needs her army!" Interrupt casts Unicorn when any other Thralls are defeated in the same Round. Stuns do not prevent this cheat.
"We must protect ourselves to protect the Queen!" If a Damage over Time Spell is cast, this Minion will cheat-cast Mass Triage.
"Anything for my queen!" Every other Round, this Queen's Thrall will cast Dark Pact (+30%) on Krokopatra.
This Thrall can defeat itself using this cheat.

[Damage] "You dare strike your Queen?!"' - If a Wizard Damages Krokopatra, she will cheat-cast a version of Healing Current, restoring 100, 400, or 1,000 Health to herself. Then, Krokopatra casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt, dealing 250, 500 or 1,000 Moon Damage on all enemies.

Indemnity will not bypass this cheat.

name: Krokosphinx (Balance)
cheats: 1st Riddle

[Round] "I am caustic and invisible; I can be measured, but never touched; and I am infinite, but always in short supply. What am I?" - At the end of the first Round, Krokosphinx will change each Wizards' Spell Decks, giving all Wizards the following Spells and 20,000 Health:
[Candy][Crowns][Space][Summer][Time]
Correct Answer: Time
"Correct. Time rots the world, renders empire into ruins." - If answered correctly, Krokosphinx will cast a +100% Damage Trap on itself.
"WRONG!" - Krokosphinx will say this if answered incorrectly.
2nd Riddle

[Round] "What is a bringer of death and life, light and shadow, and energy and exhaustion?" - At the end of the second Round, Krokosphinx will change each Wizards' Spell Decks, giving all Wizards the following Spells and 20,000 Health:
[Conversation][Exercise][Sugar][The Moon][The Sun]
Correct Answer: The Sun
"Yes, the sun enables, but also burns. Its light casts shadow. And its energy brings wearying heat." - If answered correctly, Krokosphinx will cast a +100% Damage Trap on itself.
3rd Riddle

[Round] "I haunt like a ghost, ensnare like a trap and lie to my creator. I bring gentle comfort or bitter sickness. I am faded, but forever. What am I?" - At the end of the third Round, Krokosphinx will change each Wizards' Spell Decks, giving all Wizards the following Spells and 20,000 Health:
[Hunger][Memory][Pictures][Poltergeist][Regret]
Correct Answer: Memory
"Indeed, memory is a fickle thing. It can deceive us, dismay us or remind us of happier days." - If answered correctly, Krokosphinx will cast a +100% Damage Trap on itself.

[Blade] [Aura] "All arms must wither!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm or helpful Aura, Lamia will cast Starfall on the recipient of the Charm or the caster of the aura Spell.
[Harmful Aura] "Death's agent withstands all!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Aura on Lamia, Lamia will cast Monk of Mourning (Tier 2b) on the caster of the Spell.
[Heal] "There is only decay" - If a Wizard casts a healing Spell, Lamia will cast Pathogen on the recipient of the Spell, or the caster if it was an All Allies Spell.
[Round] "You are slow to take in the world around us!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round and every fourth Round afterwards, Lamia will cast Scarecrow.

All Enemy Spells don't trigger this cheat.
This cheat also triggers whenever Half-Witted Skeleton uses Blizzard, or Tired Fighter uses Tempest.

name: Le Pingouin
cheats: "Le whu! Whu!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already started, Le Pingouin will cast Winter Moon at the Wizard.
"Seek and destroy, mes petites!" - At the end of each Round, if there are fewer Minions than Wizards currently in the duel, Le Pingouin will cast a Moon Spell to summon one of the following Minions:
"Whu! Whu!" - Every three Rounds, Le Pingouin will trigger his Minions to cast the following spells.
Clockwork Spanner (Death) will cast a multi-target version of Poison, which deals max Health Damage to self, and 700 Death Damage to all Wizards.
Clockwork Spanner (Fire) will cast a Sun multi-target version of Immolate, which deals max Health Damage to self, and 700 Fire Damage to all Wizards.
"Borealis is hard to pierce, eh?" - Clockwork Spanner (Storm) will cast a Star version of Insane Bolt which deals max Health Damage to self, and deals 700 Storm Damage to all Wizards.

Chromatic Blast
Frenzy

"My half-life means you're half done!" - When Leadite is defeated, at the end of the Round, two Uraniumites will join the duel.
"Ha! Even my half-lives have half-lives." - When a Uraniumite is defeated, at the end of the Round, two Protactimites will join the duel.

name: Loon Knight (Challenge)
cheats: Loon Knight

[Round] "Become Khonsu's Avatars!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Loon Knight will Polymorph each Luna-Tick Minion into one of the following forms for 99 Rounds, depending on the number of Wizards present:
2 or fewer: Old Quack (Challenge)
3 or more: Old Quack (Challenge), Quentin Quail (Challenge) and Drake Lockley (Challenge).
[Round] "I am the Beak of Vengeance!" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, Loon Knight will cast Shatterhorn (Moon) on a random Wizard.

[Polymorph] "The Moon Phases..." - If there are no Luna-Tick Minions present, Loon Knight will Polymorph into one of the above forms, retaining all cheats listed above while gaining all cheats of that Polymorph.
[Polymorph] "QUACK!" - Loon Knight will Polymorph into another one of the above forms, gaining the cheats of both that form and the previously Polymorphed form.
Old Quack

[Charm] "That's not the real you!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Helpful Charm, or a multi-blade Spell, such as Elemental Blade, Old Quack will cast an Ice version of Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
If a multi-target Blade Spell is cast, such as Bladestorm, Old Quack will cast an all enemies version of Enfeeble.
A wizard casting a blade-type spell that is not of their original school will also trigger this cheat.
Spells with secondary effects, such as Savage Paw, will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis will not prevent the removal of Blades.
Quentin Quail
"Let me see. Oh, yes! That's the ticket!" - If a Wizard casts one of the following hanging effects, Quentin will cheat-cast one of the following Spells:
Secondary effects, such as the Blade on Sound of Musicology, will trigger this cheat.
[Friend][Charm] Single Target Helpful Charm - Weakness
[All Friends (Squared)][Charm] Helpful Charm on All Allies - Mass Weakness
[Enemy][Curse] Single Target Harmful Charm - Balanceblade
Starfall will also trigger this cheat.
[All Enemies (Squared)][Curse] Harmful Charm on All Enemies - Bladestorm (+25%)
[Friend][Ward] Single Target Helpful Ward - Hex
[All Friends (Squared)][Ward] Helpful Ward on All Allies - Mass Hex
[Enemy][Jinx] Single Target Harmful Ward - Tower Shield (Balance)
Attenuate will also trigger this cheat.
[All Enemies (Squared)][Jinx] Harmful Ward on All Enemies - Legion Shield
Drake Lockley

[Blade][Trap][Aura][Global] "Time for the old one, two PUNCH!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Pierce Charm, Trap, Aura, or Global, Drake will cast a Storm version of Duck Savage that deals 800 Storm Damage on the receiver.
Multi-Target Spells will result in the Duck Savage being cast on the caster.
Secondary effects will trigger this cheat.

name: Loother Ray
cheats: [Late] "Another combatant? Bring it on!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Loother Ray will cast Bad Juju (Shadow) at the Wizard.
[Minion] "Come out and play!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Loother Ray casts a Shadow Spell that summons a random combination of two of the following: Fangs Guards and/or Fangs Assaulters.
[Feint] "You'll pay for that!" - If a Feint is placed on Loother Ray, he will cast a Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) that removes all unprotected Traps and Prisms on himself.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
[Heal] "I saw that coming" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cast Mass Infection (-65%).
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
[Steal] "Experience the strength of street knowledge" - He will randomly cheat-cast Steal Health.
spellNotes: Casts a multi-target version of Poison (45+450).

name: Lord Barghest (Challenge)
cheats: [Trap] "You think to trap the hunters?" - Triggers the first time a Negative Ward is applied to Lord Barghest or his minion(s).

"Your traps become our tools!" - Triggers the second time a Negative Ward is applied to Lord Barghest or his minion(s). When this cheat is triggered, all traps will be removed from Lord Barghest and his minion(s).
"*growl* I'll get you yet!" - After attacking Lord Barghest while he has a trap, Lord Barghest's minion will say this to indicate that it is safe to trap again.
Cheat Cycle

[Protected Damage over Time] "Now the real hunt begins!" - At the beginning of the first round, Lord Barghest will cast a protected Damage Over Time effect that deals 17,775 damage over 60 rounds, followed by a protected Damage Over Time effects that deals 118 damage over 60 rounds.

[Polymorph] "The coin flips in your favor, for now." - At the end of the third round, Lord Barghest will polymorph into his human form and cast a protected Damage Over Time effect that deals 17,775 damage over 60 rounds, followed by a protected Damage Over Time effects that deals 118 damage over 60 rounds.
Fire dispels can prevent the above cheats.
"*snarl*" - If a wizard casts a Positive Charm, this cheat will be triggered one round early.
In his human form, Lord Barghest's school changes to Ice and his boost/resist values are different.
[Polymorph] "And now... the coin flips to my favor." - At the end of the sixth round, Lord Barghest will polymorph back into his wolf form and cast a Sun version of Incindiate.
"*hrumph*" - If a wizard casts a Positive Charm, this cheat will be triggered one round early.
Minion Cheats
At the beginning of the first round and every other round afterwards, Lord Barghest's minion will cheat-cast one of the following cheats.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - Lord Barghest's minion will cast a special version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% damage charms to all allies.
[Shield] "Brace yourself!" - Lord Barghest's minion will cast Mass Tower Shield.
[Heal] "You will not fall!" - Lord Barghest's minion will cast a version of Pigsie that heals all allies and applies 3 heal over times for 4 rounds.
[Afterlife] "*whimper*" - After being defeated at the end of the turn, Lord Barghest's minion will revive itself for 100% of its max HP and will leave combat.
Helpful Cheats
In the earlier stages of the dungeon, three clues are spread throughout the area, each of which corresponds to one NPC. If the clue is found, that means the corresponding NPC is innocent.
Tranett = Silver Necklace
Tawni = Lock of Hair
Ignacio = Red Cloth
Dimiti = Strange Footprints

If the correct NPC is chosen, the following helpful cheats may activate. Each incorrect choice typically removes one helpful cheat, but helpful cheats frequently won't trigger even if no incorrect choices were made. More testing is needed to determine what causes cheats not to trigger.
[All Friends (Squared)][Aura] "Here to help, wizard!" - At the end of the first round, a special version of Quicken that lasts 6 rounds will be applied to all wizards.
[All Friends (Squared)][Manipulation] "Now, Barghest, your end comes." - At the end of the first round, a special version of Guardian Spirit that restores 100% hp will be applied to all wizards.
[All Friends (Squared)][Aura] "Don't hit me!" - Before Lord Barghest cheat-casts Incindiate, a special version of Fortify that reduces incoming damage by -135% will be applied to all wizards.
[All Friends (Squared)][Aura] "Now, you face Ignacio. Pity you." - After Lord Barghest casts Incindiate for the first time, the 6-round Quicken will be re-applied to all wizards.

name: Lord Nightshade (Challenge)
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness is unacceptable" - If a Wizard is late after the second Round, Boss will cast a Moon version of Ship of Fools dealing 100,000 Moon Damage. If a Damage cap is active, the Damage will be set to the maximum.
[Global] Equalize Cycle
"Your doom approaches" - At the start of Round 1, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 250.
"Closer" - At the start of Round 3, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 500.
"and closer..." - At the start of Round 5, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 1,000.
"It's almost here" - At the start of Round 7, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 2,000.
"Your doom is NOW!" - At the start of Round 10, the Boss will cast Doom and Gloom (Moon).

"You wish to hasten your doom?" - If a Wizard changes the Global, Lord Nightshade will immediately replace the Equalize, resetting the Damage cap to 250. The cycle will continue normally, and future Equalize casts will set the Damage cap to the proper limit for that round.
[Round] End of Round Cycle

"Kneel! For I am the harbinger of your demise!" - At the end of every Round, Lord Nightshade will interrupt with a Moon version of Deer Knight dealing 100,000 Moon Damage. If a Damage cap is active, the Damage will be set to the maximum.
If a Wizard changes the global, the Deer Knight will deal 250 damage due to the Equalize being temporarily reset.

name: Ludus the Beastmaster
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Ludus the Beastmaster will cheat-cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
"My beasts will defend me!" - Every 4 Rounds, he will cast Attack Sea Lion which summons an Attack Sea Lion.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Feint is placed on Ludus, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward which swaps the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Melt can be used to Dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
"I alone can unleash these beasts!" - If a Trap is placed on an Attack Sea Lion, he will interrupt-cast Cleanse Ward.
Vaporize can be used to Dispel this cheat.
"Ha! We shake off your measly charms!" - Whenever a single target negative Charm (excluding Dispel) is placed on a Minion, he will cast Cleanse Charm.
Secondary Spell debuffs will trigger this cheat.
"No one hurts my beasts but me!" - If a Wizard attacks one of the Attack Sea Lions, he will interrupt-cast Berserk.
"My beast! Must... Avenge... GRR!" - If an Attack Sea Lion is defeated, he will cast an enhanced version of Berserk (+45%/+35%).
spellNotes: His Beastmaster's Eagle Pet casts Bladestorm.

name: Luminous Prophet
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Field of Reeds
cheats: "Death rules here. I doubt even Taweret could shield you." - At the beginning of the first Round, Luminous Prophet casts a negative version of Acrimony on all Wizards that turns all incoming Healing into Damage for 99 Rounds.
Luminous Prophet will not recast the Aura if replaced.
"WE ARE DEATH!" - At the beginning of the first Round and every 6th Round afterwards, Luminous Prophet will cast a version of Anubis that deals 666 Death Damage to all Wizards.
"Allow us to help you..." - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm, Luminous Prophet will cast a Shadow version of Blessing that restores 666 Health to all Wizards.
"You will destroy yourself." - If Forgotten Soul is defeated, it will cast Regenerate (Tier 5a) on Luminous Prophet.
spellNotes: Casts an all enemies version of Shadow Trap.

name: Luska Charmbeak
cheats: Luska will remove traps that are placed on him.
Pet spells will not trigger this cheat.
Only the first trap will be removed from spells that apply more than one trap.
Traps with Indemnity enchantment will prevent any traps from being removed.
AoE traps such as Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.

"Only attack if you have been inked!" - At the beginning of each Round, Luska will "Ink" the Wizard in the first spot (Sun spot), which applies a Smoke Mantle. All other Wizards will receive a Feint (this does not apply the +30% trap on himself).
"Still fighting? Have some more ink!" - When he loses 25% of his health (12,690 health remaining), he will also Ink the second Wizard (Eye spot) each Round.
When he loses 50% of his health (8,460 health remaining), he will also Ink the third Wizard (Star spot)each Round.
He will never Ink the fourth Wizard (Moon spot).

"If you can still see me, don't look at me!" - If a non-inked Wizard casts a single-target spell at Luska (e.g. attacks, traps, negative charms), he will interrupt to remove all wards from himself and all Wizards, then cast either Skeletal Dragon (at the Wizard who cast the spell), dealing 1,200 Death damage or Power Link (at the Wizard who cast the spell), dealing 2,100 Fire damage and 600 healing for 3 rounds.
Only Wizards who have been Inked can cast single-target spells at Luska without retaliation. He will still remove traps from himself.
Any Wizard can cast attack all-enemy spells without triggering this cheat.

name: Lyon Lorestriker
locations: Wysteria: Pegasus Place - Tower of the Helephant
cheats: Minion Cycle:
"Welcome to the party!" - At the end of every Round, he will summon one Lyon Lorestriker (Minion) starting with 1,200 Health.

”Friends, stay a bit longer!” - If Lyon has 50% or less of his total health remaining, the new minions summoned start with 2,000 Health and an extra Power Pip. Any existing minions will not be replaced until they are defeated.
Rarely, Lyon Lorestriker may summon multiple minions, or not summon a minion at all.
"Tsk tsk. Another friend for you!" - Lyon Lorestriker will summon an additional minion during the same round if the Wizard casts any following spells:
If a Wizard uses a Heal Over Time spell (e.g. Regenerate, Sprite), he will cast a Life Spell that removes the HoT on the Wizard who received it.
Pet spells do not trigger this cheat.
Availing Hands does not trigger this cheat.
If a Trap or Prism is placed on him, he will use Cleanse Ward.
Multi-target Traps or Prisms such as Windstorm and Mass Storm Prism do not activate this cheat.
This cheat can be avoided using Indemnity or Vaporize.
"Crowds just warm my icy heart!" - Once Lyon Lorestriker acquires three minions, he will cast a version of Blizzard dealing 750-790 Ice Damage at the beginning of every Round.
"We need a bigger crowd!" - If all Minions are defeated, he will once again summon them at the end of each Round.

name: Malistaire the Undying (Shadow)
cheats: [Manipulation] "I cannot be stopped" - At the beginning of the first Round, Malistaire will cheat-cast a Shadow Mega Taunt on the first Wizard.
This cheat restores Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) to 100 health and spawns as Haunted Malistaire after being defeated for the first time.

[Blade] [Trap] "What was yours is mine" - If a Wizard casts a universal Damage Blade (e.g. Balanceblade) or universal positive Damage Ward, he will cast Steal Charm or Steal Ward on the Wizard that received the effect.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade or Ward Spell, such as Bladestorm or Legion Shield is used, only the caster will be affected.
Secondary Spell effects, such as the Ward from Abominable Weaver, will not trigger this cheat.
If a universal Trap (e.g. Feint) is placed on Malistaire, he will cast Cleanse Ward on himself.
If a universal negative Charm (e.g. Black Mantle) is placed on Malistaire, he will cast Cleanse Charm on himself.
Pet Spells, Life Feint, and secondary Spells other than negative Damage Charms will trigger this cheat.
All Enemy Charms and Wards, (e.g. Mass Feint), and negative Health Charms (e.g Infection) will not trigger this cheat.
If Elemental Defuse or Spirit Defuse is used, Malistaire the Undying will use a version of Cleanse Charm to remove all three Dispel Charms.
[Weakness] "I grow stronger every moment" - If any Weakness is placed on Malistaire, he will cast a version of Empower that deals 300 Death Damage on himself and gains 3 pips.
Weakness-based pet spells, roshambo spells (even if no Weakness is placed on Malistaire), and secondary Spell effects (e.g. Efreet) will trigger this cheat.
[Damage] "Can you face my stare?" - If a Wizard uses a single target Damage Spell against Malistaire, he will cast Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip) on the Wizard after the attack.
The Secondary Interrupt Cycle after attacking Malistaire before the last round can remove the Mantle from the Wizard.
Interrupt Cycles Malistaire's first form revolves around two cheat cycles; a "Primary Interrupt Cycle" and a "Secondary Interrupt Cycle." Both cycles overlap each other throughout the battle.

[Moon] "Fear the power of Dragonspyre" - At the end of the third Round in this cycle, Malistaire will polymorph into a Black Dragon and attack a random Wizard, dealing 535 Moon Damage and destroying any Aura effects.
[Sun] "A gift from Tartarus" - At the end of the fourth Round, he will cast a Sun version of Deadly Minotaur on a random Wizard.
[Star] "My curse upon you!" - At the end of the fifth Round, he will cast an Aura that grants him +20%, +15%, and +10% outgoing Death Damage and three +15% Power Pip Chance for five Rounds.
"Its power shakes your spells apart!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round, Malistaire's Skeletal Dragon will remove all Charms and Wards from each Wizard.
Wizards using Empowerment or Adapt will gain one Pip or Power Pip, respectively.

"I touch Shadow!" - Haunted Malistaire, a Shadow Boss, will spawn within the battle after Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) has been defeated or reaches below 1,000 health. The Boss interrupts a Shadow Spell at the beginning of the round that sets its max health to 48,000 and restores 99,999 Health, followed by a Secondary Interrupt Cycle.
This cheat occurs once, even if Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is not defeated.

If Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is not defeated and only Haunted Malistaire is defeated first, he will revive himself with 100 Health, retain his stats from Haunted Malistaire, and delay his Shadow version of Primary Interrupt Cycle by one round.
Malistaire's Health after healing can be decreased to 25,050 if a Treasure Card Doom and Gloom is present when Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is defeated.
A regular Doom and Gloom will not reduce his health below 48,000.
If Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is not defeated, the health can be reduced further using negative Healing Charms, such as Mass Infection.
Haunted Malistaire retains all the above cheats except his Primary Interrupt Cycle, along with the one cheat below.
"Curse you beast! Do not defy me!" - Malistaire's Skeletal Dragon will randomly place a +100% universal Trap on Malistaire and his allies.
Interrupt Cycles The "Primary Interrupt Cycle" is replaced with a Shadow version. The "Secondary Interrupt Cycle" stays the same.
"I call upon Shadow" - At the end of the first Round, he will cast Shadowblade on himself.
"I call upon Shadow" - At the end of the second Round, he will cast Shadowspear on himself.
"I call upon Shadow" - At the end of the third Round, he will cast an All Enemies version of Shadow Trap.
"I will show you the Shadow" - At the end of the fourth Round, he will cast Shadow Steal, which deals 1,600 Shadow Damage, and converts 100% of the Damage to Health.
"Its power shakes your spells apart!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, Malistaire's Skeletal Dragon will remove all Charms and Wards from each Wizard.
spellNotes: Even though the Spell text does not indicate this, it has been observed that Skeletal Dragon (666+333) does Damage to All Enemies.
Lord of Night (625) applies a -40% Healing Ward to the target.

name: Malus (Challenge)

[Round] "Embrace your hatred!" - At the beginning of every Round, Malus will cast one of the following Spells:
Black Mantle (-55%, 3 Pip)
Choke
Efreet (1,000) - Targets Wizard with the lowest amount of Health
Monster Mash (1,170) - Targets Wizard with the highest amount of Health
Muzzle

[Manipulation] "Heroism is vanity. FIGHT ME!" - If a Wizard targets Malus while his "Flames of Hatred" Aura is active, he will cast a 0 Damage Star version of Supernova on himself, removing the Aura.
[Afterlife] "Good. AGAIN!" - If Malus is defeated while the "Flames of Hatred" Aura is active, Malus will heal himself for 50% Health, and applies a +200% outgoing Damage Aura on himself.

name: Mandrake the Maligician
locations: Lemuria: Wildlands - Heart of the Wilds
cheats: "Late Arrivals Disrupt the Show" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Mandrake will cast Camp Bandit (Tier 5a) on them.

"I Am Unstoppable!" - At the start of the battle, Mandrake puts on a -10% version of Fortify that lasts four Rounds. Each succeeding Round that he takes new Damage, Mandrake will cast a new version of the Aura Spell that increases his Resist by -10%, until it is at -100% Damage. (Note: This Resist Aura is not always shown in the Creature's effects.)
Later ticks of Damage over Time Spells do not appear to trigger this increase.
"Feel the Power of Mandrake" - If Mandrake is at full health at the end of a Round, he'll cast a -15% Health Leafstorm that removes 15% of the total Health of the target to all enemies.
After this spell is cast, Enfeeble will be cast on all enemies.
Leafstorm will trigger Adapt's spell requirement giving a Power Pip.
If Mandrake is not at full health at the end of a Round, he'll cast a version of Blessing to restore 5% of his total Health (+698).
Entangle will not prevent this heal.
"By the Power of Mali-Magic" - If a Healing Spell is cast, Mandrake will cast a +20% Dragonspear on the healer.
Pet May Casts will trigger this cheat.
"I knew you would try that!" - If Mandrake's Aura is removed or changed, he will change his Aura to a -100% version of Fortify.
If Mandrake's Aura is replaced with a negative Aura, such as with Pathogen, this cheat will be activated and the Aura's appearance will be altered, but the effects of it will not.
If Humango Assistant is not defeated by the time Mandrake's -10% Aura expires, this cheat will be triggered.

If Humango Assistant is defeated by the time Mandrake's -10% Aura expires, Mandrake will replace it with a -20% version of Fortify, triggering this cheat 1 Round later. (needs verification for trigger)
spellNotes: Casts a version of Life Feint that affects all enemies.

name: Marcio
cheats: During the first Round, Marcio will use his turn to cast a -5% version of Doom and Gloom.
If a Wizard casts a Global Spell, then Marcio will interupt-cast the -5% version of Doom and Gloom.
If a Trap Spell is placed on Marcio, he will interrupt-cast Cleanse Ward to remove it.
If a multi-Trap Spell such as Elemental Trap is cast, then Marcio will remove only the last Trap cast.
Multi-target Trap Spells such as Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.

"Your desperation pains me!" - If a Wizard attacks Marcio when they have 1/3 health, then he will interrupt to damage himself for the following amount based on the number of spots filled by the Wizard team:
800 Ice Damage (1 Wizard present)
500 Random Damage (2 Wizards present)
This attack is currently bugged and does not deal Ice Damage, nor does it deal damage of any of the seven primary Schools of Magic.
400 Ice Damage (3 Wizards present)
300 Ice Damage (4 Wizards present)
Even though the Spells that deal Ice Damage are not cast by Marcio, they can still be dispelled.

name: Marcus
locations: Krokotopia: Underground Market - Shrine of Marcus
cheats: [Round] "WOOF! Let's play!" - Marcus will say this at the beginning of the first Round.
[Blade][Global][Trap] "Fetchy-Fetchy!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Global, or Trap, Marcus will cast a version of Frozen Armor on himself that absorbs 800 Damage.
"Zoomies!" - If there are 3 or more helpful Wards on Marcus at the end of a Round, he will cast a protected Iceblade (+40%) on himself.

name: Marduk ThunderHeart

name: Margherita Vizzini
cheats: "Riposte!" - If Margherita is damaged by a Spell, then she will interrupt-cast Mythblade on herself, then use her Natural Attack on the Wizard that attacked her.
This Mythblade will stack with her regularly cast Mythblade.
If Margherita is damaged more than once by a single Spell, then she will use this cheat for every time that she is damaged on her attacker.
Ticks from Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
If this cheat triggers while Margherita has no Pips, then her Natural Attack will act as though she has one Pip.
Although her Natural Attack is not cast when used through this cheat, it can still be Dispelled.
"I have studied in Crab Alley!" - Margherita will interrupt-cast Storm Prism and then use Triton on a Wizard due to a few triggers.
If there are two Wizards present in the duel, then this cheat will trigger on the first Wizard to cast any kind of Spell.
May-Cast Spells will trigger this cheat.

If there was initially only one Wizard present in the duel, and a second joins later or a Minion is summoned, then this cheat will activate on the first Wizard to cast any kind of Spell when the second character becomes active in the duel.
This cheat will not activate if there are initially more than three Wizards in the duel and a Wizard(s) flee, leaving only two Wizards left.
If a Lanyon Scratcher is defeated, then this cheat will trigger on whoever defeated the Scratcher.
This cheat will only trigger once if more than one Laynon Scrathcer is defeated with a single Spell.
This cheat will only trigger once if there are only two Wizards present in the duel and the first Spell cast by the Wizard team defeats a Lanyon Scratcher(s).
This cheat will not trigger if a Lanyon Scratcher is defeated by a Damage over Time effect.
Although Triton is not cast, it can be Dispelled.

"I pierce your defenses!" - If Margherita uses her Natural Attack on a Wizard and they still have a Shield or Trap placed on them after her Natural Attack, then she will interrupt to use Pierce on the Wizard that she attacked.

"I disarm your words!" - If Margherita uses her Natural Attack on a Wizard and they still have a Shield, Trap, or positive Charm placed on them after her Natural Attack, or there is an active Global Spell, and her previously used Natural Attack activated her Pierce cheat, then she will interrupt to use Disarm on the Wizard that she attacked.
Although Disarm is not cast, it can be Dispelled.
"I invoke the power of Myth!" - At the beginning of the Round after Margherita's Health drops to 50% or lower, she will interrupt-cast a +35% version of Time of Legend.
"I'll back you into a trap!" - At the beginning of every Round after Margherita's Health drops to 50% or lower, she will interrupt-cast Myth Trap on a random Wizard.

name: Margherita Vizzini (Challenge)
cheats: [Myth][Ward] "Tropement!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Margherita Vizzini will cast Myth Trap on the recipient of the Ward.
[Myth][Curse] "Derobement!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm, Margherita Vizzini will cast Myth Trap on the caster.
[Damage] "Riposte!" - If Margherita is damaged by a Spell, she will cast Myth Trap and then her Natural Attack on the Wizard that attacked her.
If Margherita is damaged more than once by a single Spell, she will use this cheat for every time that she is damaged.
Ticks from Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
If this cheat triggers while Margherita has no Pips, then her Natural Attack will act as though she has one Pip.

[Spell] "I have studied in Crab Alley!" - When a Wizard defeats a Lanyon Scratcher, Margherita will cast Storm Prism and then Triton on the Wizard.

cheats: "My people won't let me fall!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Mayor McCreary will cast a Sun spell to summon Enthralled Villager (Balance), Enthralled Villager (Life), and Enthralled Villager (Storm) and will cast an Invulnerability Aura lasting indefinitely.

If Mayor McCreary is not defeated within three Rounds of his Frenzy cast (including the round he casts it), he will re-summon his minions and Invulnerability Aura.

cheats: After Sparck's first cycle, Medulla will command these cheats.
"Mr. Sparck, man the Essence Cannon" - This is a one Round warning before Sparck fires the Essence Cannon.
"Fire!" - At the end of the Round, Sparck will use a Shadow Spell to fire the Essence Cannon, which deals 1,050 Shadow Damage to all Wizards and removes one Shadow Pip from each Wizard.
Before Beans' Blossom's Thorns cheat, Medulla will use the following cheat.
"Prepare to be mind-meddled!" - Medulla will cast a Shadow Spell to randomly 'Confuse' a Wizard for three Rounds (begins in the following Round).
All Spells have a chance of targeting a random ally or enemy.
Pet may-casts will keep their original target.
If a Wizard attempts to pass while 'Confused', a random Spell will be selected from the Wizard's hand.
This action will not take place if the Wizard is stunned.
"Sparck! I need technical support" - Medulla will shout this out whenever a Wizard casts an Aura, and Sparck will cast Supernova (535, 75%).
Cycle 1 - Sparck will begin the first cycle

"As you wish, Boss" - Sparck will cast Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip).
"You're up Mr. Sparck" - Medulla will shout this out, and Sparck will cast Niles' Knock-Out.
Beans
"What’s up doc?” - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Beans will cast an all allies Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2).

"[Aftershock]" - He will shout this out after using Earthquake (525).
"Your combat skills are laughable, ha!" - Once all his Minions are defeated, further attempts to Feint will result in all negative Wards and Charms being removed from Medulla.

Beans (Ally)
"Hold still--I'll have you fixed in a jiffy!" - Beans will randomly cast Pigsie, restoring 550 Health to all Wizards.

name: Medulla (Standard)
cheats: At the end of the first Round, all Wizards will be given 3 of the following Spell, which instantly removes Pork, Beans, or Sparck from combat, and moves them to the Wizard team:
[Clear Mind]
If Pork, Beans, or Sparck is healed on the same Round that Clear Mind is cast on them, they regain their prior Health, and remain on Medulla's team.
"Pork!" / "You freed my mind, Wizard. Let's finish this!" - When Pork is defeated, he will assist the Wizard team.
"We must work together!" - Randomly at the beginning of the Round, Pork will cast Smoke Screen (-45%) at the enemy team.
"Beans!" / "The doctor is in!" - When Beans is defeated, he will assist the Wizard team.
"Hold still - I'll have you fixed up in a jiffy!" - Beans will randomly cast a version of Pigsie, restoring 550 Health to all Wizards.
"Sparck!" / "Medulla's mind-control spell is broken. Fascinating!" - When Sparck is defeated, he will assist the Wizard team.

"Preparing to fire the Essence Cannon" - At the end of the Round, Sparck will cast a Shadow Spell to fire the Essence Cannon, which deals 6,000 Shadow Damage to all enemies and removes one Shadow Pip from each enemy.

name: Melanj
locations: Mirage: Caterwaul Canyons - Cavern of the Wurm
cheats: "*Melanj Mana Burn*" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Melanj will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
"*Melanj rules only*" - At the beginning of Round 1, Melanj will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%).
If a Wizard tries to replace the Doom and Gloom with another type of Global Spell, Melanj will recast Doom and Gloom.
This cheat can be bypassed by using Strangle.
If Melanj replaces the Doom and Gloom with another type of Global Spell (e.g. Gaze of Fate.), the Global Spell won't change.
"*Melanj spice strike*" - At the end of every fifth Round, Melanj will cast Sand Wurm at a random Wizard.
"*Wurm domination*" - If a Wizard uses a Blade Spell, then Melanj will interrupt-cast her Natural Attack, Steal Charm (Shadow), and a Weakness at the Caster.
A Global Spell, such as Darkwind, will trigger this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade Spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), Melanj will only target the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Shadow Shrike's Dragonspear will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis will prevent the blade from being removed but the weakness will still be placed.
Blades given from spells will not trigger this cheat.
Pet casts will trigger this cheat.
"*Wurm plague*" - If a Wizard uses a Feint, there is a chance that Melanj will cast Virulent Plague.
Life Feint will trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: Casts a multi-target version of Poison (45+450).

name: Melweena Smite
locations: Wizard City: Firecat Alley - Forlorn Tower

name: Meowiarty (Challenge)
cheats: [Stun] "I've taken the liberty of inviting some of your old friends" - At the beginning of the first Round, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast a Star Spell to summon three Protection Pox (Challenge) minions, and a version of Blinding Light that doesn't place a stun shield.

[Afterlife] "Settle in. We've much to catch up on." - At the beginning of the second Round, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast a Star version of Guardian Spirit that restores 100% Health after defeat on all allies.
[Shield] "Shields up!" - At the beginning of the second Round, and every three Rounds after, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast Legion Shield.

name: Meowiarty (Rank 12)
cheats: Meowiarty will cleanse any negative Charms off of him and his allies.
Dr. Von Katzenstein will use this cheat to clear off any negative Charms.

"Let's start this party off with a bang!" - At the start of Round 1, he will attack a random Wizard, leaving a DoT around them for five Rounds. On the fifth Round, the DoT will detonate and deal ~2,500 Myth Damage to the affected Wizard, then place +10% "lock" Traps on him and his allies.
"Tisk, tisk!" - Using Triage or Mass Triage will trigger a 2,000 Shadow Damage Insane Bolt on the receiver.
Gibson O'Leary who cast Detonate on a Wizard with a DoT will not trigger this cheat.

Shift and Cooldown do not trigger this cheat. Shift works. Cooldown is trickier. It must be cast between the 4th and the 5th tick. The turn Meowiarty casts it is turn 1. To remove the last tick from a Wizard before removing it from your Wizard, cast Cooldown on turn 4, because the 4th tick has already happened. If the receiver of the cheat follows you in the battle circle, cast Cooldown on turn 5 (the 4th tick happened last turn), and the 5th tick is about to happen. This does not change even if Cooldown is cast more than once during that cycle. The last large tick will be removed if Cooldown is cast between the 4th and 5th tick.

"Blast! You foiled my ingenious plan!" - If all the "locks" are removed, he will cheat-cast Mass Triage on himself and proceed to throw the Myth DoT again on a Wizard (identical to the one thrown in the cheat "Let's start this party off with a bang!").
"Poor Wizard, you can't win for losing." - If the last "lock" is removed by an AoE spell, he will cheat-cast his Shadow Insane Bolt on the Wizard who cast the AoE.
Scald does not trigger this cheat.
"Tick... Tock... Boom! Too late!" - If any of the "locks" remain after five Rounds, he will remove all negative and positive Wards on him and his allies, and replace the "locks".
"Every star fears the Supernova!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell, the O'Leary Thug will cheat-cast Supernova on the Wizard.
He will also say this if a Wizard casts Shadow Shrike, but will not actually perform the cheat.
"A thug's job is to balance the fight!" - If a Wizard has five or more Pips after casting a Spell, he will cheat-cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"Keep up the good work!" - If an enemy successfully casts an attack, the Midnighter will cheat-cast Empower on the ally.
"Bad luck abounds!" - If an enemy Spell fizzles, he will cheat-cast Black Cat (100-140) on the fizzled Spell's target (or a random Wizard if the Spell was intended for an ally).
"Sharp move, my friend!" - If an enemy casts a Blade Spell, he will cheat-cast Dark Pact on the receiver of the Blade.
"Tis a good time for my monster to come out to play!" - At the end of every third Round, Dr. Von Katzenstein will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster on a random Wizard.
"Don't worry, there's time for everyone to play!" - At the start of every fourth Round, he will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard.
"It's not safe to run with Blades!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, he will cheat-cast Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
"One must keep a sanitary work environment!" - If a Wizard casts a negative Charm, he will use Cleanse Charm on the receiver of a negative Charm.
"Uh oh, you dropped your little Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Gibson O'Leary will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the blade, then he will proceed to destroy it.
"I think that Blade would look better on me!" - If a universal Blade, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade.
"You can't mess with us!" - If any type of universal Trap is placed on an enemy, he will cheat-cast Backdraft on the Wizard who cast the Trap.
"Allow me to lower the boom!" - When a single Trap or DoT is placed on a Wizard, he will cheat-cast Detonate on the Wizard.
"For me? You shouldn't have!" - If any type of universal Shield Ward is cast, Jacques the Scratcher will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield.
Legion Shield only affects the caster.
"No need for such things here!" - If any type of School-specific Shield Ward is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield, and then proceed to destroy it.
"You can't have nice things when there are cats around!" - If any type of Heal over time spell is cast, he will cheat-cast Snow Drift on the receiver of the heal.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Allow me to scratch some of that dirt off you, friend." - If an enemy takes Damage from a DoT Spell, he will cheat-cast Cooldown on the ally.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage.

name: Meowiarty (Rank 14)
cheats: "Let's start this party off with a bang!" - At the start of the first Round, he will attack a random Wizard, leaving a defered DoT that deals 2,345 Shadow Damage after 3 Rounds.
"Tisk, tisk!" - Using Triage or Mass Triage will trigger a 2,000 Shadow Damage Insane Bolt on the caster.
After every five Rounds, Meowiarty will place +10% "lock" Traps Damage Ward on him and his Allies.
"Blast! You foiled my ingenious plan!" - If all the "locks" Damage Wards are removed, he will cheat-cast Mass Triage on himself.

"Poor Wizard, you can't win for losing." - If the last "lock" Damage Ward is removed by an Attack All Enemies Spell, he will cheat-cast a 2,000 Shadow Damage Insane Bolt on the Wizard who cast the AoE.
"Tick... Tock... Boom! Too late!" - If any of the "locks" Damage Wards remain after five Rounds, he will remove all "Locks" and apply the same Shadow defered DoT and replace the "locks."
"Every star fears the Supernova!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell, the O'Leary Thug will cheat-cast Supernova (535, 85%) on the Wizard.
"A thug's job is to balance the fight!" - If a Wizard has five or more Pips while casting a Spell, he will cheat-cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"Keep up the good work!" - If an enemy successfully casts an attack, the Midnighter will cheat-cast Empower on the Ally.

"Bad luck abounds!" - If an enemy Spell fizzles, he will cheat-cast a version of Black Cat dealing 1,000 Damage on the fizzled Spells' target (or a random Wizard if the Spell was intended for an Ally).
"Sharp move, my friend!" - If an enemy casts a Blade Spell, he will cheat-cast Dark Pact (+30%) on the receiver of the Blade.
"Tis a good time for my monster to come out to play!" - At the end of every third Round, Dr. Von Katzenstein will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster (1,005) on a random Wizard.
"Don't worry, there's time for everyone to play!" - At the start of every fourth Round, he will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard.
"It's not safe to run with Blades!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Damage Charm, he will cheat-cast Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
"One must keep a sanitary work environment!" - If a Wizard casts a negative Damage Charm, he will cast an AoE version of Cleanse Charm.

"Uh oh, you dropped your little Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Damage Charm, Gibson O'Leary will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade, then uses a Fire version of Cleanse Charm to remove the Blade from himself.
"I think that Blade would look better on me!" - If a universal Blade Damage Charm, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade.
"You can't mess with us!" - If any type of universal Trap Damage Ward is placed on an enemy, he will cheat-cast Backdraft on the Wizard who cast the Trap.
"Allow me to lower the boom!" - When a single Trap Damage Ward or DoT is placed on a Wizard, he will cheat-cast Detonate on the Wizard.
"For me? You shouldn't have!" - If any type of universal Shield Ward is cast, Jacques the Scratcher will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield.
"No need for such things here!" - If any type of School-specific Shield Ward is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield, and then proceed to destroy it.
"You can't have nice things when there are cats around!" - If any type of Heal over time Healing Spell is cast, he will cheat-cast Snow Drift on the receiver of the Heal.
Pet Heals will trigger this cheat.
"Allow me to scratch some of that dirt off you, friend." - If an enemy takes Damage from a DoT Spell, he will cheat-cast Cooldown on the Ally.

name: Mr. Cane
cheats: "Careful now. Mind the Hostages." - At the beginning of Round 1, Mr. Cane will cast a 6,000 "Restraints" Damage Absorb Virulence Aura for 99 Rounds on both Hostages.
The Aura can sometimes prevent Criticals from doubling Damage, or prevent Traps from being activated.
If Mr. Cane is defeated while either of the Hostages are still in combat, he will use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% max Health to all allies, and will recast the Auras.
Removing the Aura will also trigger this cheat.

"Worthless hostages can't even stay captured!" - If a Hostage takes more than 0 Damage (more than 6,000 after the Absorb Aura) in one hit, Mr. Cane will force the Wizard to cast Exit Combat on the Hostage, removing them from combat.
Drain Spells will go directly through the Aura, triggering this cheat.
"I'll show you corruption!" - At the beginning of every third Round, Mr. Cane will cast Poison.

name: Mr. Carver
cheats: [Life] Mr. Carver
Every Round, before casting a Spell, Mr. Carver will say one of the following lines:

"I will do whatever I can!" - Mr. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. Before his next turn, Mr. Carver will cast version of Imp that deals 85 Life Damage with no additional effects.

"Please, just help me, help yourself" - Mr. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. Before his next turn, Mr. Carver will cast version of The World that deals 930 Life Damage with no additional effects.
[Ice] Ms. Carver
Every Round after the first, before casting a Spell, Ms. Carver will say one of the following lines:

"I will never let my child go!" - Ms. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. After her next turn, Ms. Carver will cast version of Frost Beetle that deals 85 Ice Damage with no additional effects.

"You do not do enough!" - Ms. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. After her next turn, Ms. Carver will cast version of The Hierophant that deals 930 Ice Damage with no additional effects.

name: Mr. Stone
cheats: [Polymorph] "By sun's light, I am as stone" - At the end of the first and fourth Round, and every fifth Round after (1, 4, 9, 14, etc.), Mr. Stone will cast Power Nova on himself. This deals no damage and Polymorphs him into his Statue form for 2 Rounds.
When in this Statue form, Mr. Stone is immune to all damage.
[Damage] "*Low unhappy growl*" - If Mr. Stone is attacked while in his statue Polymorph, he will unpolymorph himself, making him vulnerable to damage again.

[Polymorph] "You sat idle like stones, so stones you shall become!" - If Mr. Stone’s Statue Polymorph expires naturally (meaning he was not attacked during the Statue phase), he will cast Medusa on the Wizard. This deals no damage but Polymorphs the Wizard into a Statue for 99 Rounds.

Polymorph has a deck of 77 Cards, all being Pass Cards, except for one Break Free (Myth) Card. The Pass Spells can be discarded and the Wizard may Pass normally to avoid a spell casting animation. The Wizard can be healed while polymorphed.
If all Wizards are already a Statue, Mr. Stone will not cast Medusa after his Statue Polymorph expires naturally.
This cheat can be dispelled with Vaporize.

cheats: "You're just in time to bask in my full, lustrous glory!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Mulleted Neaux will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.
"Argghh... I didn't ask for a trim" - If Mulleted Neaux takes more than 50% of his max Health, he will cast Choke.
"No matter, hair grows back!" - Mulleted Neaux will cast a version of Heartbeat that restores 1,500 Health every Round until he is fully healed.
"Glory be! My mullet is safe!" - Mulleted Neaux will respond with this when he's fully healed, restarting the cheat cycle.
"Fool! You must trim before you shave!" - If Mulleted Neaux is defeated before his trim cheat is triggered, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.

name: Mykedee
locations: Empyrea: Aeriel Shores - Beastman Fight Club
cheats: [Late] "You ready to fight for your right to be tardy?" - If a Wizard enters the duel late, Mykedee will cheat-cast Metalops that leaves a +200% protected Death Trap on the Wizard and Beguiles the Wizard for three Rounds.
Cheat will also trigger if Yowck and Adrok are defeated, leaving Mykedee the last one in the duel, and it will trigger on all Wizards that aren't already Beguiled.
[Blade] If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell on another target, Mykedee will perform one of the following actions:
"No way, Wizard" - The first time, Mykedee will cast a Bad Juju on the target.
"This ain't no Charm School" - The second time, Mykedee will cast Virulent Plague.
"Third time ain't a charm!" - The third time, Mykedee will cast Metalops. After the third time, the cycle begins again.
Damage Spells with a secondary effect Blade, like on Savage Paw, will result in the action being cast on the target of the Damage.
All Ally Blade Spells will not trigger this cheat.
[Healing] If a Wizard casts a Healing spell, Mykedee will perform one of the following actions:
"No healing, it time to get ill!" - The first time, Mykedee will cast Infection on the Wizard.
"Feeling better? I fix that!" - The second time, Mykedee will cast Infection on the Wizard.
"Ha! Now everyone get ill!" - The third time, Mykedee will cast Mass Infection. After the third time, the cycle begins again.
"Hold it -- now hit it!" - After casting his Natural Attack, Mykedee will follow up with a Feint (+75%) on the target.
"Time for one last Beatdown" - Randomly, if Beets is in the battle, Mykedee will use his turn to cast Guardian Spirit (20%) on Beets.

name: Narallor Nightborn
cheats: If Narallor Nightborn is damaged by any of the seven primary Schools of Magic, he will interrupt-cast a Blade of the opposite School used to attack him on himself.
[Fire] - Fireblade & Helephant
[Ice] - Iceblade & a version of Colossus that deals 460-540 Ice Damage
[Storm] - Stormblade & a version of Stormzilla that deals 650-730 Storm Damage
[Life] - Lifeblade & Centaur
[Myth] - Mythblade & Orthrus
[Death] - Deathblade & Wraith
Balance attacks will result in Narallor Nightborn receiving a Deathblade.
Each tick from Damage over Time (DoT) Spells can trigger this cheat, although he will apply the Blade after his regular turn as opposed to before if he goes second.
Dispels can prevent this cheat.
If Narallor Nightborn is damaged by the same primary School twice in a row, he will interrupt-cast a 0 Pip Damage Spell of the opposite School used to attack him on the caster.
Balance Damage attacks that are used twice in a row will trigger Narallor Nightborn to use Wraith on the caster.
Each tick from Damage over Time (DoT) Spells can trigger this cheat, although he will use the attack after his regular turn as opposed to before if he goes second.
Dispels can be used to prevent this cheat.

cheats: [Heal over Time] "The Life Energy won't stop!" - At the beginning of every Round, Nethellnax will interrupt-cast Ha-ha! A 1-Up! on himself.
[Healing] If Nethellnax is defeated while his Heal over Time cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "I am not this, I am Death!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, and when Nethellnax revives, he gives Wizards the following Spell:
[Skeletal Pirate (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
[Death] "Is that one of my tricks?" - If a Wizard casts Skeletal Pirate (Tier 2b) on Nethellnax, he removes all Heal over Time effects on himself and stops his Heal over Time cheat.

"What... what just happened?" - Other Spells that remove Heal over Time effects for Weaknesses or Shields, such as Dr. Von's Monster (Tier 2b) or Primal Stampede, will trigger Nethellnax to remove all Heal over Time effects on himself and stop his Heal over Time cheat.

name: Neumian Lion
locations: Empyrea: Southwest Aero Plains - Meowl Den
cheats: "*ROAR*" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, the Boss will cast Impede and two Indemnity protected universal Traps for +25% and +35%.

"*GROWL*" On the first turn, the Neumian Lion will cast Tower Shield (Star) on itself, as well as a -50% version of Fortify. Aura will be re-cast on the last Round of its cycle. Supernova makes the Boss recast Aura.
"*GRAWWWW*" - Every tenth Round, starting on the ninth for the first cast, the Boss will cast Chimera (Star) on a random Wizard.

locations: Wizard City: Arena - Celestia Battlegrounds,Arena - Mirage Battlegrounds
spellNotes: Casts a 2-Pip version of Power Conversion that gives the target 1 regular Pip upon casting and grants the target 1 Pip from incoming Spells ranked 2+.
Casts a 4-Pip version of Invigorate that lasts for 4 Rounds.
Casts a version of Spiked Buckler that applies a +75 Damage Blade and an unprotected 75 Damage Absorb Ward.
Casts a 5-Pip version of Phalanx that applies +125 Damage Blades and unprotected 150 Damage Absorb Wards on all allies.
Casts a 3-Pip, 200 Balance Damage version of Stunning Swarm.

name: Nightwyng
cheats: "For the Bat" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Nightwyng will cast a version of Insane Bolt that deals 1,000 Moon Damage on the Wizard.

"I have trained under the Bat, a child of Grandfather Spider, I know more of Shadow Magic than you can imagine, can cast spells you've never seen, behold my power!!! Aw, nuts!" - When Nightwyng gains a Shadow Pip, he will attempt to cast Infinite Shadow, but it will always fizzle and uses a Spell that removes his Shadow Pip.
"This time I'll get it! Bam! Pow! Zowie!" "Aw, nuts!"- Nightwyng will state this every other attempt to cast Infinite Shadow after his first.
"Holy ineffectual wizard buffs!" - If a Wizard casts a positive or negative Charm, then Nightwyng will cast Steal Charm on the Wizard who cast the Spell. (Sometimes this cheat doesn't activate)
This cheat will only be triggered by the first Wizard to cast a Charm each Round.
Damage Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
AoE Charms will only remove from the caster.
"You can't trap a disciple of the Bat!" - If a Damage Debuff such as Weakness is placed on Nightwyng he will cast Cleanse Charm to remove it from himself. (Sometimes this cheat doesn't activate)
Using a Damage Debuff can sometimes trigger the Steal Charm cheat, as well as only trigger Steal Charm.

name: Nordri
cheats: At the start of Round 1, Nordri will use his turn to cast a -65% Tower Shield on himself.
At the start of Round 2, Nodri he will cast a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself.
This Shield will be replaced every time Nordri is hit.
Spells such as Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward can be used to remove the Tower Shield, but will still trigger the "Tag" cheat.
"Tag! You are It!" - At the start of the fight, and every three Rounds thereafter, one of the Bosses will cast a Stun Shield labeled "Tag! I'm It!" on a random brother, "tagging" them.
On repeats of this cheat, the previously "tagged" Boss will have all Wards on him removed, including the "Tag! I'm It!" Stun Shield.
"Wrong brother! I'm it!" - Casting Spells at any Boss who is not "tagged" will trigger an interrupt-attack from the "tagged" boss. The attack varies depending on which boss is "tagged".
May Cast Spells will trigger this cheat.
Multi-target, all-enemies attack Spells will not trigger this cheat.
This cheat will stop when Nordri is defeated.
"Now where did my brothers go?!" - When all of Nordri's brothers are defeated, he will remove all Wards from himself at the beginning of the following Round.
"You hurt my brothers!" - Four rounds after all of his brothers have been defeated, and every three Rounds thereafter, Nordri will cheat-cast a version of Meteor Strike that deals 500 Fire Damage.

locations: Wizard City: Sunken City - Norton's Tower

name: Nosferabbit

name: Noxious Gas Golem
cheats: "Alert: Noxious Gas Release Imminent" - At the beginning of the second Round, two Gas Elementals will join the duel, and will become active during the third Round.

"Initiate: Support Maintenance" - At the beginning of the Round after one of the Gas Elementals reaches 50% Health or lower, the Noxious Gas Golem will interrupt-cast Take Power on each of the Gas Elementals individually. Two Rounds after the Elementals are sacrificed, additional Elementals will join the duel, and become active on the third Round after this cheat occurs. This cheat will repeat endlessly until the Noxious Gas Golem is defeated.
If the Chief Whip is defeated before the Noxious Gas Golem, then a third Gas Elemental can join the duel when the Elementals are recycled.
If Wizards flee and then return to this area so that Gas Elementals are present from the beginning of the Duel, then Gas Elementals will not be recycled once defeated.
"Initiate: Defensive Screen" - At the beginning of every fourth Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will interrupt-cast Smoke Screen (-45%).
Chief Whip
"Chip up!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage Spell, there is a chance that the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Spirit Armor on the target of the attack.
If a multi-target Damage Spell triggers this cheat, then the Spirit Armor will be cast on whoever is in the first spot.
"Hold the line!" - Chief Whip can interrupt-cast Rebirth and revive fallen companions.
"I'll catch you up!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, then the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Entangle on the Wizard.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.

name: Noxious Gas Golem (Challenge)
cheats: Noxious Gas Golem

[Fire][Jinx] - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (110 Fire Damage when including the Golem's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.

[Fire][Ward] - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (110 Fire Damage when including the Golem's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.
[Round][Minion] "Alert: Noxious Gas Release Imminent!" - At the beginning of the first Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will summon two Gas Elementals, which become active during the second Round.
[Round][Trap] "Initiate: Defensive Screen" - At the end of the second Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast Mass Fire Trap.
[Round][Damage] "Initiate: Offense!" - At the end of the third Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast Meteor Strike.
Chief Whip
[Life][Jinx] - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, the Chief Whip will cast a Life Spell that restores 75 Health over 3 Rounds to himself.
[Life][Damage over Time] - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect, the Chief Whip will cast a Life Spell that restores 75 Health over 3 Rounds to himself.
[Minion][Afterlife] "Hold the line!" - The first time that an enemy is defeated, the Chief Whip will cast a Spell that restores 15% max Health to all allies, reviving the defeated enemy.

name: Numbskull Chieftain
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, the Numbskull Chieftain will interrupt to place a +30% Spirit Trap on the Wizard.

"Don't do that!" / "I'm warning you." / "I warned you!" - If a Wizard casts a Charm Spell (e.g. Blades), he will shout a warning. After the third Charm is cast, he will cheat-cast Chivalrous Centaur on the Wizard who cast the Spell, then proceed to destroy three Charms on the Wizard.
Pet Blades will trigger this cheat.
"Do your worst!" - If a Trap is placed on the Numbskull Chieftain, he will cheat-cast Legend Shield and Dream Shield on himself.
"Heals? I can play that game too!" - If a Healing Spell is used, his Pet will cheat-cast Guiding Light on him.
Pet Heals will trigger this cheat.
"I hunger for your pain!" - He will cast Hungry Caterpillar.

name: Nyx Night Bringer (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Forbidden Library Gauntlet - Forbidden Library Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: [Global] "Nightfall is inevitable!"' - At the start of combat, Nyx Night Bringer casts a version of Doom and Gloom (Moon).
[Harmful Aura] "The cloak of night obscures all sight!" - At the end of Round 1, Nyx Night Bringer will cast Darkness: -50% Accuracy Acrimony Auras on all Wizards for 99 Rounds.
[Damage] "The worst monsters prowl at night!" - Casts a versions of Dark Nova, Dark Fiend & Dark Shepherd that immediately attack, or a Natural Attack that also steals pips.

[Caster][Spell] "If only your friends could find you in the dark!" - If a player casts any spell on themselves, Nyx will interrupt and say this as a hint to cast spells on allies. This has no effect other than the Interrupt.
[Feint] "The dark of night shields me!" - If a wizard casts Feint on Nyx, she will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward that removes all traps from her.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will trigger this cheat.
Pet spells will trigger this cheat.

cheats: There is a cheat that removes all positive charms from Wizard's team. It was observed twice, both times after a player used Dark Pact.
[Global] "Nightfall is inevitable!"' - At the start of combat, Nyx Night Bringer casts a version of Doom and Gloom (Moon).

(Likely triggered from casting a Blade on an ally)
[Torch]
"No! The light!" - When a Wizard casts Torch, Nyx Night Bringer will react with this line.

locations: MooShu: Village of Sorrow - Oakheart's Lair

name: Oblongata
cheats: "Ice freezes Storm." - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Oblongata will cast Wall of Blades or Oni's Destruction.
"Ice survives Death." - If a Wizard casts a Weakness or Dispel, Oblongata will cast Glacial Fortress or Jinn's Vexation.

"Don't... mind if I do!" - Oblongata will cast either a helpful Charm, or helpful Ward, or Heal over Time effect on himself, or a harmful Charm, or harmful Ward, or Damage over Time effect on a random Wizard. These effects do not interact with any Damage Spells, and the Heal and Damage over Time effects restore no Health and deal no Damage, and last forever, they are labelled "Brain Power".

Removing one of these effects with one of the following Spells will cause Oblongata to apply a +200% Damage Ward on himself. This 200% Trap is unaffected by Stack Overflow, so it can be a 5th Trap, however if you apply a 6th Trap (including from Jinn's Reversal), it will be removed by Stack Overflow.
Jinn's Affliction
Jinn's Defense
Jinn's Larceny
Jinn's Restoration
Jinn's Reversal
Jinn's Vexation

name: Old Quack (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade] "That's not the real you!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Helpful Charm, or a multi-blade Spell, such as Elemental Blade, Old Quack will cast an Ice version of Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
If a multi-target Blade Spell is cast, such as Bladestorm, Old Quack will cast an all enemies version of Enfeeble.
A wizard casting a blade-type spell that is not of their original school will also trigger this cheat.
Spells with secondary effects, such as Savage Paw, will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis will not prevent the removal of Blades.

name: Old Quack (Standard)
cheats: "That's not the real you!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Helpful Charm, or a multi-blade Spell, such as Elemental Blade, Old Quack will cast an Ice version of Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.

name: Omen Stribog
locations: Polaris: River of Frozen Tears - Omen's Hideout
cheats: Omen Stribog
"Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has already started, then Omen will cast an enchanted version of Mana Burn dealing 1,500 base Damage.
Unbalance can be used to bypass this cheat.
"We are not easily fooled" - If a Wizard casts Feint on any enemy, then Omen will then remove the Feint from himself or his allies, unless it's protected.
Mass Feint will remove the trap placed on the user of the spell.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this.
"You are darkened by the Shadow as well?" - If a Wizard casts a Shadow Creature Spell, then Omen will cast a Moon Spell that adds +40 Backlash to the Wizard.
Because of updates to Shadow mechanisms this cheat no longer increases Backlash beyond 30.
If Omen is the last Boss remaining, he will cast these variations of Amplify. (Needs clarification on how often Aura changes)
+10% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+20% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+30% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+40% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+50% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
This Aura has the appearance of Berserk.
If a Wizard removes the Aura, he will recast it.
Dark Sentinel
At the beginning of the first Round, Dark Sentinel will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Shadow Sentinel, which intercepts 75% Damage, lasts 99 Rounds, and has no Backlash.
If Dark Sentinel is revived from a Heal All Spell, then it will not cheat-cast Shadow Sentinel.
"Your parlor tricks are to no avail!" - If a Wizard casts any type of Charm or Global Spell, Dark Sentinel will cheat-cast Enfeeble (Shadow) on both the caster and receiver.
If a Wizard casts a Blade on themself, then the Dark Sentinel will cast Enfeeble on them twice.
Negative charms such as Entangle will result in all blades being removed from the targeted opponent as well as the caster.
If a multi-target Spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), then the Dark Sentinel will only remove the Blade from the Wizard who cast the Spell.
If a Wizard casts any type of Ward Spell, there is a chance that Dark Sentinel will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Dark Seraph
Whenever the Dark Seraph uses a Healing Spell, an Absorb will be placed on all enemies, similar to the Shadow Seraph effect.
spellNotes: Shadow Decrease Backlash gives -10 Backlash to any Shadow Spells on himself.
Deals 100 Shadow Damage per Pip despite the Spell's 3-Pip maximum description.
The Natural Attack is a Steal that heals 100% of the Damage dealt.

name: Orrick Nightglider
locations: Wizard City: Colossus Boulevard - Briskbreeze Tower
cheats: If a Trap is placed on Orrick, he will use Cleanse Ward on himself to remove one Trap on himself.
Prisms and mass Traps, such as Windstorm, do not trigger this cheat.
This cheat can be Dispelled or prevented with Indemnity.

Every 3 Rounds, while Orrick's health is above 4,000, he will interrupt-cast a Heck Hound on the Wizard dealing 1,820 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds. This is the greatest threat in this battle. This cheat can be Dispelled.
This Heck Hound will still be cast on a Wizard even if Orrick is Beguiled.
Starting on Round 4, Orrick will interrupt with Meteor Strike dealing 750 Fire Damage.
This cheat can be Dispelled.
Beguile can delay this cheat by one round when he has no allies.
Under 4,000 Health:
When Orrick's health drops below 4,000 during a round, then three Stompers join the duel at the end of the round and become active 2 rounds later.
If Wizards flee and return, the Stompers will still remain.
When Orrick's health drops below 4,000, he will use Meteor Strike (750) at the start of every 3 Rounds once the current four round cycle completes.
When Orrick's health is between 4,000 and 2,000, he will interrupt to use a -65% Tower Shield on himself whenever he is damaged.
Drain Spells do not trigger this cheat.
If Orrick is hit with a multi-hit spell, he will use Tower Shield (-65%) on himself for every hit.
This also includes Damage over Times, which will be cast after his turn ends.
Under 2,000 Health:

At the start of the round Orrick's health drops below 2,000, Orrick will interrupt with an Earthquake that only deals 750 Myth Damage. Spell doesn't remove charms or wards. This cheat can be Dispelled.
In addition every 3 Rounds, while Orrick's health is below 2,000, he will interrupt-cast a Heck Hound on the Wizard dealing 1,820 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds.

name: Osseus Nightreaver
cheats: "A Charm! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Blade will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat, but Aura Spells will not.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A ward! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Trap (including Feint) or a Shield will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
The text for this cheat only appears after Osseus casts the Hex.

name: Overlord Xerxes
cheats: "Another intruder! Interesting" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, then Overlord Xerxes will cast a version of Steal Health on the Wizard, which deals 1,596 Damage and Pierces through all Resist, healing him for 100% of the Damage.

"All right, no more playing around" - At the beginning of Round 1, he will cast an enhanced version of Steal Health on all of his Minions, dealing 9,378 Damage to each them and healing Xerxes for 100% of the Damage.
If a Minion has the first position, then the Minion will not be sacrificed.
If no Minions are present, Xerxes will cast a version of Blessing on himself that restores 10,000 Health.
"Your modern tactics are weak" - If a Feint is placed on Xerxes or a Minion, he will cast Sacrifice (Death) on himself.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
"Let's see how you handle this" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, he will cast a Steal Charm (Star) that steals all Blades from all Wizards.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
Pet casts will trigger this cheat.
Aegis can be used to protect your Blades once. Casting a second Charm will cause Xerxes to remove the first Charm.
spellNotes: Casts an all-enemies version of Curse (95%).
Casts a +70% / +30% version of a Spell similar to Mass Feint.

name: Paranoia
cheats: "HUH?":
At the beginning of the first Round, Paranoia will cast Astraphobia and will recast this spell when replaced.
At the beginning of the fourth Round, and every two Rounds thereafter, Paranoia will cast a Dispel determined by the School of the Wizard's highest number of School Pips.
If a Wizard casts a single Blade, Paranoia will cast a -80% Shield of the Blade's School, or a -70% Tower Shield for Balanceblades.
If a Wizard casts Elemental Blade or Spirit Blade, Paranoia will cast Elemental Shield or Spirit Shield, respectively.
If a Wizard casts Star Blade, Moon Blade, or Sun Blade, Paranoia will cast the corresponding -50% triple Shields.
Alternative Blades, such as Raindrop Blade, Catch of the Day (Tier 2b), etc. don't trigger this cheat.
