name: Pendragon
cheats: [Dispel] At the start of the duel, the Pendragon will interrupt-cast an Entangle Treasure Card on all Wizards.
[Damage over Time] During Round 1, he will use his turn to cast Fire Dragon (650+20,000) which deals 100 Damage Over Time per Round lasting 200 Rounds. This can 'Critical' for double Damage.
Triage and Shift can be used to safely remove the DoT.
Shift and Detonate can be used as a counter attack to this cheat.
Quench can be used to dispel the Fire Dragon if Wizard has the initial cast.
On the fifth Round, Pendragon will use his turn to recast this Spell if initially dispelled.
[Ice] If a Wizard attacks the Pendragon, he will cast an Ice Wand attack that doesn't deal Ice Damage dealing 500 Damage on himself.

If a Wizard attacks the Pendragon with the Sword of Kings Wand hits, he will re-cast the Wand hit as a Natural Attack on himself, and sometimes take the 500 Damage from the Ice Wand attack that doesn't deal Ice Damage.
The Sword of Kings Wand attacks will always trigger this cheat.
[Steal] If a Wizard casts a Rank 6 or higher single-target Spell (including X-Pip), he will interrupt-cast an Ominous Scarecrow dealing 550 Damage.

name: Pendragon (Challenge)
cheats: [Death] Pendragon - Pendragon punishes Wizards who Damage him with Spells that cost more than 3 Pips.

"Your power only feeds me" - If Pendragon takes Damage from a Spell that costs more than 3 Pips, it will cast a version of Scarecrow that deals 1,000 Death Drain Damage and restores half the Damage dealt.
[Myth] Black Dragon - Black Dragon punishes Single Target spells as followed.

"You are myopic, too focused on one and not the whole"' - If a Wizard casts one of the following single target effects, Black Dragon will cast one of the following Shadow removal Spells based upon the amounts of effects the spell placed. These will ignore protection.
If a Damage Spell places an effect, the target and caster will be hit by whichever effect the spell had. (Example:Volcanic Golem will trigger single Pierce on target, and a double Pierce on caster)
Damage Spells that apply a Healing effect to the target (Example:Link), won't have the HOT removed but will trigger the single-target damage cheat on the Wizard.
[Blade] - Disarm removes 1, 2, or 3 Blades depending on how many Blades were cast.
Globals will trigger a single Disarm removal.
[Ward] - Pierce removes 1, 2, or 3 Wards depending on how many Wards were cast.
[Heal over Time] - Remove HOT removes 1, 2, or 3 HOTs depending on how many Heal over Time effects were cast.
[Healing] - If a Wizard casts a Heal, Black Dragon will cast, Celestial Calendar on the Wizard.
Heal over Times included.
[Damage over Time] - If a single target DOTs is placed on on Black Dragon, Triage will remove 1, 2, or 3 DOTs depending on how many Damage over Time Effects were cast.
This also triggers single target Damage the cheat below.
[Damage] - If a Wizard casts a single target Damage spell on Black Dragon, a Moon version of Blessing will restore 100% max Heath on itself.
[Trap] - If Traps are placed on Black Dragon, Cleanse Ward will remove 1, 2, or 3 negative Traps depending on how many Traps were cast.
Prisms are uneffected.
[Curse] - If negative Charms are placed on Black Dragon, Cleanse Charm will remove 1, 2, or 3 negative Charms depending on how many Charms were cast.
[Ice] White Dragon - White Dragon punishes Multi-Targeting spells as followed.

"You are weak and unfocused"' - If a Wizard casts one of the following multi-targeting effects, White Dragon will cast one of the following Shadow removal Spells based upon the amounts of effects the spell placed. These will ignore protection.
[Blade] - Casting a multi-target Blade, an AoE Disarm will remove 1 or 2 Blades depending on how many Blades were cast on all Wizards.
[Ward] - Casting a multi-target Shield, an AoE Pierce will remove 1 or 2 Wards depending on how many Wards were cast on all Wizards.
[Heal over Time] - Casting a multi-target HOT, an AoE Remove HOT will remove 1 or 2 HOTs depending on how many Heal over Time effects were cast on all Wizards.
[Healing] - If a Wizard casts a multi-target Heal, White Dragon will cast, Frost Giant (530).
[Damage over Time] - If a multi-target DOTs is placed on on White Dragon, Triage will remove 1, 2, or 3 DOTs depending on how many Damage over Time Effects were cast on White Dragon.
This also triggers multi-target Damage the cheat below.
[Damage] - If a Wizard casts a multi-target Damage spell on White Dragon, a Moon version of Blessing will restore 100% max Heath on itself.
[Trap] - If a multi-target Trap is placed on White Dragon, Cleanse Ward will remove 1, 2, or 3 negative Traps depending on how many Traps were cast on White Dragon.
Prisms are unaffected.
[Curse] - If a Wizard casts a multi-target negative Charm on White Dragon, Cleanse Charm will remove 1, 2, or 3 negative Charms depending on how many Charms were cast on White Dragon.

name: Pendragon (Standard)
cheats: [Death] Pendragon - Pendragon punishes Wizards who Damage him with Spells that cost more than 3 Pips.

"Your power only feeds me" - If Pendragon takes Damage from a Spell that costs more than 3 Pips, it will cast a version of Scarecrow that deals 1,000 Death Drain Damage and restores half the Damage dealt.
If defeated with a spell that costs more than 3 pips, he will heal himself and then use the previously mentioned Scarecrow.
"Even stragglers will be devoured!" - If a wizard joins late, Pendragon will attack the late player with Medusa (unknown version).

If a Damage Spell places an effect, the target and caster will be hit by whichever effect the spell had. (Example:Volcanic Golem will trigger single Pierce on target, and a double Pierce on caster)

[Curse] - If a Wizard casts a multi-target negative Charm on White Dragon, Cleanse Charm will remove 1, 2, or 3 negative Charms depending on how many Charms were cast on White Dragon.

name: Personal Daemon
cheats: "Ice freezes Storm." - If a Wizard casts a Helpful Charm, Personal Daemon will cast Wall of Blades or Oni's Destruction on all Wizards that clears all positive Charms for 3 -20% damage shields or 3 +15% ice damage traps.

"Ice outlasts Death." - If a Wizard casts a Harmful Charm, Personal Daemon will cast Glacial Fortress on itself or Jinn's Vexation on the casting wizard that clears all negative Charms for 3 -20% damage shields or 3 -10% damage weaknesses.
"Stack overflow" - If one of the following effects goes over a certain threshold, the extra effects will be removed.
Over 4 Helpful Wards on Wizards.
Over 4 Heal over Times on Wizards.
Over 4 Damage over Times on Daemon.
Over 4 Harmful Wards on Personal Daemon.
traps on Personal Daemon placed as part of his interrupt cycle are ignored for this count
Interrupt cycle Personal Daemon revolves around a interrupt cycle repeating every 5 rounds
At the beginning of the first round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to remove all harmful wards from itself.
At the beginning of the second round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to place a cloaked trap on itself.
The cloaked trap is a +0% trap of the school corresponding to the school that primarily owes the hanging effect that the hit for the cycle counters
Fire: Damage over Times
Ice: Helpful Wards
Life: Heal over Times
Myth: Harmful Wards
Balance: Pips
The Bat Bot minions may change their self-buffing behavior if this trap is removed on the same turn it is placed
At the beginning of the third round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to place a cloaked trap on itself.
The cloaked trap is a +0% trap of the school corresponding to the school of the hit of the cycle

Removing this trap on the same turn it is placed will cause Bat Bot minions with shielding capabilities to interrupt cast a -125% damage shield of the school of the hit on the next turn, overriding their default behavior
At the beginning of the fourth round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to place a cloaked trap on itself.
The cloaked trap is a +0% trap of the school corresponding to the school that primarily owes the hanging effect that the hit for the cycle generates
All the school/effect pairings noted on turn one are used plus the following new options
Storm: Helpful Charms
Death: Harmful Charms
Shadow: Shadow Pips

"Execute background process" - At the end of the fifth round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to cast one of the following spells based on the self applied traps. All of these spells have 45 additional pierce.

A version of Earth Walker that deals 6,666 Life Damage to all enemies, and removes up to three Damage over Time effects from itself to apply a Damage over Time effect on all enemies dealing 130 Life Damage over 3 Rounds.
A version of Centaur that deals 6,666 Life Damage to all enemies, and removes up to five harmful wards from itself to apply a +25% damage charm on itself per trap removed.
A version of Akori Nirini that deals 6,666 Fire Damage on all enemies, and removes up to five harmful wards from itself to apply a +30% damage trap on all enemies per harmful ward removed.

A version of King Artorius (Fire) that deals 6,666 Fire Damage on all enemies and removes up to helpful wards from all enemies per shield removed to apply a Heal over Time that restores 700 Health over 5 Rounds.

A version of King Artorius (Storm) that deals 6,666 Storm Damage to all enemies, and removes up to three Heal over Time effects from enemies to apply a Heal over Time effect on itself restoring 300 Health over 4 Rounds.
A version of Leviathan that deals 6,666 Storm Damage to all enemies, and removes up to three Damage over Time effects from itself to apply a -25% damage weakness on all enemies.
A version of Basilisk that deals 6,666 Myth Damage to all enemies, and removes up to eight helpful wards from enemies to apply a -50% Damage shield on itself per shield removed.

A version of Skeletal Pirate that deals 6,666 Death Damage to all enemies, and removes up three Heal over Time effects from enemies to apply a -50% damage shield on itself per Heal over Time effect removed.
A version of Hypothermi-HIYA! that deals 6,666 Ice Damage to all enemies, and removes 3 pips from all enemies and gives itself 3 pips.
A version of Clockwork Spider (355) that deals 6,666 Balance Damage to all enemies, and removes 3 pips from all enemies and gives itself a shadow pip.

At any point, if one of Personal Daemon's self placed traps are removed without hitting, by gambit, swap or clear, Personal Daemon will interrupt to skip the rest of the cycle and start the "Execute background process" phase instantly.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Freeze Ray that deals 920 Ice Damage for 5 Pips and 2 Shadow Pips.

name: Pharaoh Rama-Krok (Balance-Death, Challenge)
cheats: [Round] "I will rise again and again!!!" - At the beginning of the first Round, and every third Round thereafter, Pharaoh Rama-Krok will cast a random hanging effect (Labled as "Krok Ankh") on himself or a random Wizard: Shield, Trap, Blade, Heal over time, Damage over time, Weakness.

"Krok Ankh" hanging effects must be cleared using spells or treasure cards that specifically clear the effect. (i.e. the Weakness Krok Ankh cannot be removed by self-damage inflicting spells, and the Shield Krok Ankhs cannot be removed by damage spells.)
[Afterlife] "I did warn you..." - If Pharaoh Rama-Krok is defeated while a Krok Ankh hanging effect is still active, then he will restore 100% of health on himself.
Due to the heal being a Max Percentage Heal, this heal cannot be decreased by Doom and Gloom and Infection.

name: Poison Oak
cheats: "Plants grow slow... but not this slow" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already started, Poison Oak will cast a Sun version of Lamassu dealing 984 Sun Damage, and places an Overtime Tick for 20,000 Life Damage over 100 Rounds.

"Nature! Protect me! Harm those who are against me!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Poison Oak will cast Wings of Fate that places an Overtime Tick for 20,000 Life Damage to all enemies and 20,000 Healing for all allies over 100 Rounds.
Both the Damage over Time and Healing over Time effects will persist throughout the entire duel.
Triage or Mass Triage can be used to remove the Damage over Time effect.
Shift and Detonate can be used to counter this cheat.
"Can't distract me!" - If a Feint is placed on Poison Oak, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this switch.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
"You lose! Good day sir!" - Poison Oak will cast Incindiate which detonates the long lasting Damage over Time dealing massive Damage.
spellNotes: Deals 105 Life Damage per Pip, 3 Pip maximum.
Applies a +35% incoming Life Damage Ward to the target.

name: Ponce de Gibbon
cheats: Interrupt Cycle This battle revolves around a cheat cycle that spans the length of five Rounds. This cheat repeats itself every five Rounds.
At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle (starting in Round 6), Ponce will remove marks he placed during the second Round in this cycle (see below). This is considered a normal Round.
"First, Let's cloud the mind!" / "Mind Weakened" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Ponce will place a "mark" on a random Wizard, in the form of a Stun Shield.
Melt can be placed on Ponce in order to Dispel this cheat.
The Stun Shield marker does not block Stuns.

The third Round in this cycle is considered a normal Round. However, it is the last Round for a marked Wizard to damage Ponce, in order to get rid of the mark. Failure to do so results in the worst part of his cheat to take place in the fourth and fifth Rounds of this cycle.
"Bah! You are too strong willed!" - If the marked Wizard attacks him, he will remove the Stun Shield mark and any other Wards from the Wizard. Stun Shields aren't removed from this spell.
"Are you sure you won't join us?" - In Round 4, if a marked Wizard did not attack Ponce by the end of the third Round in this cycle, he will Beguile the Wizard.

"If you aren't with us, you're against us!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Sacrifice on the marked / Beguiled Wizard, stealing Health from the Wizard and Healing himself for 2,090 Health (as though the Wizard used Sacrifice on him).
Universal and Death Blades that are active on the Wizard, will increase the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
Universal and Death Shields that are active on the Wizard will decrease the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
Infection will not lessen this Damage, however, it will decrease the Healing effect by -50%. Needs confirmation.

name: Ponce de Gibbon (Rank 22)
cheats: Interrupt Cycle This battle revolves around a cheat cycle that spans the length of five Rounds. This cheat repeats itself every five Rounds.
At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle (starting in Round 6), Ponce will remove marks he placed during the second Round in this cycle (see below). This is considered a normal Round.
"First, Let's cloud the mind!" / "Mind Weakened" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Ponce will place a "mark" on a random Wizard, in the form of a Stun Shield.
The Stun Shield marker does not block Stuns.
Earthquake can remove this marker and will prevent the rest of this cycle.

The third Round in this cycle is considered a normal Round. However, it is the last Round for a marked Wizard to damage Ponce, in order to get rid of the mark. Failure to do so results in the worst part of his cheat to take place in the fourth and fifth Rounds of this cycle.
"Bah! You are as strong-willed as I remember" - If the marked Wizard attacks him, he will remove the Stun Shield mark and any other Wards from the Wizard. Stun Shields aren't removed from this Spell.
"I can't control you, but I can confuse you!" - In Round 4, if a marked Wizard did not attack Ponce by the end of the third Round in this cycle, he will Confuse the Wizard for 2 Rounds.

"If you aren't with us, you're against us!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Sacrifice on the marked / Confused Wizard, stealing Health from the Wizard and Healing himself for 2,090 Health (as though the Wizard used Sacrifice on him).
Universal and Death Blades that are active on him will increase the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
Universal and Death Shields that are active on the Wizard will decrease the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.

Spells with single-target secondary effects will trigger this cheat.

name: Power Draconian
cheats: Power Draconian
"Oh look, a wizard with attitude!" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Power Draconian casts Impede on the Wizard.
"Power Equalize!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Power Draconian will cast a Sun Global Spell called Equalize, which sets the Damage limit to 3,000.
"Bubble Morph!" - If a Wizard tries to replace the Equalize Spell with another type of Global Spell, he will recast Equalize.
"Power Stunner!" - At the beginning of every third Round, Power Draconian casts Power Stunner, which Stuns for 1 Round and applies a -40% Accuracy Charm on all enemies.
"Power Recharge!" - If Power Draconian is damaged by an All Enemies Spell, Power Draconian casts a Shadow version of Blessing restoring 100% max Health on all allies.
All Enemies Spells that deal Drain Damage, like Scarecrow, do not trigger this cheat.
White Tiger
"*GROWL*" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, White Tiger casts Sabertooth on the Wizard.
"*WARNING ROAR*" - White Tiger will say this the turn before their *ROAR* cheat.

name: Prince Viggor
locations: Wizard City: The Catacombs - Viggor's Tomb
cheats: "Knights of Dragonspyre, to me!"' - Casts a Sun Spell to summon a combination of the following Minions:
Summons 1 Minion if only 1 Wizard is present.
Summons 2 Minions if 2 Wizards are present.
Viggor's Crusader (Death)
Viggor's Crusader (Ice)
Viggor's Vanguard (Fire)
Viggor's Vanguard (Life)
Viggor's Lorekeeper (Myth)
Viggor's Lorekeeper (Storm)
Viggor's Havox (Balance)
Viggor's Havox (Shadow)
"The cycle repeats..."' - Starting on Round 3, Prince Viggor will recast his Sun Summon Spell to summon one additional Minion.
"That knowledge is beyond you!" - If a Feint is cast on Boss, he will cast a Shadow Spell to remove all Traps.
Indemnity can be used to preserve the Feint if Viggor's Lorekeeper isn't present.
"Steel yourselves!" - At the end of Round 2, Prince Viggor will cast a -55% Mass Tower Shield (Shadow).
"This is for your own good" - Prince Viggor will cast a -30% Mass Weakness.
"Attack!" - Viggor will cast Bladestorm (+25%).
"Feel our fury!" - Viggor will cast +70% Feint (Shadow) universal Trap on all Wizards.

"You can't sneak up on me!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Prince Viggor will cast +25% Bladestorm, -30% Mass Weakness, a -55% Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) that applies 3 shields per ally, and an AoE +70% Feint (Shadow).
Life and Death Minions
If a Shield is cast, this Minion will use Pierce (Star) to remove 1 Shield then proceed to cast Tower Shield (-55%) on themselves.
AoE Shields only trigger this spell on the caster.
Fire and Ice Minions
If a Blade is cast, this Minion will use Cleanse Charm (Star) to remove all Negative Charms from the receiver of the Blade and then proceed to cast Balanceblade (+30%) on themselves.
For multi-target spells such as Bladestorm, only the caster will be cleansed.
Storm and Myth Minions
If a Trap is cast, this Minion will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) to remove 1 Trap from their ally and places a Feint (+75%) on the Wizard that cast the Trap.
Multi-target Traps such as Windstorm will cause the Minions to cast this spell on the caster.
Balance and Shadow Minions
If a Negative Charm is cast, this Minion will use Cleanse Charm (Star) on the target it was used on then proceed to cast a -30% Weakness on the Wizard.

cheats: "Your charm has worn thin!" - During the first Round, and after each direct attack against him, Ptolemos will cast a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself.

Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
Ptolemos will remove harmful Wards that are placed on him (e.g. Traps, Prisms, etc.).
Vaporize can be used to prevent this cheat.
Multi-target Trap Spells, such as Mass Feint, will not trigger this cheat.
Only the first Ward is removed from a multi-Trap Spell, such as Spirit Trap.
Indemnity will prevent Trap removal once.

name: Quincy Morris
cheats: Quincy Morris
[Polymorph] "*Rrrrroooooaaaarrrrr!*" - At the beginning of the first Round, Quincy Morris will polymorph into Werewolf form.
This cheat is re-triggered at the beginning of the fifth Round after unpolymorphing.
[Aura] "No buffs can pierce this hide!" - After polymorphing into werewolf form, Quincy Morris will cast an aura limiting incoming Damage to 1,000.
After Quincy Morris takes six turns, he will revert back to his human form at the end of the Round, and then cast Amplify.
[Trap] "Gah! Snagged! I will recover!" Casting a Trap on Quincy Morris will decrease the time it takes for him to unpolymorph by a single Round.
Spells that apply Traps on all enemies, such as Mass Feint or Windstorm, will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that apply multiple Traps on a target, such as Elemental Trap, counts as a single Trap, and only decreases the time of unpolymorphing by a single Round.

[Harmful Aura] "The wilds make us stronger, we endure." Attempting to replace this Aura, such as with Starfall, will cause Quincy to heal himself to full health, recast his damage cap Aura, and then cheat cast Orthrus.
John Seward
[Polymorph] "*Hoooowwwwllllll!*" - At the beginning of the first Round, John Seward will polymorph into Were-Bear form.
"My power will return..." - At the end of the 6th Round, John Seward will unpolymorph.
This line will also play when an Aura Spell, such as Starfall, is used on him when in Were-Bear form. It will not unpolymorph him early, however.
After John Seward takes six turns, he will revert back to his human form at the end of the Round.
[Curse] Casting a harmful Charm on John Seward will decrease the time it takes for him to unpolymorph by a single Round.
Spells that apply harmful Charms on all enemies, such as Mass Infection, will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that apply multiple harmful Charms on a target, such as Threefold Fever, counts as a single harmful Charm, and only decreases the time of unpolymorphing by a single Round.
[Damage] "The wilds make us stronger, we endure." Using a Damage Spell on John Seward, while in Were-Bear form, will cause him to heal for 780 Health.

name: Ra
locations: Krokotopia: The Oasis - Ra's Reading Room
cheats: "Let us accelerate this contest" - At the beginning of the first Round, Ra will interrupt-cast Balance of Power.
"No no, we play by my rules" - If a Wizard attempts to replace the Global, Ra will recast Balance of Power.
"Accuracy is such a fickle thing" - Randomly, Ra will use his turn to cast Black Mantle or a +10% version of Precision, either of which can affect either all allies or all enemies.
If Ra is Spied when this cheat occurs, only the normal versions of Precision or Black Mantle will be revealed.

"Let's balance things out a bit!" - At the end of every fourth Round, Ra will interrupt-cast a Spell that applies an Aura on all participants in the duel. The first Aura that is cast will be Amplify, then Fortify, and then it will cycle between the two until the duel ends.

name: Ra (Rank 23)
locations: Krokotopia: Grand Exposition - Theater of War
cheats: "The Night serves to balance, to match, the Day." - Ra will say this at the beginning of the first Round.
[Round] "Can you complete the Day?" - At the end of the first Round, Ra will place three protected positive Charms on himself or three protected negative Wards on all Wizards.
These effects indicate the exact amount of helpful Charms or harmful Wards Wizards need before damaging Ra.
These effects are Cloaked and will not be consumed until removed by the cheat below.

[Damage] "Night must be equal to Day, which must match Night." - If a Wizard damages Ra without exactly matching his protected hanging effects, he will cheat-cast his Natural Attack on the offending Wizard, dealing 1500 Balance Damage, then remove all Charms and Wards.
[Afterlife] "You shouldn't skip the story." - If Ra is defeated while his hanging effects are not matched, he will cast Availing Hands, then remove all Charms and Wards.
[Damage] "And thus the Day is complete." - If a Wizard damages Ra while exactly matching his protected hanging effects, he will remove all Charms and Wards, then give the caster a +100% Frenzy Aura.
The hanging effects do not have to be consumed when dealing Damage, and any hanging effects consumed will count towards the total.
After receiving this cheat, all other cheats will stop.

name: Rattlebones (Rank 14)
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, Rattlebones will cast a Mana Burn at the Wizard, which deals 3,000 Damage.
Unbalance can be used to dispel this cheat.
"Rattlebones will be avenged!" - The Fairy Queen will cast this cheat if Rattlebones has been defeated.
"Let's dampen the Heals!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cheat-cast Doom and Gloom.
Strangle can be used to dispel this cheat.
Once dispelled, heals can be cast successfully (for an unconfirmed amount of time).
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Feint is placed on Rattlebones, he will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.

"Fairies! Protect your Queen!" - At the beginning of the first Round (and every 4 Rounds after a Minion's death), the Fairy Queen will summon 2 Fairies (Rank 14) of random Schools, which become active the following Round, and have 4,160 Health.
Entangle will not dispel this cheat, but instead will cause the Minions to become active two Rounds later.
"You'll never be as charming as Rattlebones!" - If a Charm or Global spell is cast, there is a random chance that she will cast Enfeeble on the Wizard who received the Charm or cast the Global spell.
Dissipate can be used to dispel this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), then she will only remove the Blade from the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Plague and Virulent Plague can trigger this cheat.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on the Fairy Queen, she will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.

"We fight as a team this time. Our victory is assured." - The Fairy Queen will cast Satyr (990) on Rattlebones, which heals an amount equivalent to the Backlash damage dealt by the previous Shadow Sentinel.
The following Fairies will cast these cheats.
Balance, Death, Ice, Life, Myth, Storm

Fire

"Ashes to ashes, we all burn down!" - Whenever the Fairy is under ~25% Health, she'll cast a version of Immolate that deals 2,100 Fire Damage to herself to deal 600 Fire Damage to the Wizard who last attacked her.
This spell isn't cast when the Wizard who attacked her is defeated.

name: Rising Dragon
locations: Dragonspyre: The Forum - Hall of Indolence

name: Roberto
locations: Wizard City: Olde Town - Roberto's Sanctum

name: Rogue Engineer
cheats: "Hands up! Hand's down!" - At the beginning of the first Round, two copies of Card Limit (14 Pip) will be added to the Wizard's hand, which cannot be discarded.
[Card Limit (14 Pip)]
"For the Minimalism!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round and every third Round after, a Card Limit (14 Pip) Spell will be added to the Wizard's hand, which cannot be discarded.
If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, four copies of Card Limit (14 Pip) will be added to the Wizard's hand.
"Raise the shields!" - At the end of every Round, even though it is not "cast", Rogue Engineer will use a version of Legion Shield on her team if she has no Shields.
This can be avoided with Melt.

name: Rotten Rob
locations: Wizard City: Triton Avenue - Rotten Rob's Hideout
cheats: During the first Round, Rotten Rob will use his turn to cast Scald.
Quench can be used to prevent this spell.

[Damage over Time] "Oh, he's doing that Fire school thing, I think Storms can counter it!" - At the end of every Round, Rotten Rob will cast a version of Efreet (Tier 2b) that deals 100 Fire Damage and deals 450 Fire Damage over 6 Rounds if target has a Damage over Time effect.
Quench can be used to prevent this cheat.
If Rotten Rob's initial Scald fizzles, or is prevented with Quench, he will continue to cast Efreet without any Damage over Time effect.
If the Wizard is second to cast, Triage can be used to remove the Damage over Time and prevent additional Damage over Time effects when Efreet is cast.
[Healing] If Rotten Rob is defeated while his Efreet cheat is still active, then he will revive with full Health.

[Spell] *"Hang on! Try this on him!" - At the beginning of the 4th Round, or after Rotten Rob has revived and then randomly at the end of subsequent Rounds, Daisy Willowmancer will give all Wizards the following Spell:
[Stormzilla (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
[Storm] "Wowzer, Storm douses Fire, huh?" - If a Wizard casts Stormzilla (Tier 2b), Rotten Rob will remove all Damage over Time effects on all Wizards and stop his Efreet cheat.

"How'd you do that?" - If a Wizard uses another Storm Spell that removes Damage over Time effects, such as Lightning Bats (Tier 2b), Rotten Rob will remove all Damage over Time effects from all Wizards and stop his Efreet cheat.

name: Runed Annihilator
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage.

name: Ruthless Gagool
locations: Zafaria: Stone Town - Kasbah of Zanj

name: Sahi Akhdir
spellNotes: Casts a version of Beastmoon Brownie that restores 100 Health over 4 Rounds to self.
Casts a 125 Health per Pip version of Dryad of Artemis.
Casts a 2-Pip, 200 Life Damage version of Clever Imp.
It is currently unknown whether the clear Ward effect occurs.
Casts a 4-Pip, 300 Life Damage version of Spirit Spider that restores 50 Health after target is defeated even if target is not a Minion.

name: Sand Squid Tentacle
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea - The Poseidon Adventure
cheats: [Minion] "My tendrils are endless!" - If at least one Sand Squid Tentacle Boss is still present, any Tentacle Minions defeated this round will respawn in two rounds.
If a Boss has been defeated when this cheat activates, a Minion will spawn in its place.
If one Boss tentacle and two Tentacle Minions are defeated, then this cheat no longer activates and no more Tentacles will respawn.
[Healing] Whenever a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, each enemy present will cheat-cast Aftershock.
Pet may-cast heals do not activate this cheat.

name: Sand Squid Tentacle (Boss, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea (Challenge) - The Poseidon Adventure (Challenge)
cheats: [Shield] "Here, take this too!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Shield Spell, a Boss will cast Myth Trap (+35%) on the caster.
All Allies Shield Spells such as Legion Shield will still only result in a Myth Trap on the caster.
[Curse] "Here, take this too!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Charm Spell, a Boss will cast Tower Shield on the recipient of the Charm.
All Enemies harmful Charm Spells such as Plague will cause the Boss to cast Legion Shield instead.

[Minion] "My tendrils are endless!" - At the beginning of the Round after a Sand Squid Tentacle Minion is defeated, any Bosses still present will say this as a warning. Any Minions defeated will be resummoned during the next Round.

name: Satharilith
cheats: "More? You too will perish!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Satharilith will cast Mana Burn (Shadow) on the Wizard.
At the start of the first Round, the Valencian Royal Guard closest to Satharilith will cast a version of Shadow Sentinel which intercepts 100% of Damage.
When the Minion who cast the Shadow Sentinel is defeated, the next Valencian Royal Guard will recast the Shadow Sentinel.
This cheat stops when all three Valencian Royal Guard Minions have been defeated.

"I twist your powers into nothing!" - When Feint is cast on the Boss or the Minions, Satharilith will cast an AoE Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps from the target. Mass Feint will also trigger this cheat.

"How simple, to turn your powers against you" - When one of the Valencian Royal Guards is defeated, the Boss will use its Natural Attack on the Wizard opposite the defeated Minion, and summon one of the following Minions with the same school as that Wizard. If there is not a Wizard in the opposite location, it will target the first target with a Natural Attack. Aspect Minions will not recast the 100% intercept Shadow Sentinel.
Balance Aspect
Death Aspect
Fire Aspect
Ice Aspect
Life Aspect
Myth Aspect
Storm Aspect

"Your mind is mine to toy with!" - If there are any missing Valencian Royal Guard minions at the start of the battle, Satharilith will summon a random Aspect Minion for each. The defeat of these Aspect Minions will trigger the above cheat.

Casts a 3-Pip version of Stunning Swarm that deals 200 Damage.

name: Shadow Self Magician
cheats: [Aura] "I am your mirror. Speak your words, feel your heart." - At the beginning of the First Round, Shadow Self Magician will cast an Invulnerability Aura with no round limit.
This Aura can be replaced with Negative Auras such as Attenuate without penalty, but the Invulnerability effect will not be removed.

[Damage over Time][Heal over Time] "You must acknowledge painful truths and comfortable lies." - At the beginning of the Second Round, Shadow Self Magician will apply as many Damage over Time effects dealing 1035 damage over 10 Rounds to itself as there are Wizards in the duel, and will apply a Heal over Time effect healing 555 health over 10 Rounds to each Wizard.

Starting at the end of the Fourth Round and every other Round thereafter, Shadow Self Magician will add one Damage over Time effect to itself or one Heal over Time effect to a random Wizard and restore its Invulnerability Aura.
[Charm][Ward] "Yes. See the obvious. Embrace the hurt. Ignore the lies." - Triggers when a Wizard casts a Charm or Ward.
If a Wizard casts a Positive Charm or Ward, Shadow Self Magician will shift one Healing Over Time effect from the Wizard who cast the spell onto itself.
Positive Charms or Wards that apply multiple hanging effects such as Elemental Blade and Elemental Shield will cause multiple Heal over Time effects to be shifted.
If a Wizard casts a Negative Charm or Ward, Shadow Self Magician will shift one Damage over Time effect from itself to the Wizard that cast the spell.
Negative Charms or Wards that apply multiple hanging effects such as Elemental Trap and Fuel will cause multiple Damage over Time effects to be shifted.
Feint, which places a Negative Ward on both the caster and Shadow Self Magician, will cause Shadow Self Magician to shift one Damage over Time effect, and one Heal over Time effect.

[Manipulation] "Are you seeing the truth beyond the comfortable lie?" - When all Healing over Time effects are applied to Shadow Self Magician and all Damage over Time effects are applied to the Wizard(s), Shadow Self Magician's Invulnerability Aura will be removed.
If a Negative Aura has been used to replace Shadow Self Magician's Invulnerability Aura, it will be removed.

[Afterlife] "You deny pain, but its shadow lingers." - If Shadow Self Magician is defeated by a Wizard who does not have a Damage over Time effect shifted to them, it will revive after being defeated.

name: Shadow Trickster
locations: Arcanum: The Arcanum - Shadow Training Room
cheats: [Damage] After using his Natural Attack, the Shadow Trickster will interrupt-cast Steal Pips.
spellNotes: Always uses his first turn to cast Dark Pact (+30%).

name: Shadow Trickster (Rank 22)
cheats: [Charm][Curse][Ward][Jinx] "Act, wizard, I always act!" - At the end of the Round after a Wizard casts a Charm or Ward, Shadow Trickster will remove one Charm or Ward and heal itself for 10% of its maximum Health.
The type of effect removed corresponds to the most recently cast effect.
This cheat can trigger multiple times in one Round.
Auras will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis or Indemnity does not prevent the removal.
Casting an attack before the corresponding effect is removed will prevent this cheat.

name: Shadow of the Land
cheats: [Damage] At the beginning of every fifth round, Shadow of the Land will interrupt with Power Nova dealing damage based on how many Laughing Calaca minions are in the duel.
"My Shadow brings ruin to all!" - No Minions: Deals 400 Balance damage.
"This Undead doubles your doom!" - 1 Minion: Deals 800 Balance damage.
"Every soul feeds the power of my Shadow!" - 2 Minions: Deals 1,600 Balance damage.
"Undead darkness fuels my Shadow!" - 3 Minions: Deals 3,200 Balance damage.
Unbalance on the boss can dispel this cheat.
[Minion] "Rise from the Dead!" - He will randomly summon another minion after any of them are defeated.
spellNotes: The Shadow of the Land uses a version of Shadow Sentinel with +15 Backlash.

name: Shadowbones
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the Second Round, Shadowbones will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
[Manipulation] "Shadow-shadow! I love shadow!" - He will randomly cast Dark Convert once in the fight, which converts three Pips into one Shadow Pip for himself.
[Feint] "Why don't we trade!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Shadowbones, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to exchange it with the Wizard.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
[Heal] "Let's dampen the heals!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%).
Pet Heals will trigger this cheat.
[Aura] "Oh, how cute!" - If any type of Fairy Spell is used (by friend or foe), he will cast Magnify on himself.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Tower Shield (-55%), Wraithblade, and Sharp Wraithblade.
Deals 82.5 Shadow Damage per Pip to all enemies, rounded down

name: Shadowwock
cheats: "It's after brillig!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Shadowwock will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard, dealing 3,000 Balance damage.
"Snicker-Snack" - Beginning on Round 2, he will interrupt to remove all Traps from himself at the start of every second Round (Round 4, Round 6, etc.).
Indemnity can be used to protect traps from this cheat.

"You make me frumious!" - At the beginning of every three Rounds, he will cast a Shadow version of Meteor Strike that randomly deals 1650 - 3,300 Shadow damage, and places a +200% universal Trap on himself.
He will not always cast the trap if a Wizard is defeated with the Meteor Strike.
Using Weakness-type spells can help reduce the amount of damage dealt by his Meteor Strike.
"How mimsy!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Shadowwock, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to swap the Traps with the Wizard.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) doest not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Whenever a Fizzle occurs, Shadowwock will cast a Shadow version of Empower, similar to Dark Surge to give himself 2 Power Pips.
After casting his Natural Attack, Shadowwock will cast a protected Black Mantle (Shadow) on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Deals 110 Shadow Damage per Pip, despite the Spell card's 3-Pip maximum description.

name: Shai Nirini
locations: Krokotopia: Palace of Fire - Shai's Chamber

name: Shaka Zebu
cheats: "Nature Spirit protect me!" - If Shaka Zebu is not at full Health, a Satyr will heal him by interrupt at the beginning of the Round.
Placing an Entangle on Shaka Zebu will dispel the Satyr for the next Round.
This cheat will stop if Shaka or all of his Minions are defeated.
"Where did everyone go?" - If all of his Minions are defeated, Shaka will say this line. The Meteor Strike cheat will follow a few rounds later.
"Feel Shaka Zebu's Might!" - Every 2-3 rounds, Shaka will interrupt-cast a 600-750 Damage version of Meteor Strike. This cast can be prevented with Quench.
This cheat will also begin if all of his Minions are Beguiled.
spellNotes: Deals 275 Balance Damage for 3 Pips maximum, despite the Spell card's description.

name: Shane von Shane
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Upper Halls
cheats: "I can see through you!" - If a Feint is placed on Shane von Shane, he will cast a Dark Pact (+30%).
Mass Feint does not trigger this cheat.
"I will not bow down to you!" - If a negative Charm, such as Weakness or Bad Juju, is placed on him, he will cast Sacrifice (750) on himself.
Multi-target negative Charms such as Plague do not trigger cheat.
"More life for me!" - If a Wizard uses a Healing Spell, he will cast an enhanced version of Vampire at the Wizard, which deals 1,050 Health Steal Damage and returns half to the caster.
Pet 'May Cast' Heals will trigger this cheat.
This cheat will not activate while beguiled.
Death Interrupt Cycle:

"We feast tonight!" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Shane von Shane will cast an enhanced version of Vampire at a random Wizard, which deals 1,050 Health Steal Damage and returns half to the caster.
"Hiss!" - This cycle can be reset by attacking Shane von Shane.
Steal spells, such as Ghoul or Lord of Night, will not reset this cycle.
Mana Burn and Supernova will not reset this cycle.
Damage over Time attacks, such as Fire Elf or Link, will reset this cycle.
"The monster begins to rise!" - At the beginning of the third Round in this cycle, he will cast Dr. Von's Monster (900) on a random Wizard.
This cycle can be reset by attacking Shane von Shane, following the same rules as above.
"It's a graveyard smash!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round in this cycle, he will cast Monster Mash (1,170), which Beguiles the Wizard.

name: Shane von Shane (Star)
cheats: [Charm] "I revel in my power!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Supercharge (+12%) on himself.
[Curse] "I will not bow down to you!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm on Shane, he will cheat-cast Sacrifice (Death) on himself.
Secondary Spell effects will trigger this cheat.
Harmful Charms that target all enemies will not trigger this cheat.
[Feint] "I can see through you" - If a Wizard casts a Feint on Shane, he will cheat-cast Dark Pact (+30%) on himself.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.

[Healing] "More life for me!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Vampire, dealing 1,050 Death Drain Damage on a random Wizard and heals the caster for half the Damage dealt.
Beguiled players will not trigger this cheat.
[Damage] "Bah, you are nothing!" - Whenever Shane is Damaged, he will cheat-cast a version of Healing Current on himself that restores 100, 400 or 1,000 Health.
Dissipate can be used to Dispel this cheat.
Damage over Time effects will trigger this cheat.
Supernova will not trigger this cheat.
Interrupt Cycles - This fight revolves around 2 independent cheat cycles: a "Storm Interrupt Cycle" and a "Death Interrupt Cycle."
"Prepare for the thunder!" - At the end of the first Round in this cycle, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Stormblade on himself.
"And the lightning!" - At the end of the second Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Stormspear on himself.
"The storm grows!" - At the end of the third Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Galvanic Field.
"And shatters you!" - At the end of the fourth Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast his Natural Attack on a random Wizard.
[Damage] "Hiss!" - Whenever Shane von Shane is Damaged by a Wizard, the cycle resets to Round one on the following turn. Otherwise, nothing happens during the first Round of this cheat cycle.

"We feast tonight!" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Vampire, dealing 1,050 Death Drain Damage on a random Wizard and heals the caster for half the Damage dealt.
"The monster begins to rise!" - At the beginning of the third Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast a Dr. Von's Monster (900) on a random Wizard.
"It's a graveyard smash!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard, beguiling them.
This cheat will continue to occur every Round until Shane von Shane is hit again.
Supernova and Mana Burn will not reset this cycle.
Wizards that are beguiled will not trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: This Boss lacks Mastery of the School of Storm.

name: Shark Principal
locations: Lemuria: Night Forest - Second Story Tomb
cheats: [Late] "Look at you, flailing around!" - If a Wizard is late, Shark Principal will cheat-cast his Storm Shark which devours the Wizard for three turns.
[Manipulation] "Mine!" - At the end of every Round, Shark Principal uses Steal Pip.
[Shadow][Spell] "Nom-Nom" - Boss will cast a Shadow version of Storm Shark which devours the Wizard.
[Feint] "Safe At Last" - If Feint is cast on Shark Principal, Shark Student will cast a Shadow Spell moving the Feint onto himself.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.

[Round] "Combat is Hungry Work!" - Every 5 turns, Shark Principal casts a Shadow version of Storm Shark, which first removes all Charms, Wards and Auras then devours a Wizard, dealing no immediate Damage.
While Wizards are devoured, they gain no Pips, lose no Pips, and do not get to see their cards. They do not benefit from group heals, or suffer damage from group attacks.
Devoured Wizards are skipped. Shark Principal still steals a Pip every turn, but Wizards do not lose a Pip when devoured.
At the conclusion of the third Round of being devoured, Shark Principal casts a Shadow version of Tempest, dealing 3,000 Damage.
[Afterlife] "Dinner... is cancelled" - After the Boss is defeated, a Tempest (Shadow) will be cast on the Wizards.
[Afterlife] After any combatant in the battle ring is defeated, a Tempest (Shadow) will be cast on the Wizards, using none of his pips.
This cheat may trigger multiple times.
This cheat will cause damage even to Devoured Wizards.

locations: Polaris: River of Frozen Tears - Cave of the Shattered Sky
spellNotes: Every fifth Round, Shattered Sky Guardian will use the Spell version of Iceblade, then the Treasure Card version of Iceblade the next Round.

name: Simon the Sayer
locations: Wizard City: Sunken City - Simon's Workshop
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Simon will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Mana Burn that deals 400 Balance Damage on the Wizard.
[Round] "Simon says no attacks!" - For the first three Rounds, using a Damage Spell on Simon or one of his Minions will result in Simon retaliating against the caster.

[Damage] "Simon didn't say!" - If a Damage Spell is cast on Simon or one of his Minions while Simon says "no attacks", then Simon will interrupt-cast a version of Insane Bolt that always hits the Wizard who cast the Damage Spell.
Steal Spells do not trigger this cheat.
[Round] "Simon says you can attack now!" - On Rounds 4 through 6, using a Damage Spell will not trigger the "Simon didn't say!" cheat.
[Round] On Round 7, Simon will once again use the "Simon says no attacks!" cheat, and this will last for three more Rounds.
The cycle of being unable and then able to attack for three Rounds at a time will repeat indefinitely for the duration of the duel.
[Round] "I have my friends too! See?" - At the beginning of the second Round, Simon will interrupt-cast a Spell that will either summon a Heartburner Golem or Virulent Gas Golem Minion.
If any of Simon's Minions are defeated before him, then Simon will summon another Golem two Rounds after they are defeated.
If multiple Minions are defeated before Simon, then Simon will summon one new Golem every two Rounds.
[Blade] "Simon says... none of that!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, then Simon will interrupt-cast Disarm on the caster.
[Ward] "My Pet knows tricks too!" - If a Wizard casts a Prism or positive Ward, then Simon's Polterpooch will interrupt-cast Feint on the caster.
Stun Block will trigger this cheat.
Daniel
[Round] On Round 3, and randomly after that, Daniel will interrupt-cast Curse on Simon after any other cheats that Simon uses at the beginning of that Round.
[Round] After Round 3, Daniel will rarely interrupt-cast a Death version of Fairy that restores 300 Health on all Wizards and also applies Guidance.
This Spell can be affected by negative Healing Charms and Life Dispels that have been placed on Simon.
"I got this." or "Easy as pie." - Daniel will always use this line before casting Fairy, although he uses it for Curse as well.

name: Sir Bedevere Lake Ward
locations: Zafaria: Stone Town - Kasbah of Zanj

name: Sir Blackwater
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Castle Darkmoor (Dungeon)
cheats: "I disarm you Wizard!" - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm or Global spell, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast Disarm on the Wizard who cast the Spell.
"Weapon Cast" and Pet "Maycast" that grant Blades will trigger this cheat.
Armor Piercing Charms will not trigger this cheat. (Example: Stormspear)
Aegis can be used to avoid this cheat.
"I reach inside myself to harm you!" - If a Wizard places any type of Weakness Spell on Sir Blackwater, he will interrupt-cast a Dark Pact (+30%) on himself.
AOE Weakness Spells, such as Virulent Plague, will not trigger this cheat.
Interrupt Cycles: All cycles are 5 Rounds each, and repeat until Sir Blackwater is defeated.
"I make this sacrifice!" - At the end of every Round in this cycle, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast Sacrifice (Death) on himself.

"I call upon the spirits of the deep!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast Age of Reckoning, Virulence, a Deathblade (+45%), and Call of Khrulhu. This Round will be the end of the Death cycle and the beginning of the Life cycle.
"Darkmoor armors us!" - At the end of every Round in this cycle, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast a Spirit Armor (500) on himself.

"I am steadfast in my resolve!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast Sanctuary, Cycle of Life, a Guiding Light, and Rebirth (730, 90%). This Round will be the end of the Life cycle and the beginning of the Storm cycle.
"I lay on hands." - At the end of every Round in this cycle, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast Healing Current on himself.

"The power of the Storm will destroy you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast Astraphobia, Galvanic Field, a Stormblade, and Glowbug Squall. This Round will be the end of the Storm cycle and the beginning of the Death cycle.

name: Sir Dracus
cheats: [Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If Sir Dracus or Demimortal Vassal is hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on himself:

cheats: "Do You Want to Battle a Snowman?" - If a Wizard is late to combat, another Awful Snowman will be summoned.

Pet and Weapon May Cast Spells can also trigger the cheat.

name: Sphinx
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Sphinx Cavern
cheats: "For those of you arriving late, TAKE THIS!" - If a Wizard is late, he will interrupt-cast Desert Hound (Sun).

Every second Round, the Sphinx will ask a riddle. The answer to the riddle will be a type of Spell. Casting a Spell of the correct type to answer the riddle will not cause any response from the Sphinx. However, giving the incorrect answer will cause the Sphinx to cheat cast a Spell in response. Once all riddles have been asked, they will then be asked in random order. Players must wait until the next riddle before casting a different type of Spell.
Pet "May Cast" talents can activate this cheat.
Passing will not activate this cheat.
The correct answer is Blade.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast Orthrus (Sun).
The correct answer is "hurt me", given by casting a Damage Spell.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast Power Nova (Shadow).
The correct answer is Trap.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast a +200% Shadow version of Feint with no self Trap.
The correct answer is Weakness.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast an Indemnity-protected Weakness (Shadow).
The correct answer is Heal.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast a version of Blessing that heals 10% of his total Health.

name: Spirit of Darkmoor
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Upper Halls
cheats: "Burn, Wizard!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global Spell, she will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Scald, which deals 999 Fire Damage over 3 rounds to all Wizards.
Pet spells will trigger this cheat.
"I shatter your chains!" - If a Wizard places a Trap on the Spirit of Darkmoor, she will cheat-cast a version of Assail, which deals 850 Myth Damage and places two +35% Myth Traps on the Wizard.
Multi-target Spells, such as Windstorm, will not trigger this cheat.

The depths of Darkmoor strikes at you! - If a Wizard defeats a Minion, she will cheat-cast a Sun version of Eternal Leviathan (777), which deals 777 Life Damage to the Wizard and restores 777 Health to all allies (reviving the defeated Minions).
Interrupt Cycles All interrupt cycles are five Rounds each, and repeat until the duel is finished.
"This place defeats us all!" - At the end of every Round in this cycle, the Spirit of Darkmoor will cast Pierce on a random Wizard.

"The stories of Lore will defeat you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, she will cast a Mythspear (+12%), a Mythblade (+40%), Mystic Colossus, and Saga of Heroes. This Round will be the end of the Myth cycle and the beginning of the Life cycle.
"Darkmoor holds my spirit." - At the end of every Round in this cycle, the Spirit of Darkmoor will cast version of Minor Blessing on herself for 750 healing.

"The world hunger consumes you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, she will cast a +12% Lifespear, a Lifeblade, Namaste, and Hungry Caterpillar on the highest threat Wizard. This Round will be the end of the Life cycle and the beginning of the Fire cycle.
"We are connected." - At the end of every Round in this cycle, the Spirit of Darkmoor will cast Backdraft worth the amount of Pips she has on a random Wizard.

"Your passion will consume you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, she will cast a +12% Firespear, a Fireblade (+40%), Combustion, and Fire from Above to the highest-threat Wizard. This Round will be the end of the Fire cycle and the beginning of the Myth cycle.

"More foes? HA! I am ready for you!" - Mandar casts a Myth Spell to Confuse the Wizard for 2 Rounds.
"Ha! I saw your ambush coming!" - Duck Savage casts Stun on the Wizard.
"Tsk, tsk. You're late!" - The Bantam casts Infection and Angel casts Entangle on the Wizard.
[Balance] Stallion Quartermane
[Spell] "Good show!" - Stallion will say this when casting his Natural Attack.
[Health] "Ah-ah-ah, Never say die!" - If any Boss is low on health, there's a chance he'll cast a Life version of Blessing that restores 10% of the target's max Health.
[Pip] "I claim these pips for the queen!" - Stallion Quartermane will use Steal Pip whenever a Wizard has a bunch of Pips.
Stallion can use a version of Steal Pip that steals 1 or 2 Power Pips.
[Myth] Mandar the Barbarian
[Aura] "Flee! Flee from the savage sword of Mandar!" - At the end of Round 2, Mandar will cast Frenzy (+40%).

[Aura] "By Grom, you'll pay for that!" - If Mandar's Aura is replaced by Stallion Quartermane's Quicken, Mandar will replace it with Frenzy (5 Pip) that applies a +40% outgoing Damage and Quicken Aura for 4 Rounds.
[Damage] "Is that the best you can do, Dog?" - At the beginning of every 7th Round, Mandar will cast a version of Earthquake that deals 145 Damage and removes 1 positive Charm and Ward.
[Heal] "Grom, give me strength!" - Mandar will cast a version of Blessing that restores 5% of his max HP, (652 Health).
[Ice] Duck Savage
[Damage] "Feel my Fists of Bronze!" - Duck Savage will say this when using the 5-Pip version of her Natural Attack and will apply a +75% protected Damage Trap on the target.
[Protected Absorb] "I am the duck of Bronze!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Duck Savage will interrupt-cast a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb version of Frozen Armor on herself.
[Manipulation] No, I don't think so" - If a Wizard casts Steal Ward on her 5,000 Damage Absorb, she'll cast Steal Ward (Star, -All) to steal all positive Wards from the caster.

[Heal over Time] "Don't worry. I'm a doctor" - If a Heal over Time effect is cast by the opponents, such as Buck Gordon casting Power Link, Duck Savage will extend the Heal over Time effect by 1 Round.
[Damage over Time] "I can formulate an antidote!" - If a Damage over Time effect is cast she will interrupt-cast either Cooldown, or a single-target version of Cooldown, on the receivers.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
[Aura] "My stamina has no limits!" - When Duck Savage's 5,000 Damage Absorb is gone, she'll interrupt cast a 99 Round -50% version of Fortify on herself.
This Aura will be recast if it is removed.
[Life] The Bantam
[Pacify] "I need a minute. Talk amongst yourselves." - At the beginning of the first Round, The Bantam will cast a version of Pacify that lasts 3 Rounds.

[Blade] "I'll be taking that, thanks!" - Whenever a Blade is cast on another Wizard or summoned Minion, The Bantam will interrupt-cast a Life Spell on the receiver, replacing it with a Balance Damage Blade equal to half the percent of the original Blade.
For multi-Blade Spells, such as Elemental and Spirit Blade, only the first Blade is removed.
For Blade Spells that target all allies, such as Bladestorm, only the caster's Blade is removed.
[Round] "Get ready, Angel" - The Bantam will shout this line at the end of the Round in which he'll cast Mass Sacred Charge next Round.
[All Friends][Damage] "Angel to the rescue!" - The Bantam will cast Mass Sacred Charge (840).
[Death] Solomon Crane

[Harmful Aura] "Curses be upon thee!" - At the beginning of Round 1 and every 11th Round following, Solomon Crane casts a Damage Aura on all Wizards, starting at -10% Damage and decreasing by 10% each time it is cast, up to a maximum of -50%.

[Aura] "Steel your resolve" - At the end of Round 6 and every 12th Round following, Solomon Crane casts a Resist Aura on his Team, starting at -10% Damage and decreasing by 10% each time it is cast, up to a maximum of -50%.
[Shield] "May your wards crumble upon you" - If a Shield is cast, Solomon Crane will cast a Shadow Spell to convert one Ward to half of the original percent as a Tower Shield.
Aegis is ineffective in preventing this cheat.
For Shields Spells targeting all allies, such as Legion Shield, he will convert all Shields for half the percent.
Absorb Wards trigger this cheat, but the Spell doesn't affect them.
[Trap] "A crude tactic" - If a Wizard casts a Trap, he'll convert the Trap into half the percent as a universal Trap.
Traps placed on all enemies will also be converted.
[Shield][Damage over Time] "Your end is nigh!" - If a Wizard casts a Shield and has a Damage over Time, Solomon Crane will cast Detonate on them.
[Fire] Buck Gordon
[Blade] "All right!" - Whenever a Blade is cast on Buck Gordon, he will convert it to a Fire Blade with double the percent.

This cheat sometimes activates when Stallion Quartermane casts Bladestorm, but it doesn't do anything as Buck Gordon didn't get the Blade. However, this cheat will activate the second Blade from Solomon's Dark Pact.
"You're going down!" - Warning call for the Rain of Fire cheat.
"Oh, come on!" - If Buck Gordon takes Damage, he'll refrain from casting Rain of Fire.
"Here it comes..." - Buck Gordon will cheat-cast Rain of Fire.
[Shadow] The Shadoe
[Round] Round 1 Cheats: - Beginning of Round 1 The Shadoe will cast the following cheats.
[Damage] The Shadoe will cast a version of Dark Fiend from The Shadoe.
[Spell] "Mind Talisman... Activate?" - The Shadoe will cast a Shadow spell giving wizards Card Limit (0 Pip) and Card Limit (2 Shadow Pip).
"I'll shoulder some of the burden" - The Shadoe will cast a version of Shadow Sentinel which intercepts 25% of Damage.
[Global] "Synthesizer's on the blink!" - The Shadoe will cast a Balance Equalize Global which sets the Damage limit to 9,999.
"That solves nothing" - If a Global is cast, The Shadoe will cast a Balance version of Equalize which limits the Damage cap to 9,999.
[Manipulation] "I believe in you!" - Randomly The Shadoe will cast Donate Shadow on a random ally of either team.
[Trap] "The Shadoe says 'No.'" - Shadoe will cast a Shadow Spell to steal a Trap from an ally to herself.
If Traps are placed on multiple enemies at once, she'll only move the Trap from the first recipient.
[Damage] "I can shoulder no more!" - The Shadoe will occasionally cast the Damage attack from Shadow Seraph.
"Hahahahahahahaha!" - After this cheat, she will cast a version of Dark Feind from The Shadoe.
[Storm] Dog Tracy
[Heal] "Hey, don't quit your day job" - Dog Tracy will cast a version of Healing Current (Shadow) that affects all allies.
[Pacify] "You're now in witness protection" - Dog Tracy will cast a version of Mega Soothe on an ally that lasts 3 Rounds.
[Damage] "That's our prime suspect. Get 'em!" - Dog Tracy will say this when casting his Natural Attack. The Natural Attack will taunt himself after dealing Damage.

name: Stallion Quartermane (Standard)
cheats: Stallion Quartermane
"Good show!" - Stallion will say this when casting his Natural Attack.
Duck Savage
"Feel my Fists of Bronze!" - Duck Savage will say this when casting her Natural Attack.
Dog Tracy
"That's our prime suspect, get 'em!" - Dog Tracy will say this when casting his Natural Attack.

name: Starfall Spider (Retired)
spellNotes: Deals an unknown amount of Balance Damage

name: Stay-Puffed Marshfellow
locations: Karamelle: Nibbleheim Mines - Skeleton Key - Marshmellow Critter
cheats: Stay-Puffed Marshfellow
"Fun for everyone!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast S'more Machine (Tier 3a) on the Wizard.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.

"Hot Potato!" - At the beginning of the first Round and every four Rounds, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Shadow DoT (Damage-over-Time) Spell on a random Wizard. This DoT has about 100% extra pierce dealing 2,000 Damage after 4 Rounds. (Deals 5,265 Damage with the Damage Outboost).

The Wizard who receives the DoT will be given a Hot Potato: Enemy Spell and a Hot Potato: Friend Spell. These Spells are 0-Pip versions of Shift and allow the Wizard to shift a DoT to an enemy or an ally, depending on the Spell used.

"That not how you play!" - If a Wizard uses Triage, Mass Triage, or Shift to remove the Hot Potato DoT, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Shadow Spell to instantly Expel both the caster of the Triage and the Wizard that had their Hot Potato DoT removed by the Triage.
Hamadryad will not trigger this cheat.
"Pass the potato!" - If a Wizard uses Hot Potato: Enemy to pass the Hot Potato to Stay-Puffed Marshfellow, it will shift the Hot Potato to a random Marshfellow Minion in the battle.
This cheat only seems to activate if the Wizard uses the Hot Potato: Enemy the Round after the Hot Potato cheat.
If there are no Marshfellow Minions present in battle, this cheat will not activate.

"That not how you play!" - Stay-Puffed Marshfellow has three cheats that trigger when a Wizard uses Blades or Global Spells. They will cycle in the order they are listed below. After the third cheat, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will start from the first cheat again.
The first time a Blade or Global Spell is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast Mana Burn (Shadow) on the receiver of the Blade.
The second time a Blade or Global Spell is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast Bad Juju (Shadow) on the receiver of the Blade.
The third time a Blade or Global Spell is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast Disarm (Shadow), which removes two Blades from the receiver.
Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will only target the caster of Bladestorm with these cheats.
"That not how you play!" - When a Feint is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Spell to remove all Traps from all his allies.
Indemnity can be used to prevent a Trap’s removal.
Mass Feint can trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.

"Go BOOM!" - After a Marshfellow is defeated by the Shadow Damage overtime that deals 5,265 Damage, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Shadow Spell to heal 100% max Health to all allies reviving the minion.
Marshfellow Minions
"Potato to you!" - At the end of a Round, if a Marshfellow Minion has the Hot Potato, the Marshfellow Minion will use a Fire School Spell to shift it onto a random Wizard.

name: Stheno
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus - Temple of Longing

name: Stheno (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Temple of Longing (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade][Aura] "Winter always wins!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm or helpful Aura, Stheno will cast Ice Elemental on the recipient.
[Harmful Aura] "Winter withstands!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Aura on Stheno, she will cast Tower Shield on herself.

name: Stieg Quicksand
locations: Khrysalis: Crescent Beach - Desert Hopper Cave

name: Stokes
cheats: [Round] At the beginning of the first Round, and every other Round thereafter, Stokes will summon an additional Minion at random:
[Ice] "Aye, Ice Magic, that's the missing ingredient!" Stokes will cast an Ice Ninja Pigs Spell that deals 500 Ice Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons an Ice Ninja Pig Minion.
[Life] "Maybe the Curse just needs more Life!" Stokes will cast a Life Cyclops Spell that deals 500 Life Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Life Cyclops Minion.
[Storm] "Now feel the Scholomance's Storm!" Stokes will cast a Storm Medusa Spell that deals 500 Storm Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Storm Medusa minion.
[Fire] "Hmm, have a little Fire!" Stokes will cast a Fire Bloodbat Spell that deals 500 Fire Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Fire Bloodbat Minion.
[Death] "Let's see the Scholomance powered by Death!" Stokes will cast a Death Troll that deals 500 Death Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Death Troll Minion.
[Balance] "I'll mix the Curse up with Balance!" Stokes will cast a Balance Minotaur Spell that deals 500 Balance Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Balance Minotaur Minion.
The final Minion summoned can take the place of Stokes if he is defeated on the same Round.

name: Stone of Echoes
locations: Azteca: Zultun Dock - Abandoned House (Zultun Dock)
cheats: [Curse] "The end must be echoed or changed!" - At the beginning of every Round, Stone of Echoes will interrupt-cast to give all Wizards a -15% Charm and a DOT that does 150 Balance Damage over 3 Rounds.
[Healing] If Stone of Echoes is defeated while his Weakness cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "...Or the end comes to pass!" - At the end of the 3rd Round, and after Stone of Echoes revives, he will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Chimera (Tier 2b)]
[Balance] "The end is delayed..." - If Chimera (Tier 2b) is used, Stone of Echoes will remove all negative Charms and DOTs.

name: Storm Caiman
locations: Azteca: Saltmeadow Swamp - Pyramid of Storm Eye

name: Stout Spawn
locations: Wizard City: Arena - Avalon Battlegrounds,Arena - Celestia Battlegrounds,Arena - Mirage Battlegrounds
spellNotes: Casts a 2-Pip version of Power Conversion that gives the target 1 regular Pip upon casting and grants the target 1 Pip from incoming Spells ranked 2+.
Casts a version of Spiked Buckler that applies a +75 Damage Blade and an unprotected 75 Damage Absorb Ward.
Casts a version of Thieving Beetle that deals 100 Balance Damage.

name: Sudri
cheats: "Wrong brother! I'm it!" - Casting Spells at a Boss without the Stun Shield will trigger Sudri casting Hydra dealing 190 Fire, Ice & Storm Damage.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Ice Damage.

name: Sugarr Eater of Souls
cheats: "More souls for Sugarr" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Sugarr Eater of Souls will cast a Shadow version of S'more Machine which deals 1,020 Shadow Damage, removes all Pips and places an Impede on the Wizard.

"Sugarr still has some bite left!" - If a Wizard defeats Sugarr Eater of Souls while a Sugar Cube minion is still alive, he'll be revived with 75% of his total health, and cast a Shadow S'more Machine that removes 100% of the Wizard's Health defeating the Wizard who defeated the boss.

"Beware the aftertaste!" - If a Wizard defeats a Sugar Cube, the defeated Sugar Cube will cast the 1,020 Damage Shadow S'more Machine on the Wizard that defeated them, removing their pips, and placing an Impede on them.

"Sugarr Rush!" - If Sugarr Eater of Souls takes damage, the Boss will cast a version of Sugar Rush that gives all allies 5 Pips, first giving 2 Power Pips then 1 Pip without an Impede aura, then places Quicken on all allies.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Death Damage.

name: Sun Darkling
locations: Khrysalis: The Hive - The Black Hole

name: Sunburn Bear
locations: Darkmoor: Howling Lands - Kingdom of Were-a-Lot

name: Sundered Seam Guardian
locations: Polaris: Frigid Maw - Cave of Sundered Seam
spellNotes: Every 5th Round this Boss will use the Spell version of Stormblade, then the Treasure Card version of Stormblade the Round after.

name: Sunfire Elemental (Rank 23)
locations: Krokotopia: Halls of Taweret - Bright's Lab

name: Swirling Durvish
spellNotes: Casts a version of Grasping Ghost that applies a +150 Damage Trap.
It is currently unknown whether the Trap effect of Death's Champion occurs.

name: Sylster Glowstorm
cheats: Interrupt Cycle: All cycles last four Rounds, except for the first Trap cycle, which lasts three Rounds. These cycles start on the first Round of the fight and repeat until all enemies are defeated.

"Traps go undetected in the light!" - Sylster Glowstorm will cheat-cast a light Doom and Gloom, during which Wizards may cast Traps and Prisms. Sylster Glowstorm will remove positive Charms by cheat-casting a version of Cleanse Charm that removes three positive Charms from the caster.
Dissipate can be used to dispel this cheat.
Negative Charm Spells, such as Weakness, Smoke Screen, and Dispels, trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells do not trigger this cheat.
Shadow Creature Blades trigger this cheat.
Aegis prevents one Charm spell from being removed.

At the beginning of the first Round of this cycle starting on Round 8, Sylster Glowstorm cheat-casts a version of Cleanse Charm that removes three positive Charms on all Wizards and a version of Cleanse Ward that removes three Traps on himself and all Minions.

"The blade goes unseen in the dark!" - Sylster Glowstorm will cheat-cast a dark Doom and Gloom, during which Wizards may cast Blades and other positive Charms. Sylster Glowstorm will remove Traps and Prisms by cheat-casting Cleanse Ward that removes one Trap or Prism.
Vaporize can be used to dispel this cheat.
For Elemental Trap and Spirit Trap, only the last Trap (Storm, Life) is removed.
Multi-target Trap Spells, such as Windstorm and Mass Prisms, do not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity prevents one Trap spell from being removed.
At the beginning of the first Round of this cycle, Sylster Glowstorm cheat-casts a version of Cleanse Ward that removes three Traps on himself and all Minions.

"No Traps?!" / "No Blades?!" - If a Wizard casts a single-target attack Spell without activating a Trap (during a light cycle) or Blade (during a dark cycle), then the enemy that was attacked will cheat-cast the following Spell:
Sylster Glowstorm casts Ra, dealing 1,460-1,540 Balance Damage.
Unbalance can dispel this cheat.
Spellhammer Sorcerer casts Scarecrow, dealing 600 Death Damage.
Water-Wing casts Rebirth (no Absorbs), healing 750 Health. With Doom and Gloom active, this cheat will only heal 375 Health.
All Enemy attack Spells do not trigger this cheat.
Single-target Spells that also attack all enemies such as Sun Serpent trigger this cheat.
If a Wizard casts any Global Spell, Sylster Glowstorm will replace the Global with the current Doom and Gloom.
Strangle can be used to dispel either Doom and Gloom, although Charms or Traps will still be removed if casted during the wrong Global, no matter what Global is currently active.
Minion Boss Cheats: - Cheats will carry over to the next in Boss in charge as follows.

"Not da' Boss!" - If Sylster Glowstorm has been defeated, the Spellhammer Sorcerer replaces Sylster's role in casting the light and dark Doom and Gloom cycles, starting with the initial three Round light Doom and Gloom.

"Where did everyone go?" - If Sylster Glowstorm and the Spellhammer Sorcerer have been defeated, the Water-Wing replaces the role in casting the light and dark Doom and Gloom cycles, starting with the initial three Round light Doom and Gloom.

name: Sysiphan
locations: Dragonspyre: Plaza of Conquests - Sysiphan's Tower

name: Taffy
cheats: Taffy

[Late] "The more the merrier!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Taffy will place an Aegis version of Tower Shield (-55%) on Ricky and Tiki, and an Aegis version of Frozen Armor (185) on himself.
[Trap] "One dirty trick deserves another!" - If a Wizard places a Trap on any of the brothers, he will cast an Ice version of Cleanse Ward to remove 1 Trap from the target.
Indemnity can be used to prevent the removal of a particular Trap.
Spells that leave Traps behind, such as Fire from Above, will trigger this cheat.
Using Mass Feint (+75%) will cause Taffy to remove the Trap placed on the caster. This will not remove any of the Traps on the Bosses.
Spells like Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.
Ricky
[Late] "Cheater!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Ricky will triple-cast Healing Current on himself, giving 100, 400, then 1,000 health.
[Blade] "Nice try!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, then Ricky will cast Steal Charm on the Wizard that received the Blade.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.
Aegis can be used to prevent removal.
If a tri-Blade Spell is used, then only the last part of the Blade will be removed (Fire for Elemental Blades and Life for Spirit Blades).
If a multi-target Blade Spell is used (e.g. Bladestorm), only the Wizard who cast the Spell will be affected.
Steal Charm can be used to steal back the Blade without a penalty.
Supercharge does not trigger this cheat.
Tiki
[Late] "You should be ashamed!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Tiki will cast Guiding Light on himself and Ricky and an Aegis-Protected Spirit Armor on Taffy.
[Damage over Time] "Let's spice things up!" - If a DoT is cast on anyone in the duel, he will remove it.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.

name: Tajniak
locations: Polaris: River of Frozen Tears - Sunless Shrine
cheats: The goal of this battle is to defeat the untruthful bear before the honest bear. Jawniak is the liar.
This Boss will shout statements about Jawniak's attacks every few Rounds.
Jawniak will shout statements about Tajniak's attacks every few Rounds.
Tajniak
Tajniak will always cast Cloaked versions of positive Charms.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Feint is placed on Tajniak, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward that switches the Traps between himself and the caster.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
Jawniak
Jawniak will always cast Cloaked versions of negative Charms.
If Tajniak is defeated first, Jawniak will cast Poison on the Wizard in the first position, and then detonate it immediately for ~33,000 Death Damage.
Every Round, he will cast Poison on another Wizard, going in order of battle position.
Fleeing or porting will not stop this cheat.
spellNotes: This Creature's Brilliant Light (Cloaked) is a single target Spell.

name: Takanobu the Masterless (Tier 1)
locations: MooShu: Cave of Solitude - Cave of Solitude Gold Key Room
cheats: Takanobu the Masterless
"No! I disarm you." - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, Takanobu the Masterless will cast Disarm on the Wizard who received the Blade.
If a triple Blade is cast (e.g. Elemental Blade or Spirit Blade), he will only remove the last Blade (Fire or Life).
If a multi-target Blade is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), he will only remove the Blade from the Wizard that cast the Spell.
"My katana dances around your shield." - If a Wizard casts Storm Shield, Volcanic Shield, or Glacial Shield, he will cheat-cast Storm Prism on the Wizard who received the Shield.
"KIAI!" - If Takanobu has at least 5 Pips, and an odd number of Pips, at the end of a Round, he will cheat-cast a 50% Supercharge on himself.
"This wind is an omen!" - If Takanobu has 6 or more Pips at the beginning of the Round, he will cheat-cast Darkwind.
Bladeless Ronin
"We must stay centered!" - If a Wizard attacks any enemy with a Balance Spell, the Bladeless Ronin in the first position (Dagger) will cheat-cast Unbalance on the Wizard.
If an All Enemies attack is used, the Bladeless Ronin will cast Unbalance for each enemy that was hit.
Attacking with Chimera results in the Bladeless Ronin casting three separate Spirit Defuses.
Attacking with Hydra results in the Bladeless Ronin casting three separate Elemental Defuses.
Attacks which deal no direct damage, but apply aura effects, such as Scald or Heck Hound will not activate any cheats by the Bladeless Ronin.
"Guard against the elements!" - If a Wizard attacks any enemy with a Fire, Ice, or Storm Spell, the Bladeless Ronin in the second position (Key) will cheat-cast Elemental Defuse on the Wizard.
If an All Enemies attack is used, the Bladeless Ronin will cast Elemental Defuse for each enemy that was hit.
Triggering a Prism that converts damage to Life, Death, or Myth, will result in the Bladeless Ronin casting Spirit Defuse instead of Elemental Defuse.
"Spirits, guard us!" - If a Wizard attacks any enemy with a Life, Death, or Myth Spell, the Bladeless Ronin in the third position (Gem) will cheat-cast Spirit Defuse on the Wizard.
If an All Enemies attack is used, the Bladeless Ronin will cast Spirit Defuse for each enemy that was hit.
Attacks that hit multiple times, such as Minotaur, will trigger the Bladeless Ronin to cast a separate Spirit Defuse for each hit.
Triggering a Prism that converts Damage to Fire, Ice, or Storm, will result in the Bladeless Ronin casting Elemental Defuse instead of Spirit Defuse.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Storm Damage.

Attacks that hit multiple times, such as Minotaur, will trigger the Bladeless Ronin to cast a separate Spirit Defuse for each hit.
Triggering a Prism that converts Damage to Fire, Ice, or Storm, will result in the Bladeless Ronin casting Elemental Defuse instead of Spirit Defuse.

cheats: Takanobu the Masterless [Spiral]
