If a multi-target Blade is cast, then Takanobu will only remove the Blade from the caster.
[Ward] "My katana dances around your shield." - If a Wizard casts any Storm Shield Spell, then Takanobu will interrupt-cast Storm Prism on the recipient of the Shield.
[All Enemies (Squared)] "Control your Energy!" - If a Wizard casts an All Enemies Spell, then Takanobu will interrupt-cast a version of Tempest that always deals 560 Storm Damage.
[Pip] "This wind is an omen!" - At the beginning of any Round, if Takanobu has at least 6 Pips, then he will interrupt-cast Darkwind.
[Pip] "KIAI!" - At the end of any Round, if Takanobu has at least 5 Pips, then he will interrupt-cast Supercharge on himself.
Bladeless Ronin
[Dagger][Balance][Dispel] "We must stay centered!" - If any enemy receives Balance Damage, then this Bladeless Ronin will interrupt-cast an all-enemies version of Unbalance.

[Key][Elemental][Dispel] "Guard against the elements!" - If any enemy receives Fire, Ice, or Storm Damage, then this Bladeless Ronin will interrupt-cast an all-enemies version of Quench, Melt, or Dissipate based on the School of the Damage that was received.

[Gem][Spirit][Dispel] "Spirits, guard us!" - If any enemy receives Myth, Life, or Death Damage, then this Bladeless Ronin will interrupt-cast an all-enemies version of Vaporize, Entangle, or Strangle based on the School of the Damage that was received.
Damage over Time effects do not trigger the Bladeless Ronins' cheats.
If a Spell deals Damage more than once, then the Bladeless Ronin will cast an all-enemies Dispel matching the School of the Damage that were received in the order that they were received.
Casting a Spell that hits multiple enemies will result in the respective Bladeless Ronin repeating their cheat per enemy hit.
Casting Spells such as Hydra on any enemy will result in the [Key] Bladeless Ronin casting Quench, Melt, and Dissipate, in that order.

Casting spells such as Scales of Destiny on any enemy will result in the [Gem] Bladeless Ronin casting Entangle, the [Key] Bladeless Ronin casting Melt, and the [Dagger] Bladeless Ronin casting Unbalance, in that order.
Dealing Damage through a Prism will result in the Bladeless Ronin casting a Dispel that matches the School of the Damage after the Prism is applied.

name: Taking Tree (Challenge)
cheats: [Round] At the beginning of the first Round, Taking Tree will summon a Wandering Weed Minion.
[Charm][Curse][Ward][Jinx][Heal over Time][Damage over Time] "Take this...and I'll take that":
If a Wizard casts a Helpful Charm, Helpful Ward, or Healing over Time effect, Taking Tree will swap all Helpful and Harmful effects of the same type with the receiver.
If a Wizard casts a Harmful Charm, Harmful Ward, or Damage over Time effect, Taking Tree will swap all Helpful and Harmful effects of the same type with the caster.
If a multi-target Spell is cast, such as Bladestorm, Taking Tree will swap with the caster.
Secondary Spell effects, such as the Damage over Time from Fire Dragon, will trigger this cheat.

name: Taking Tree (Standard)
cheats: "Take this...and I'll take that":
If a Wizard casts a Helpful Charm, Helpful Ward, or Healing over Time effect, Taking Tree will swap the effects with the receiver.
If a Wizard casts a Harmful Charm, Harmful Ward, or Damage over Time effect, Taking Tree will swap the effects with the caster.
If a multi-target Spell is cast, such as Bladestorm, Taking Tree will swap with the caster only.
If a multi-effect Spell is cast, such as Elemental Blade, only the last effect will be swapped (e.g. Fire for Elemental Blade).
Secondary Spell effects, such as the Damage over Time from Fire Dragon, will trigger this cheat.

The second time this cheat triggers, the Sun version of Mass Supernova will deal 66% of every Wizard's Health.

name: The Devourer
locations: Wizard City: The Catacombs - Edge of the Spiral
cheats: If a Wizard joins the battle late, The Devourer will "devour" the Wizard.
At the start of the first Round, he casts a Sun Spell to summon any of the following Minions:
Acrid Bile - Fire
Pestilient Bile - Ice
Seething Bile - Storm
Noxious Bile - Myth
Poison Bile - Life
Toxic Bile - Death
Hazardous Bile - Balance
After the first Round, and again after the fifth, he will cast a Natural Attack that "devours" a random Wizard. This cycle will stop after the second Wizard is "devoured".
The Natural Attack removes all unprotected Wards, Charms, and Auras from the Wizard.

After being devoured, the Wizard will lose their combat interface and will be forced to pass for the duration of the idle timer. The Wizard is not able to be targeted and gains no Pips during this state.
During the third Round after his turn, he will cast an AoE Shadow Natural Attack for 1,500 Shadow Damage, and return the devoured Wizard to combat.
Any Spells or cheats cast by him after three Rounds will trigger this cheat.
He will then summon one of the above Minions.
If a Wizard has a Blade present for three Rounds (including the Round it was cast), The Devourer will cast an AoE version of Steal Charm (Shadow) that steals one positive Charm.
For example, on Round 1, a Wizard places a Stormblade on himself. At the beginning of Round 3, The Devourer will cast his Spell to steal one Charm from everyone.
"<SHRIEK!>" - If a Feint is placed on him, The Devourer will cast Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2) on himself which removes the last two Traps placed.
This can be avoided by using Indemnity.
At the end of every fourth Round, The Devourer will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward, which will remove all Traps from himself.
For each Trap he removes, he will add a protected Balanceblade (+100%) to his Minions or himself.
Indemnity-Protected Traps will result in two Blades being added.

cheats: "Tardy, Tardy!" - If a Wizard is late, The Headmaster will cast a Star version of Insane Bolt taking 75% of the target's max Health.

Interrupt Cycles The Headmaster uses two different cheat cycles: a "Primary Interrupt Cycle" and a "Secondary Interrupt Cycle." The primary cycle occurs only once, and the secondary cycle can occur at any point after the first ends.
Primary Interrupt Cycle:
During this cycle, the Headmaster will not attack the Wizard team.
At the start of the duel, the following cheats occur:
Boss will interrupt-cast a Star Spell giving him -999% Shields for each of the seven Schools of Magic.
If a Wizard joins the duel after the health bars show after someone has joined, this cheat triggers twice on the first Round.
Casting a Damage spell on his Shields will result in him casting a single -999% Shield for the resulting School Shield that was broken.
Pierce and Homework can be used to remove the Shields.
The single Balance Shield has the appearance of a Tower Shield.
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard casts Shatter, Boss will recast a Star Spell giving him -999% Shields for each of the seven Schools of Magic.
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard casts Steal Ward, Boss will cast Steal Ward (Star, -1) to steal it back.
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard casts Double Steal Ward, Boss will cast a Star version of Double Steal Ward to steal them back.
If a Wizard casts Empower, Boss will cast a Star Spell giving him -999% Death Shield.
An invisible Polymorph will be cast on all Wizards giving them different Spells for the cheats that follow.
All Wizards' Health will be set at a fraction of 10,000, proportionate to the Health they had entering the duel.
Wizards will not be able to use their own Spells until the end of this cycle.
At the end of this cycle, the invisible Polymorph will end, and each Wizard's Health will be set back to their own, proportionate to the Health they had out of 10,000.
This cycle only occurs once at the beginning of the duel.
Treasure Cards that have been drawn during the Polymorph Stages cannot be discarded.
"Roll Call! How Many Students in Attendance?" - Each Wizard must select the total number of Wizards in the duel.
Polymorph Spells given during this stage include. Cards are in a randomized order.
Attendance (1), Attendance (2), Attendance (3) & Attendance (4)
Draw will give Fake Attendance
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard passes, Boss will cast a Star version of Crow removing 10% max Health.
"Wrong!" - If a Wizard chooses incorrectly, Boss will cast a Star version of Crow removing 10% max Health.
"No Cheating!" - If a Wizard casts Fake Attendance, Boss casts a Star version of Immolate removing 35% max Health.
"Hand In Your Homework!" - This turn can be used for Shield breaking.
Polymorph Spells given during this stage include.
Homework (Balance), Homework (Death), Homework (Fire), Homework (Ice), Homework (Life), Homework (Myth) & Homework (Storm)
Discarding and using Draw spell will give Copy.
"Gold Star" - Boss will say this if a Wizard casts any Spell at him.
"No Cheating!" - If a Wizard uses Copy, Boss casts a Star version of Frostbite removing 15% max Health.
"You Disappoint Me" - If the second Wizard doesn't turn in Homework he'll cast a Star version of Insane Bolt removing 20% max Health on the Wizard. This includes if they casted Copy.
"The Lecture is About to Begin" - Each Wizard may select the buff/debuff combination they wish to use on themselves.
Listen, Participate, Take Notes, Snack Break & Ask Questions
Draw will give Doze Off.
Casting Snack Break will cause the Wizard to fail the next Pop Quiz.
A Pet-cast Conviction before this turn may help the Wizard avoid the Stun.
"Hey, Wake Up!" - If a Wizard passes, Boss will cast a Star version of Cyclops removing 30% max Health.
"Cheaters never prosper!"' - If the Wizard casts Doze Off, Boss will cast a Star version of Leprechaun removing 25% max Health, and a Star version of Cyclops removing 30% Health from the Wizard.
If two Wizards both cast Doze Off, this cheat doesn't trigger.
"Pop Quiz! HA HA HA!" - Boss will cast a School Aura on each Wizard and ask which of the School Spells is opposite.
"Name the opposing school"
"Death!!!" "Fire!!!" "Ice!!!" "Life!!!" "Myth!!!" "Storm!!!" - are the Aura Effects that appear when the Aura is cast on a Wizard.
Death School, Fire School, Ice School, Life School, Myth School & Storm School
Draw will give Cheat
"Let's See How You Did..." - Boss will cast a Star version of Supernova removing 40% max Health on the Wizards who fail to answer correctly.
"Correct! Surprising..." - If a Wizard answers correctly, the Aura will be dispelled without Damage.
"Hmm...Correct!"' - Boss will use a version of Supernova removing the Aura without Damage.
"Cheaters never prosper!"' - If a Wizard casts Cheat, Boss will cast Impede on the Wizard.
Secondary Interrupt Cycle: "Hand In Your Homework!"
Occasionally, Boss will interrupt with two-Round Homework cycles from the Second Polymorph Stage.
During this cycle, the Headmaster has normal cards, and will attack the Wizard team.
Boss will cast an invisible Polymorph that ends at the end of the second Round.
These turns can be used for further Shield breaking.
Shadow Creature and Shadow Self spells will be removed when the Polymorph is placed on the Wizards, causing the Backlash to deal 4,000 Damage.

name: The Monster (Challenge)
locations: Marleybone: Barkingham Palace - Ransacked Throne Room (Challenge)
cheats: [Minion] "I just... I just... want my father back." - At the beginning of the first Round, The Monster will make himself untargetable.
If a Wizard targeted The Monster with a Spell this Round, they will be forced to Pass. Spells that do not target The Monster will still cast.
If no Notes, or Note 1, have been selected, The Monster will summon a Memory of Katzenstein (Balance, Challenge).
The Monster will become targetable again starting on the third Round.

[Afterlife] "Father's memory drives me. I will not fall!" - If The Monster is defeated before all of his Minions have been defeated, he will cast a version of Heartbeat that restores 25% of his max Health (1,012) to himself.
[Death][Charm] "Your pitchforks can't hurt me." - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm, The Monster will cast Banshee (Tier 5c) on the recipient of the Charm.
[Storm][Heal over Time] "There is no healing in this life." - If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time effect, The Monster will cast Triton (Tier 5b) on the recipient of the Heal over Time effect.
[Storm][Damage over Time] "Torches won't burn me." - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect, the recipient of the Damage over Time effect will cast Stormzilla (Tier 5b) on the caster.
[Monstrology Rank 1][Balance] If Note 1 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Balance, Challenge) on the first Round.
[Trap] "Father kept all things in Balance... as they should be!" - While the Balance Minion is present, whenever a Wizard casts a Trap, The Monster will cast Trap Dilution on all enemies.
[Monstrology Rank 2][Death] If Note 2 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Death, Challenge) on the first Round.

[Afterlife] "Father's loss will be felt by all!" - While the Death Minion is present, whenever a Minion is defeated, The Monster will cast a Spell that deals 25% max Health Damage to the Wizard who defeated the Minions for each Minion defeated.
This cheat will still trigger if the Death Minion was one of the Minions that was defeated.
[Monstrology Rank 3][Fire] If Note 3 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Fire, Challenge) at the end of the second Round.
[Global] "Father taught me the power of rage!" - At the end of the third Round, The Monster will cast a Fire Spell that applies a +50% outgoing Damage Global.
This cheat will not re-trigger if the Global is replaced.
This cheat will still trigger even if the Fire Minion is defeated on the third Round.
[Monstrology Rank 4][Ice] If Note 4 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Ice, Challenge) at the end of the second Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Threat] "Father wanted to shield me from the world." - At the beginning of the Round after the Ice Minion becomes active, and every other Round thereafter, the Ice Minion will cast Mega Taunt.
[Monstrology Rank 5][Life] If Note 5 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Life, Challenge) at the end of the third Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Monstrology Rank 6][Myth] If Note 6 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Myth, Challenge) at the end of the third Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Trap] "Father's glory lives through me!" - While the Myth Minion is present, after The Monster damages a Wizard, he will cast an all enemies version of Hex.
[Monstrology Rank 7][Storm] If Note 7 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Storm, Challenge) at the end of the third Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Damage] "Father used lightning to give me life. I have another use." - While the Storm Minion is present, randomly at the end of the Round The Monster will cast Wild Bolt on a random Wizard.

name: The Nullity
cheats: Buffs:
During this fight a Wizard can cast Shadow Surge and Retribution during this battle.

Additionally, Wizards can use all previously available inspired casts spells including: Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle, and Monumental (All Allies) which gives all Wizards one Monumental card.
Inspire Cantrips may also be cast into this fight by anyone who hasn't joined after a player initiates the fight.
The Nullity:
At the beginning of the first Round, The Nullity will cast a Shadow Spell that forces all Wizards to pass.
At the end of every Round, The Nullity will cast Power Current on itself.

The Nullity will announce a School at the beginning of the battle and will choose another School every three Rounds. The new School will be announced after the fourth turn begins. These Schools conform to Roshambo rules and govern what Charms, Wards, Heal- or Damage over Time Spells may be used. At the end of the third Round, The Nullity will cast an all enemies Power Nova version of the Scion Spells that benefits based on the chosen School.
Schools are announced in text as follows:
"The Nullity is Fired Up" - Fire Phase: [Fire]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Fire Elf on a wizard.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Scald on all wizards.
The Scion is powered up if two or more Damage over Time effects are on a Wizard.
"The Nullity Keeps Its Cool" - Ice Phase: [Ice]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Tower Shield on itself.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Shield Wall on itself.
The Scion is powered up if four or more positive Wards are on the Nullity.
"The Nullity is Electrifying" - Storm Phase: [Storm]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Star Blade on itself.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Guiding Light (x3) on itself.
The Scion is powered up if four or more positive Charms are on the Nullity.
"The Nullity Ascends to Legend" - Myth Phase: [Myth]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Mythic Fuel on a wizard.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Mass Myth Trap on all wizards.
The Scion is powered up if four or more negative Wards are on a Wizard.
"The Nullity Feels Alive" - Life Phase: [Life]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Sprite on itself.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Sprite Swarm on all allies.
The Scion is powered up if two or more Heal over Time effects are on the Nullity.
"The Nullity Foresees your Doom" - Death Phase: [Death]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Threefold Fever on a wizard.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Mass Infection on all wizards.
The Scion is powered up if four or more negative Charms are on a Wizard.

The Nullity is joined in battle by an elite Minion from a random School, both of which also follow Roshambo rules to counter Wizard Spells. Fire counters positive and negative wards. Ice counters positive and negative Charms. Storm counters Damage over Time and Heal over Time effects. Myth counters negative Charms and positive Wards. Death counters positive Charms and Heal over Time effects. Life counters negative Wards and Damage over Time effects. Balance counters when over nine Pips.
spellNotes: Casts a 6 Pip version of Blizzard that deals 555 Ice Damage and removes 3 positive Charms from all enemies.
Casts a 6 Pip version of Ratatoskr's Spin that deals 555 Life Damage, and removes 3 negative Wards from all allies.

name: The Pestilence
spellNotes: Casts a protected Myth Shield, Stormblade, and Bladestorm.

name: The Prophet
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Tomb of Night
cheats: The Prophet

"No Sanctuary in the Night." - At the beginning of the first Round and if a Wizard joins the duel late, The Prophet casts a negative version of Acrimony on all Wizards that turns all incoming Healing into Damage for 99 Rounds. The Aura is applied twice, but the effects do not stack.

If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell on the first Round, one of the negative Auras will be replaced, and the Wizard will receive the effects of the remaining one, as well the one they cast, at the same time. This will change the 99 Round Aura timer to 4 Rounds to match the Wizard's Aura Spell, and all Aura effects will end once the timer expires.
Casting any Aura on any other turn will cause The Prophet to cast a no-Damage version of Supernova, removing the Wizard's Aura, and then will recast this cheat on all wizards.
The Aura does not convert healing received from drain spells
Shadow Guardian
At the beginning of the first Round, both Shadow Guardians will cast a permanent version of Shadow Sentinel which provides Intercept 90%, +80 Critical Block, and -30% incoming Damage.

locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Chamber of Phoebe
cheats: The Prophet

If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell on the first Round, one of the negative Auras will be replaced, and the Wizard will receive the effects of the remaining one, as well the one they cast, at the same time.
Casting any Aura on any other turn will cause The Prophet to cast a no-Damage version of Supernova, removing the Wizard's Aura, and then will recast this cheat on all wizards.
Enki the Heretic
"To face the wolf, bare the claws." - At the beginning of every second Round, Enki the Heretic will cheat-cast a +25% Fireblade or +25% Deathblade on each Wizard.
"The Light burns!" - At the beginning of the every fifth Round, Enki will cheat-cast Incindiate.
"The Light blesses you." - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm, Enki will cheat-cast an all enemies version of Sprite, restoring 500 Health over 4 Rounds on each Wizard.
"Feel the bite of Raven!" - After Enki attacks, or if a Wizard joins the duel late, Enki will cheat-cast Scald.

name: The Quake Charmer
cheats: "Better quake than never!" - The Quake Charmer will cast his Shadow Natural Attack on a Wizard who joins the battle after the first Round.
This cheat is followed up by the "Boom Chick-a-Boom" cheat.
"Let's shake things up!" - At the end of the first Round and once again at the end of the fifth Round, The Quake Charmer will cast an Earthquake.

"Makin' with the quakin'" - At the end of the third Round and every three Rounds after that, The Quake Charmer will cast a Balance Spell that deals 1,000 Balance Damage after three rounds. No damage is taken during the first two rounds and activates on the third round.
"What's shakin'?" - Casts an Earthquake dealing 145 Myth Damage removing one positive Charm and one positive Ward.

"BOOM-CHICKA-BOOM!" - The Quake Charmer will say this after casting his Natural Attacks, but only his Shadow Natural Attack will trigger an Immolate spell that removes one positive Charm and one positive Ward.
spellNotes: Casts a +30% version of Mass Hex.

name: The Rat
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - Wizards who join the duel after the battle has started, will be punished with a +60% Fire, Ice, and Global Trap, as well as a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that transfers all negative hanging effects on The Rat to the late Wizard.
"Fool me once?" - If a Feint is placed on The Rat, he will shout this as a warning.

"Can't get fooled again!" - If a Feint has already been placed on The Rat, any subsequent attempts to Feint him will trigger a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that removes all negative hanging effects on him, and places them onto the Wizard who cast the Feint.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint will not be removed, but will instead summon a Titan Experiment 07 minion as per The Rat's "The Spiral is a disease, we have the cure!" cheat.
Minions
As long as there are Minions occupying all three enemy spots, the Rat will be unable to summon additional Minions in response to a trigger cast until a spot opens up.

"The Spiral is a disease, we have the cure!" - If a Wizard casts a Trap spell, The Rat will cast a Death Spell to summon a Titan Experiment 07 Minion with 1 Health 103% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 3% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
This can be dispelled with Strangle, preventing the Minion from being summoned.

On its first turn, the Minion will cast a Plague (-25%), then polymorph to have 10 Health and 105% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 5% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its second turn, the Minion will cast a Mass Infection, and polymorph to have 100 Health and 107% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 7% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
On its third turn, the Minion will cast a multi-target all enemies Bad Juju (2 Pip), then self-destruct.
Mass Feint will trigger these cheats.
Mockenspiel will trigger these cheats.
Spells that place negative charms will also trigger these cheats.

"You call that healing? Behold my creation!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, The Rat will summon a Titan Experiment 34 Minion with 1 Health and 103% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 3% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its first turn, the Minion will cast Guiding Light and Sanctuary, then polymorph to have 10 Health and 105% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 5% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its second turn, the Minion will either cast Regenerate (225+1,100), if The Rat has been injured, or pass if he is uninjured. It will then polymorph to have 100 Health 107% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 7% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
On its third turn, the Minion will either cast Rebirth (730, 100%) (if The Rat has been injured), or Spinysaur on a random Wizard (if The Rat is uninjured), then self-destruct.
"Say bye-bye to my friend, and hello to your doom!" - If the Minion used Rebirth, The Rat will also cast a Doom and Gloom (-75%).
When a defeated Minion is affected by Rebirth, it will be revived for an additional Round. The revived Minion will always cast the same spell it used previous to its defeat.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
Spells such as Link and Power Link will not trigger this cheat.

"You have blades, but I have Borealis!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global spell, The Rat will cast a Storm Spell to summon a Titan Experiment 291 Minion with 1 Health and 103% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 3% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
This can be dispelled with Dissipate, preventing the Minion from being summoned.

On its first turn, the Minion will cast Windstorm (+25%), and polymorph to have 10 Health and 105% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 5% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its second turn, the Minion will cast Wild Bolt (15-150-1,150) on a random Wizard, and polymorph to have 100 Health and 107% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 7% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
On its third turn, the Minion will cast a multi-target all enemies Insane Bolt (1,125), then self-destruct.
"Borealis is hard to pierce, eh?" - The Rat will shout this whenever a Titan Experiment 291 self-destructs.
Titan Timer

"Down comes the Sky Anchor, and the Spiral with it!" - At the beginning of Rounds 2, 10, and 20, the Titan will attack the Sky Anchor, causing 1,043 Shadow Damage to all Wizards. The Damage will increase for Rounds 10 and 20.
"My Titan will see you disintegrated!" - At the beginning of Rounds 5, 15, and 25, the Titan will deal 1,043 Shadow Damage to all Wizards. The Damage will increase for Rounds 15 and 25.

"And alas, the Anchor falls, the Spiral unravels..." - At the start of Round 30 (and every Round thereafter), the Titan will deal 1,000,000 Shadow Damage with a higher Armor Piercing to all Wizards, thus ending the fight.
spellNotes: Uses Brainfreeze that also stuns all enemies.

name: The Rat (Challenge)
cheats: [Aura] "You are no match for my ambitions" - At the beginning of the first Round, the Rat will cast an Invulnerability Aura on himself.
[Shadow][Damage] "My titan will see you disintegrated!" - Whenever a Minion dies, the Titan will deal 450 Shadow Damage to all Wizards.

[Shadow][Damage] "Down comes the Sky Anchor, and the Spiral with it!" - Whenever the Rat has 7 Shadow Pips, he will use his turn to cast a Shadow Spell that makes the Titan deal ??? Shadow Damage to all Wizards.
[Aura] "Enough! This ends now." - At the end of the Round that all Minions have been defeated, the Rat will cast a Frenzy to replace his Aura.
[Death]
"The Spiral is a disease, we have the cure!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Trap Spell, the Titan Experiment 07 will give a Shadow Pip to The Rat.
[Storm]
"Blades, shields, neither will save you." - Whenever a Wizard casts a Blade or Shield Spell, the Titan Experiment 291 will give a Shadow Pip to The Rat.
[Life]
"Your power is nothing before the Borealis!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Global Spell, the Titan Experiment 34 will give a Shadow Pip to The Rat.

name: The Rat (Husk)
cheats: Tardiness Cheats:
The Rat

"Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins a Round late, The Rat will cast a +60% Fire, Ice, and Universal Trap, and a Shadow-version of Dimension Shift transfering all negative hanging effects on The Rat to the late Wizard.
The Scorpion
"Oh Yes! You will fall too!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, The Scorpion will cast Bad Juju (Shadow) on the Wizard.
Round 1 Cheats:
Starting Round 1, The Scorpion casts a version of Donate Shadow that gives himself 2 Shadow pips.
"Thank you, dear brother" - The Rat then will cast a Shadow spell that steals one Shadow pip from The Scorpion.
"I'll take that, weakling" - The Scorpion will retaliate back casting Steal Pips at The Rat, stealing 2 Pips.
The Rat:
"Fool me once?" - If a Feint is placed on The Rat, he will shout this as a warning.

"Can't get fooled again!" - If a Feint has already been placed on The Rat, any subsequent attempts to Feint him will trigger a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that removes all negative hanging effects on him, and places them onto the Wizard who cast the Feint.
Mass Feint does not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells do not trigger this cheat.
The Scorpion:
"I invoke the power of Shadow!" - At the end of every three rounds, he will cheat-cast Poison (Shadow) to all Wizards.
"Now... your doom!" - At the end of every Round, he will cheat-cast a Shadow version of Detonate to all Wizards, as long as one Wizard has a DoT.
The HoT portion of healing spells, such as from Spritely, can prevent a DoT from detonating when the HoT is positioned before the DoT. The HoT is removed by the Detonate.
"The Rat warned me of that!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on The Scorpion, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all unprotected traps from himself.
"I planned this special for you!" - If a Wizard uses a healing spell, he will cast his Natural Attack at the Wizard.
Pet heals will not trigger this cheat with the exception of Fairy Friend and Happy Accident.
The HoT portion of healing spells, such as from Spritely, will not trigger this cheat.
Retaliation Cheats:
If either The Rat or The Scorpion casts a spell that upsets the other, they'll retaliate and cast the following cheats on each other.
"Fool! Stay out of my way!" - If The Scorpion casts Enfeeble (2 Pip) or Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 1,120), The Rat will cast Scorching Scimitars at The Scorpion.

"You DOLT! I have had enough of you!" - Sometimes after The Scorpion's Iron Sultan (Shadow) cheat aimed at The Rat, he will retaliate by casting Grand Blue Phoenix at The Scorpion, and casting Impede at The Scorpion.
"No! That spell was mine!" - If The Rat casts Steal Charm or Steal Ward, The Scorpion will cast Iron Sultan (Shadow) at The Rat.
"Enough! I'm telling father!" - Sometimes after The Rat's Scorching Scimitars cheat, The Scorpion will cast Impede and Supernova (Shadow) at The Rat.
spellNotes: Uses Brainfreeze that also stuns all enemies.

name: The Reaper
locations: Wizard City: Ravenwood - Hallowe'en Adept Tower

name: The Red Herring
cheats: The inspired cast spells obtained from the 4 bosses surrounding Mr. Ghastly are also sent to The Red Herring's fight.

"Only one school can hurt me. But which one? And why?" - At the beginning of the first Round, The Red Herring will cast a Quasi-Invulnerability Aura, making him immune to six of the seven main Schools. The School he is vulnerable to is random.

At the end of each of the first 6 turns after he casts his Quasi-Invulnerability Aura, The Red Herring will shout out one of the following phrases, each phrase informing Wizards of which Schools of Damage he is immune to. The phrase he does not shout out is the School he is vulnerable to:
[Fire] “I’m steaming mad right now!” - The Red Herring is immune to Fire Damage.
[Ice] “Play it cool. You’ll figure it out.” - The Red Herring is immune to Ice Damage.
[Storm] “Even the fish of the seas fear the tempest.” - The Red Herring is immune to Storm Damage.
[Myth] “I sure hope you don’t CONJURE the solution…” - The Red Herring is immune to Myth Damage.
[Life] “What is the secret of Life?” - The Red Herring is immune to Life Damage.
[Death] “I have a sudden fear of my own mortality!” - The Red Herring is immune to Death Damage.
[Balance] “I hate sand. Too dry." - The Red Herring is immune to Balance Damage.

"Wrong! Oh, were you misled by a... Red Herring?" - If The Red Herring is hit by Damage from a School he is immune to, he will say this and cheat cast a Shadow-Enhanced AOE based on the School he was hit with on all Wizards.

"Really? Of course I thought of that. Reset!" - If a Dispel is cast on The Red Herring or The Red Herring's Quasi-Invulnerability Aura is removed, The Red Herring will remove all negative Charms from himself, cast a new Quasi-Invulnerability Aura, and cheat-cast a version of Glowbug Squall that deals Damage equivalent to 50% of all Wizards' maximum Health.

"You are late. How embarrassing. " - If a Wizard joins after the first turn, The Red Herring will cheat cast a version of Storm Shark that deals Damage equivalent to 25% of that Wizard's maximum Health.
spellNotes: This boss casts a version of Tempest that costs 4 Pips.
This boss casts a version of Storm Lord that doesn't Stun.

name: The Shedder
locations: Wizard City: The Drains - Shedder's Lair
cheats: ”Your tardiness is disgusting!” - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, the Shedder will cast his Natural Attack on the late Wizard, then cast Stun (Shadow) on the Wizard, which lasts for two Rounds.
When the Shedder is hit with a Damage Spell, he will take 0 Damage due to his immunity. Then, he will damage himself for 1 Damage.
Multi-hitting Spells, such as Minotaur and Hydra, will trigger this cheat multiple times.
Damage over Time will not trigger this cheat.
This cheat also affects the Boss when he is Polymorphed as well removing 6% of his total Health (This may be a Bug).

When the Shedder is reduced to 1 Health, he Polymorphs into an enlarged version of himself, giving him 56,000 Health as well as removing his Damage immunity and the rest of his stats. He will also clear all Charms from himself.
Using Multi-hitting Spells such as Hydra can be used to skip the Polymorph stage and defeat the Shedder if cast when his Health is low enough.
After casting his Natural Attack, the Boss will cast Stun (Shadow) on the target that lasts for two Rounds.
On the 1st Round and every 6 Rounds after they've been defeated, The Shedder summons any combination of the following three Minions, with the following cheats:
Hired Hoof (Balance) - If a Wizard casts an Aura spell, Hired Hoof (Balance) will cast Supernova (535, 75%).
Pet May Casts will trigger this cheat.
Hired Hoof (Death) - If a Healing spell is cast, Hired Hoof (Death) will cast Infection on the caster.
Hired Hoof (Fire) - If a Wizard casts a Blade spell, Hired Hoof (Fire) will cast Steal Charm.
Hired Hoof (Ice) - If a Wizard casts a Ward spell, Hired Hoof (Ice) will cast Steal Ward. If Legion Shield or Rebirth is cast, it will Steal Ward from the caster.
Hired Hoof (Life) - If a Wizard casts a Damage Over Time (DOT) spell, Hired Hoof (Life) will cast Mass Triage.
Hired Hoof (Myth) - If a Wizard casts a Trap spell, Hired Hoof (Myth) will cast Cleanse Ward.
Hired Hoof (Storm) - If a Wizard casts a Weakness spell, Hired Hoof (Storm) will cast Cleanse Charm.
His Natural Attack? can stun for 2 rounds. (needs confirmation)

name: The Writer
locations: Lemuria: Night Forest - Third Story Tomb
cheats: "How can you follow the plot if you're late?" - The Writer will cast a Star Spell to confuse the late Wizard for three Rounds.
"Look upon my works and despair" - The Writer will cast a Star Spell to summon three Minions at random.
Blank Pages, which target the Wizard's team.
Unhelpful Blank Pages, which target The Writer's team.

"At least my work will live on!" - The Writer will cast a -200% Brace which states Invulnerability on each of his minions.
If a Minion is defeated and revived on the same turn, it will lose its Invulnerability aura.
"No, this isn't ready for feedback!" - If The Writer is defeated, on the next round, the Minions will each use their turn to cast Furnace (6 Rounds), replacing their Brace Auras.
If a Minion fails to cast Furnace (6 Rounds), it will continue to re-cast it the following rounds until it succeeds.
Cheat Cycle:
"Listen to my story. What do you feel?" - At the end of every turn, The Writer will cast a Shadow Spell that applies two of the following random AoE affects, one to each team:
+100% Damage Charm
+100% Accuracy Charm
4 Pip Gain
(Boss' Allies Only) Restore 10% of target's Health
Due to this being a percentage heal, all charms, wards, and stats do not affect this.
-100% Damage Charm
-100% Accuracy Charm
4 Pip Loss
(Wizards Only) Remove 10% of target's Health
Due to this being percentage damage, all charms, wards, and stats do not affect this.
"This script makes no sense" - The Writer will Confuse one of his Minions for two Rounds.
"Time for a rewrite" - The Writer will Sacrifice one of his Minions and heal himself for 2,000 Health.
"Ugh, Fans!" - The Writer will cast Beguile on a Wizard.

name: Thoth
locations: Krokotopia: Grand Exposition - The Great Lie-Brary
cheats: [Round] "Let me tell you a tale of one city..." - Thoth says this at the beginning of the first Round.

[Round] Story Cycle: Thoth will say a line that references one of the gods, and give Wizards two Aura Spells of different Schools. Casting the Aura that matches the School of the god mentioned will progress the story. At the end of the second Round, he will cheat-cast a Spell of the god's School.
[Aura] After a Wizard casts an Aura, one of the following will occur:

"Someone has been paying attention." - If a Wizard casts the correct Aura, they will take 0 Damage from the cheat-casted Spell. Then, Thoth will cast a version of Sacrifice, dealing 25% max Health Damage to himself.
"Hmm, lost my place." - If a Wizard fails to cast the correct Aura, Thoth will follow up with his Natural Attack, dealing 700 and 1300 Myth Damage.

[Spell] "Ra came down to smite the Moon Children..." - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. The next Round, he will cast a version of Ra, dealing 0 Balance Damage and applying a -45% Weakness blade.
[Next Verse (Balance)][Next Verse (Fire)]
Correct Answer: Balance
[Spell] "Shu's wrath shook the earth itself..." - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. Two Rounds later, he will cast an Ice version of Earthquake (525) that does not remove weaknesses.
[Next Verse (Ice)][Next Verse (Myth)]
Correct Answer: Ice

[Spell] "And thus did my tales rebirth history into mythology!" - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. Two Rounds later, he will cast a Myth version of Rebirth that restores 0 Health and applies a 600 Absorb Ward.
[Next Verse (Myth)][Next Verse (Life)]
Correct Answer: Myth
[Spell] "Heqet called her Sirens from the deep!" - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. Two Rounds later, he will cast a version of Sirens, dealing 685 Storm Damage.
[Next Verse (Storm)][Next Verse (Ice)]
Correct Answer: Storm
It is not actually necessary to progress through Thoth's story to defeat him.

This increases by 5% each Round, up to a maximum of 40%.

cheats: "The Tiddalik consumes. It's ever so sad.":
At the beginning of Round 1, and 3 Rounds after his Shield is removed, Tiddalik casts a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward can not be used to remove the Shield. It will trigger the "That's not funny!" cheat.
If the Shield is broken by one of the above Spells or an All Enemies Spell, Tiddalik will recast it once.
If the Shield is broken by a single-target Damage Spell, Tiddalik will recast it twice.
Occasionally, if a Wizard has a Blade or Shield, Tiddalik will cast Steal Charm or Steal Ward, respectively.

[Late] "You should be ashamed!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Tiki will cast Guiding Light on his teammate(s) and an Aegis version of Spirit Armor on a random ally.
[Damage over Time] "Let's spice things up!" - If a DoT is cast on anyone in the duel, he will cast Lifeblade, remove the DoT, and heal with Sprite Swarm (2 Pip).

Ricky
[Late] "Cheater!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, then Ricky will triple-cast Healing Current on himself, healing 100, 400, then 1,000 Health.

If a multi-target blade spell is used (e.g. Bladestorm), only the Wizard who cast the Spell will be affected.
Steal Charm can be used to steal back the Blade without a penalty.

name: Tim-tim Snakeeye
cheats: Tim-tim Snakeeye will interrupt-cast a Tower Shield (-90%) at the start of the duel.
Tim-tim replaces this Shield by interrupt each time it is removed by Damage.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield, and Tim-tim will not replace the Shield until he is damaged.
Tim-tim will not replace the Shield until after the end of a multi-hit attack, like Hydra.

"Feel better? Now I'll show you my stuff!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will use Pierce and remove his Tower Shield. This indicates to hit the boss during the next Round, else he'll recast Tower Shield after that Round.
Pet Spells, Pet Talent Cards (e.g. Heart o'Pixie), and subsequent Rounds of Heal over Time Spells (e.g. Sprite) will not affect this cheat.
Although Pierce is not cast, it can be Dispelled.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: "You're late! Or are you early?" - Casts a Stun (Shadow) on any Wizard who joins the duel after the first Round.
Every two Rounds, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) will cast a Shadow version of Empower to give 1 Power Pip to all allies, and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the duel.
If a Feint is placed on Time Butterfly (Tier 1), then it will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast it.
If Time Butterfly (Tier 1) is defeated before or on the same turn that all of the Minions are defeated, then it will cast a Sun version of Blessing to restore 100% Health on all allies.
This Spell will revive any Minions defeated during the Round.
Minion Summon Cycle:
At the start of the battle, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) will cast a Shadow Spell to summon Young Morganthe (Tier 1). Occasionally, three of the Minions will be summoned.
On the following Round after Young Morganthe (Tier 1) has been defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) casts a Shadow Spell to summon Young Morganthe (Tier 1) and Roberto (Tier 1).

On the following Round after Young Morganthe (Tier 1) and Roberto (Tier 1) have been defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) casts a Shadow Spell to summon Young Morganthe (Tier 1), Roberto (Tier 1) and Broodmother (Tier 1).

On the first Round, three Young Morganthe (Tier 1) will be summoned instead of just one. In this case, the Stage 2 summon will be omitted, and the Round after the three Minions are defeated, the Stage 3 summon will occur.

If Young Morganthe (Tier 4) and Roberto (Tier 4) are defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 4) will resummon both of them, and also Broodmother (Tier 4) on the following turn.
[Death] Young Morganthe:
[Pip] "Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 3) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects more than 7 Pips.
[Ward] "No More of That!" - If a Ward is used, she'll use Power Nova, followed by Earthquake dealing 400 Myth damage to all enemies, and 100 Fire Damage to self.
[Fire] Broodmother:
[Steal] "Give me your health, drone!" - Every 3 Rounds Broodmother (Tier 3) will cast Cheat to steal 15% max Health from Time Butterfly and restores 15% of its max Health
Sometimes after this cheat, a Dragonblade is cast on a random ally, and Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) (-55%) to all allies.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 5)
cheats: "You're Late, or Are You Early?" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will use Stun (Shadow) that will last two turns.
Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will cast a Mass Shadow version of Empower on itself and its allies to gain 1 Power Pip and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the battle.
If a Feint is placed on Time Butterfly (Tier 5), then it will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast it.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
Minions:

Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will summon Minions cast as Shadow, starting with Young Morganthe (Tier 5) on the first turn. Occasionally, there will be three Minions summoned on the first turn rather than just one, in which case all three Minions will be resummoned once.

If Time Butterfly (Tier 5) is defeated before or on the same turn that all of the Minions are defeated, then it will revive itself and all of the Minions by casting a Mass Sun version of Blessing that restores 100% Health.

If all Minions in battle are defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 5), then Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will re-summon the defeated Minions along with the next Minion with a Shadow Spell on the following turn. The Minions are summoned in order:
Example: (Morganthe, Morganthe & Roberto, Morganthe, Roberto, and Broodmother)
Young Morganthe (Tier 5)
Roberto (Tier 5)
Broodmother (Tier 5)
When all three Minions have been defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 5), then Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will stop respawning itself and its allies.
If Time Butterfly (Tier 5) is defeated on any turn it summons Minions, then it will not revive itself. It is unclear if this is a bug or not.
Young Morganthe:
"Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 5) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects more than 7 Pips.
"No More of That!" - If a Ward is used, she'll use Power Nova, followed by Earthquake dealing 400 Myth damage to all enemies, and 100 Fire Damage to self.
Broodmother:

"Give me your health, drone!" - Broodmother (Tier 5) will cast Cheat to steal 15% max Health from Time Butterfly and restore 15% of its max Health, then a Dragonblade to a random ally, and Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) (-55%) to all allies. "Need to verify which Round this trigger happens."
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage for 4 Pips maximum.

name: Titan's Trident (Challenge)
cheats: On the first Round, Titan's Trident will interrupt-cast an Equalize Global which sets the Damage cap to 949,999. The Trident will recast the Global when it is replaced.
Joining the battle late (after Round 1) will cause the Storm Titan to interrupt-cast Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 2,250) on the Wizard, and summons a random Refraction of the following.
Deadly Ice Refraction
Mythic Fire Refraction
Stormy Life Refraction
The Storm Titan will pull up the Trident and slam it down randomly, removing all unprotected Wards and Charms from itself.

The Storm Titan will randomly laugh at the end of a Round, and at the end of the following Round he will shoot lasers from his eyes, dealing 1,375 Shadow Damage to all and removing a Shadow Pip from all Wizards.

The Storm Titan will pull up the Trident at the end of the Round that it gets its fifth Shadow Pip, which will render the Trident untargetable. While in this state, the Boss can only pass or cast Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 2,250). At the end of the third Round after it was pulled up, the Storm Titan will swing the Trident, dealing 5,500 Shadow Damage to all Wizards, and return it to the battle.
While the Trident is gone from the battle, the Storm Titan can still laugh, and subsequently use his laser eye attack the following Round.

Mellori - "Aww, you gotta be kidding me! The Paradox Chain just keeps charging him back up! Look out!" - After taking 20,000 Damage, the Storm Titan will emerge at the end of the Round and completely restore the Trident's health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
When the Trident is healed, it summons Refractions depending on the number of Wizards present. (Confirmation needed)
Mellori - "The Paradox Chain just keeps recharging him. This is hopeless!"

Bat - "No it's not! I know what we have to do now! Wizard, just keep attacking!" - After taking another 20,000 Damage, the Storm Titan will again completely restore the Trident's health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
The Trident's healing will summon more Refractions.
After the Trident takes 20,000 Damage for a third time, a cutscene will occur where Mellori breaks the Paradox Chain, destroying the Trident, and defeating the Boss.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Stormspear and Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).

Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 2,250) is not a Glowbug Squall, but rather the Trident's special move. When cast, the Storm Titan appears behind a Wizard and squeezes them, dealing massive Damage. Despite what the Spell text indicates, this is a single-target Spell.

name: Titan's Trident (Rematch, Challenge)
cheats: [Storm][Aura] - At the beginning of the first Round, the Titan's Trident will cast a Storm's Protection Aura on itself that greatly reduces all incoming Damage.
[Death][Ice]
"The storm stirs with Death and Ice magic." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Death or Ice Damage, it will say this.
"Death and Ice fuse into a roil." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Death or Ice Damage for a second time, it will say this.
"A refraction emerges from the storm." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Death or Ice Damage for a third time, it will summon a Deadly Ice Refraction (Rematch, Challenge).
At the beginning of the first Round that the Deadly Ice Refraction is in combat, it will cast Mass Weakness.
At the beginning of the third Round that the Deadly Ice Refraction is in combat, it will cast a Shield on itself that limits all incoming Damage to 50.

When the Deadly Ice Refraction is defeated, the Titan's Trident will replace its Aura with a Death's Refraction Aura on itself and cast a version of Scion of Ice on itself that deals 24,999 Damage. It will then re-cast its Storm's Protection Aura on itself.
[Fire][Myth]
"The storm stirs with Fire and Myth magic." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Fire or Myth Damage, it will say this.
"Fire and Myth fuse into a roil." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Fire or Myth Damage for a second time, it will say this.
"A refraction emerges from the storm." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Fire or Myth Damage for a third time, it will summon a Mythic Fire Refraction (Rematch, Challenge).
At the beginning of the first Round that the Mythic Fire Refraction is in combat, it will cast Mass Fire Trap.
At the beginning of the second Round that the Mythic Fire Refraction is in combat, it will cast Fire Dragon.

When the Mythic Fire Refraction is defeated, the Titan's Trident will replace its Aura with a Myth's Refraction Aura on itself and cast a version of Scion of Fire on itself that deals 24,999 Damage. It will then re-cast its Storm's Protection Aura on itself.
[Storm][Life]
"The storm stirs with Storm and Life magic." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Storm or Life Damage, it will say this.
"Storm and Life fuse into a roil." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Storm or Life Damage for a second time, it will say this.
"A refraction emerges from the storm." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Storm or Life Damage for a third time, it will summon a Stormy Life Refraction (Rematch, Challenge).
After the Stormy Life Refraction casts a Spell, it will cast Balanceblade (+35%) on itself.

When the Stormy Life Refraction is defeated, the Titan's Trident will replace its Aura with a Life's Refraction Aura on itself and cast a version of Scion of Storm on itself that deals 24,999 Damage. It will then re-cast its Storm's Protection Aura on itself.
[Aura] "The storm breaks." - When all three Scion Spells have been cast, the Titan's Trident will remove its Storm's Protection Aura and become vulnerable to Damage.
At the beginning of the Round after all Minions have been defeated, the Titan's Trident will cast Shadow Trap on all enemies, or Spirit Armor (500) on itself.
spellNotes: Casts a protected version of Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).

name: Titan's Trident (Standard)
cheats: On the first Round, Titan's Trident will cast an Equalize Global which sets the Damage cap to 7,999, and will recast it when replaced.
Unbalance can dispel this cheat.
If a Wizard uses a Nightbringer Enchanted Attack and changes the Global the next Round, the Global will not be changed back until after the next hit.

Mellori - "Aww, you gotta be kidding me! The Paradox Chain just keeps charging him back up! Look out!" - After taking more than 7,750 Damage, the Storm Titan will emerge at the end of the Round and completely restore the Trident's Health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
Mellori - "The Paradox Chain just keeps recharging him. This is hopeless!"

Bat - "No it's not! I know what we have to do now! Wizard, just keep attacking!" - After taking another 7,750 Damage, the Storm Titan will again completely restore the Trident's Health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
After the Trident takes 7,750 Damage for a third time, a cutscene will occur where Mellori breaks the Paradox Chain, destroying the Trident, and defeating the Boss.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Stormspear and Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow)

Tree Root (Death Elite)

name: Treemugger
cheats: "More Wizards sprouting? Such a tree thing to do" If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, Treemugger casts Stun (Shadow) on the late Wizard.

"I will break this, with my rage!" - At the start of the duel, Treemugger will use a Shadow Spell to Remove all Pips from all Wizards, impose a 99-Round version of Impede, and use Blinding Light (85%).
"One-two PUNCH!" - At the end of every Odd Round, Treemugger will say this.
"Three-four PUNCH!" - At the end of every Even Round, Treemugger will say this.
In both these stages he will remove all existing Pips and use individual Donate Power Spells to sequentially increase the number of Pips based on the number of Rounds which have elapsed.
For example, on the first Round, each Wizard is given one white Pip; second Round, two white Pips, etc., until each Wizard is given seven Power Pips.
During the stage when Power Pips are given, even though all Pips get removed during the animation, the single pips return back to the Wizard when the next Round starts.

"Make like a tree and fall down!" - If a Wizard doesn't have an Impede active on them and any Wizard in the fight casts any spell, Treemugger will cast the 99-Round Impede on all Wizards without an Impede on them.
Replacing the Aura will allow the Wizard to regain Pips, until any Wizard casts another spell, repeating this cheat.

name: Trengil Hoarder
cheats: [Late] "*Eyes Glower*" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, he will cast Steal Pips (Moon) stealing 1 Pip from the Wizard.

[Blade][Shield] "*Eyes Widen*" / "*Eyes Shine*" - At the end of every Round if a positive Charm or Ward is cast, the Trengil Hoarder will cast an AoE Steal Charm (Shadow) that steals one positive Charm (including Pierce Charms), or an AoE Steal Ward (Shadow) that steals one Shield from all Wizards.
Traps and Stun Block will trigger the AoE Steal Ward (Shadow).
Using Steal Charm and Steal Ward on Trengil Hoarder won't trigger this cheat.
Aegis can be used to prevent the steal.
[Shadow][Manipulation] After casting his natural attack, Trengil Hoarder will use Steal Charm (Shadow) and Steal Ward (Shadow) on it's target.
[Shadow][Manipulation] "*Eyes Sparkle*" - If the Trengil Hoarder casts Bladestorm or Legion Shield, he will cast Steal Charm (Shadow) or Steal Ward (Shadow) from his Minion.

name: Trickster Jack
cheats: [Pet][Shield] "Protect Me!" His Pet Wartle will cast a Spirit Shield Item Card.

name: Tse-Tse Snaketail (Challenge)
cheats: "I am the master of disguise!" - At the end of the 1st Round, Tse-Tse Snaketail will Polymorph into one of the following listed below.
"Curses! Let's try another guise" - After Tse-Tse Snaketail is defeated the first time, he will cast a version of Blessing that restores 100% of his health. This cheat only activates once.
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tim-tim Snakeeye (Challenge) - Elephant Polymorph Transformation
After Polymorphing, Tim-tim Snakeeye will cast an Aura that limits incoming damage on himself to a max of 100 for 99 Rounds.

"An elephant never forgets, do you?" - At the beginning of the 2nd Round, and every 2 Rounds after, Tim-tim Snakeeye will cast 3 different Blades on himself. This indicates the order of which schools attacks he will need to be hit by. The 3 Blades will always be one of the following possibilities:
[Death] [Life] [Myth]
[Fire] [Ice] [Storm]
[Balance] [Myth] [Ice]
The progress made into finishing the 3 hits will not be reset when Tim-tim Snakeeye recasts the Blades every 2 Rounds.
"You Remembered!" - Tim-tim Snakeeye will say this after each successful hit in the code.
"Your memory is perfect!" - After getting the code correct, Tim-tim Snakeeye will replace his damage limit aura with Frenzy.
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tom-tom Snakeskin (Challenge) - Rhino Polymorph Transformation

"This is gonna hurt!" - At the beginning of the first Round of this Polymorph, Tom-tom Snakeskin will cast a +15% per Pip version of Supercharge, a +10% per Pip version of Supercharge and a +35% Stormblade on himself.
"Charge!" - At the end of the first Round, Tom-tom Snakeskin will cast a non-dispellable version of Storm Owl (Tier 5a).
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tik-tik Snaketongue (Challenge) - Lion Polymorph Transformation
"Just a little catnap..." - If Tik-tik Snaketongue receives any damage during this Polymorph, he will cast a version of Cat Nap that restores 100% max health. This cheat only activates once.
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tse-Tse Snaketail (Challenge) - Spider Polymorph Transformation (3 [Round])
At the end of each Round, Tse-Tse Snaketail will cast a unique +30% Shadowblade that stacks with all of his other Shadowblades.

"The Shadow Queen sends her regards!" - At the end of the third Round of this Polymorph, Tse-Tse Snaketail will cast Dark Fiend and a version of Sacrifice on himself that deals 100% damage and restores no health, defeating him.

name: Tse-Tse Snaketail (Standard)
cheats: [Polymorph] "I am the master of disguise!" - At the end of the 1st round, Snaketail will Polymorph into one of the following listed below:
[Polymorph]Tim-tim Snakeeye (Standard) - Elephant Polymorph Transformation
After Polymorphing, he will cast an Aura that limits incoming damage on himself to a max of 100 for 99 Rounds.

This aura will be cast again if a wizard attempts to replace it with a negative aura. However, if he removes it by using another aura such as Galvanic Field, the effects of the aura will be removed and will not be recast.

"An elephant never forgets, do you?" - At the beginning of the 2nd round, and every 2 rounds after, Snakeeye will cast 3 Blades of different Schools on himself. This indicates the order of which schools attacks he will need to be hit by. The 3 Blades will always be one of the following possibilities:
[Death] [Life] [Myth]
[Fire] [Ice] [Storm]
[Balance] [Myth] [Ice]
The progress made into finishing the 3 hits will not be reset when Snakeeye recasts the Blades every 2 rounds.
"You Remembered!" - Snakeeye will say this after each successful hit in the code.
"Your memory is perfect!" - After getting the code correct, Snakeeye will replace his damage limit aura with a Frenzy.
[Polymorph] Tom-tom Snakeskin (Standard) - Rhino Polymorph Transformation
"This is gonna hurt!" - At the beginning of the round after Snaketail revived, he will cast a +15% version of Supercharge, a Supercharge and +35% Stormblade on himself.
"Charge!" - At the end of the round where the above cheat is used, Snaketail will cast No Dispel version of Storm Owl (Tier 5a)
[Polymorph] Tik-tik Snaketongue (Standard) - Lion Polymorph Transformation
"Just a little catnap..." - Casts a version of Cat Nap that restores 100% max health. Activates when he is attacked, once per round. This cheat can revive the boss.
[Polymorph] Tse-Tse Snaketail (Standard) - Spider Polymorph Transformation
This Polymorph is immune to damage
This Polymorph only lasts 3 rounds, and each round Snaketail will cast a Shadowblade which stacks.

"The Shadow Queen sends her regards!" - At the end of the third round after the Spider Polymorph was activated, Snaketail will cast Dark Fiend and will use a 100% Max Health version of Sacrifice on himself.

name: Tumbler
cheats: "Delay .. recorded!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Tumbler will cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"New Target Acquired!" - If a Wizard returns to the duel after fleeing, Tumbler will cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.

"Offense Mode Engage" - At the start of the Round, Tumbler gets a +100% Berserk Aura for 98 Rounds, increased by an additional +100% for each Wizard beyond the first in the duel, up to a maximum of +400%.

"Defense Mode Engage" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, it changes to a -100% Brace Aura on self for 98 Rounds, increased by an additional -5% for each Wizard beyond the first in the duel, up to a maximum of -115%.
The Auras cycle every three Rounds after the fourth Round.
"Anomaly detected...corrected" If a Wizard removes Tumbler's Aura, Tumbler will recast it.

name: Tymen WhiteFlame
cheats: At the beginning of every third Round, the Wizard with the most Pips will be hit with Mana Burn.

If a Wizard has already queued a Spell to be cast before being hit by the Mana Burn, then the Spell will finish, regardless of how many Pips are available at the time it is cast.
If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will Stun the Wizard for two Rounds.
Pet Heals and Item Cards received from Pets will not trigger this cheat.
Guardian Spirit will trigger this cheat.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 1)
cheats: The boss cheat casts a Humongofrog spell when the Grendel Ransacker (Tier 1) is defeated at the beginning of the next round.
The boss cheat casts a Blinding Light after the Grendel Cracknail (Tier 1) or Grendel Scavenger (Tier 1) is defeated at the beginning of the next round.
Boss will also casts these cheats if he does not begin a battle with these minions.
All cheat-spells will be casted at the same time if all minions are defeated on one round.
If Boss starts out with no minions, all cheat-spells will be casted at the same time on first Round.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker Minion casts a Tower Shield (Sun -55%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +30%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger Minion casts a Sprite (50+250), starting on Round 3.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 2)
cheats: This Boss has Resistance to the Myth and Death Schools and has Boost to the Storm and Life Schools.
This Boss will cheat cast Spells dependant on if a Minion dies, and which Minion dies.
Once his Grendel Ransacker (Tier 2) or Grendel Scavenger (Tier 2) Minion is defeated, he will cheat cast Blinding Light the next Round.
Once his Grendel Cracknail (Tier 2) Minion is defeated, he will cheat cast an enhanced version of Humongofrog the next Round.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker Minion casts a Tower Shield (Sun -55%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +35%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger Minion casts a Sprite (75+270), starting on Round 3.

locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 3)
cheats: Upon killing each of his Minions a spell triggers.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following spells on him.

His Grendel Cracknail Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +40%), starting on Round 2.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 4)
cheats: Upon Defeating each of Tyrfing's Minions, an interrupt Spell will trigger.
Upon Defeating the Grendel Ransacker (Tier 4), Tyrfing will cast 325 + 351 version of Deer Knight.
Upon Defeating the Grendel Cracknail (Tier 4), Tyrfing will cast a 300 + 300 version of Deer Knight.
Upon Defeating the Grendel Scavenger (Tier 4), Tyrfing will cast Blinding Light.
Defeating all three of Tyrfing's Minions in one Round will trigger all three of these Spells to interrupt in succession.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following Spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker (Tier 4) casts a Tower Shield (Sun -70%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail (Tier 4) casts a Dragonblade (Sun +45%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger (Tier 4) casts a Regenerate (60+1,130), starting on Round 3.

name: Valencian Naval Fleet (Standard)
cheats: After each Boss is defeated, another random Naval Fleet Boss will respawn in its place. This continues until 4 new Fleets have been summoned.
If all four Bosses are defeated in the same round, one will be healed instead of summoning a fourth.
Doom Moon
The Doom Moon will charge it's laser for each Hanging Effect, and will shoot a laser beam on all enemies after a condition is met.
This laser will use the Balanceblade from Marleybonian Naval Fleet's Justiceblade.
The laser will be fired per condition met, and then all hanging effects used to meet the condition will be removed.
Protections can not prevent removal of any hanging effects.
4 Blades on Wizards -> Dog Tracy
4 Negative Charms on Enemies -> Solomon Crane
4 Shields on Wizards -> Duck Savage
4 Traps on Enemies -> Mandar the Barbarian
2 Damage Overtimes on Enemies -> Buck Gordon
2 Heal Overtimes on Wizards -> The Bantam
2 Blades on Wizards and 2 Negative Charms on Enemies -> Stallion Quartermane
2 Shields on Wizards and 2 Traps on Enemies -> Stallion Quartermane
2 Uses of Shadow-Enhanced Spells by Wizards -> The Shadoe
Note: Charge Up cheats have no function. In order to shoot the Doom Moon laser, the Gambit Conditions must be met.
Dog Tracy: - (4 [Blade])
"We're fleas biting at a rhino. Need more punch..." - Charge Up.
"Power rerouted to weapons!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and clears all Damage Blades from all Wizards.
Solomon Crane: - (4 [Curse])
"The machines have identified weak points..." - Charge Up.
"Target these areas for increased effect!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage and clears all negative Charms from all enemies.
Duck Savage: - (4 [Ward])
"Need some help? Your shields need reinforcement..." - Charge Up.
"Let's polarize the hull with... bronze!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and clears all positive Wards from all Wizards.
Mandar the Barbarian: - (4 [Trap])
"Bah, only fools fall into traps..." - Charge Up.
Frenzy may also trigger this cheat.
"By the power of Mandar!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to and clears all Damage Traps from all enemies.
Buck Gordon: - (2 [Damage over Time])
"Sorry I'm late. You'll see why..." - Charge Up.
"Took a detour for some sabotage." - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to and removes all Damage over Time effects from all enemies.
The Bantam: - (2 [Heal over Time])
"Damage reports from all decks..." - Charge Up
"Oh, what's this button do?" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies to and removes all Heal over Time effects from all Wizards.
Heal Overtimes do NOT have to be on the same Wizard for the condition to be met.
Stallion Quartermane: - (2 [Blade] & 2 [Curse])
"Ha-ha! That's how you adventure..." - Charge Up.
"Targeting systems engaged!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and removes all positive Charms from all Wizards and all negative Charms from all enemies.
Stallion Quartermane: - (2 [Ward] & 2 [Trap])
"Targeting systems engaged!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and removes all positive Wards from all Wizards and all negative Wards from all enemies.
The Shadoe: - (2 [Shadow School])
"I sense fear on those ships. Keep going..." - Charge Up.
"The Shadoe Bestows!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and removes all Shadow Pips from all Wizards.

name: Vanitus
cheats: "Help! Save Me!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Vanitus casts Beguile (Star) that lasts three Rounds on the Wizard, and casts a Star Spell to Summon one additional Justicon (Storm). Cannot summon more than three Minions.
"Servants!" - Starting on Round 2, he casts a Star Spell which summons Justicons.
"Justicons! Defend me!" - If attacked, he will casts a Star version of Steal Health without the Heal, to remove all Health on one of his summoned Minions.
"I Surrender!" - Starting on Round 2, if the Boss is attacked without any Minions present, he will interrupt-cast a Spell that defeats himself.
spellNotes: Deals 420 Star Damage for 4 Pips maximum.

name: Verboten Mimic
locations: Mirage: Aggrobah - Vault of the Verboten
cheats: "A surprise for you too!" - If a Wizard is late, he will cheat-cast Steal Pips (Moon) to steal 1 Pip from the Wizard.
"A treasure of tricks!" & "I can change forms all day long!" & "Perhaps this form's too easy?" - Every three Rounds, the Verboten Mimic will Polymorph into one of the forms listed below.
"You can't trick a Mimic!" - He will remove all Traps from himself the first time trapped.
"Oh! I've got an idea!" - He will cast Mystic Colossus.
During this Cycle, the Boss will pass since all these Spells are interrupt.
Round 1 - He will cast a triple Tower Shield on himself, Legion Shield, and Brace.
Round 2 - He will cheat-cast Wings of Fate.
Round 3 - He will cast Blinding Freeze.
"Only attack if you have been inked!" - At the beginning of each Round, he will "Ink" the Wizard in the first spot (Sun spot), which applies a Smoke Mantle.
"Still fighting? Have some more ink!" - After losing 25% of his health, he will begin to ink the Wizard in the second spot (eye).
"If you can still see, don't look at me!" - If a non-inked Wizard uses a single target attack Spell on him, he will cast one of the following Spells.
Poison - 800 + 2,000 Death Damage over 3 Rounds
Power Link - 400 + 1,500 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds & 200 + 800 Heal over Time on itself.
He will remove traps which are used on him.
Indemnity can be used to to avoid this cheat.
If a multi-trap Spell is used, such as Elemental Trap, he will only remove the last trap applied (Storm/Life Trap).
"Protector!" - At the beginning of the first Round, she will summon a Stellar Protector.
She will cast Smoke Screen (-50%) on her first turn and every round when a Wizard successfully casts a Spell.
"The cards align! Now's your chance!" When both Blades and Traps are present in the battle, the cheat will end until they are no longer present.
"Your body and soul are mine once more." - At the end of the first Round, he will summon a Hagakure Blade and a Seijun Killer.
"Again face the poetry of war!" - On any given turn, he will cast a Death Damage over Time Spell that deals 4,000 Death Damage over 10 Rounds.
This can be countered with Triage or Mass Triage.
"The way of the Samoorai is found in death!" - Casts a Detonate (Death) on the target.
"I retain my resolve!" - If a Wizard casts any Ward, he will cast Shatter.
spellNotes: See the respective pages for the spell casts and stats of each of this Boss's Polymorph Forms.

[Shield] "Tower defense is no game." - He will cast Tower Shield on himself.

locations: Darkmoor: Black Lagoon - Lair of the Winged Sorrow
cheats: [Spell] "Music is truth, truth music." - On turn 1, give all wizards a 1-pip Star spell called "Exit Combat" that allows them to leave combat without penalty.
[Global] "Perish in the storm!" - After a wizard sets the brazier to shadow fire, Winged Sorrow will cast a global spell resembling Darkwind and become untargetable.
[Heal] "The thunder is harsh and dangerous!" - If a wizard uses a healing spell while this global is active, Winged Sorrow will heal back to full health.

While this global is active, at the end of each turn, Winged Sorrow will be struck by lightning and gain full power pips. Despite having the visual appearance of a wand hit, this effect deals no damage.
[Global] "Flourish in the sunshine!" - After a wizard sets the brazier to regular fire, Winged Sorrow will cast a global spell resembling Power Play and become targetable.
Beginning on the second round after this global becomes active, each wizard will take damage equal to 25% of their health at the end of each round.

[Round] "The clear day is calm and enchanting" - Activates the turn after the brazier is switched to regular fire and clears all hanging effects from everyone in combat. If a wizard casts any hanging effect or aura while this global is active, this cheat will activate and will immediately clear the effect.
While this global is active, Winged Sorrow will not cast any attack spells, only buffs.
Randomly applies a -50% chromatic Weakness to wizards after they take damage at the end of the round. It is unknown what, if anything, causes this weakness to be cast.

name: Xibalba Elemental
cheats: "What is death can never die!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Xibalba Elemental casts an Equalize Global, setting the Damage cap to 1.
After the first cheat, Xibalba Elemental casts one of the following Invunerability Auras:
[Balance] - "Xibalba brings balance!" - Immune to all schools except Balance for 99 Rounds.
[Fire] - "Xibalba burns!" - Immune to all schools except Storm and Life for 99 Rounds.
[Ice] - "Xibalba freezes!" - Immune to all schools except Fire and Myth for 99 Rounds.
[Storm] - "Xibalba crackles!" - Immune to all schools except Ice and Death for 99 Rounds.
[Death] - "Xibalba is death!" - Immune to all schools except Ice and Myth for 99 Rounds.
[Life] - "Xibalba is life!" - Immune to all schools except Storm and Death for 99 Rounds.
[Myth] - "Xibalba is legend!" - Immune to all schools except Fire and Life for 99 Rounds.
"No!" - If a Wizard uses Supernova, it will deal 1 Damage. Xibalba Elemental will then use Blessing on himself, healing for 100% of his max Health.
Because this cheat deals Damage, the next cheat will be activated.
"Aaaahhhhhh!" - If Xibalba Elemental takes any Damage, he will use a no-Damage version of Supernova on himself, then use Exit Combat on the caster, and change to a new Immunity Aura.
"Death comes for you all!" - At the end of every Round, Xibalba Elemental casts a version of Deer Knight that deals 20% max Health Damage on all Wizards.
"I feel... MORTALITY." - After 7 Aura cycles, Xibalba Elemental will cast Doom and Gloom, removing the Damage cap.
(Needs confirmation on Doom and Gloom version).

name: Yevgeny NightCreeper
cheats: [Blade] "I counter your Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, Yevgeny will cheat-cast Freeze on the Wizard.
[Global] Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
Conviction can be used to try countering this cheat.
[Ward] "You give away your hand!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward Spell (Shields, Absorbs, etc.), he will cheat-cast Pierce on the target of the Ward Spell.
Using a spell that gives the caster a ward, such as Sabertooth, will cause Yevgeny to use this cheat on himself.
"Leave this place!" - The Minions will also cheat-cast Steal Ward, in order to remove any remaining Wards on the Wizard.
[Curse] "I vex you!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm, Yevgeny will cheat-cast Bad Juju on the Wizard.
Secondary Spell effects will trigger this cheat (e.g. Efreet's negative Charm).
Harmful Charms that target all enemies (e.g. Plague) will not trigger this cheat.
Interrupt Cycles
"I wrap you in armor!" - At the beginning of every Round in this sub-cycle, Yevgeny will cast Frozen Armor (185) on himself or one of his Minions.

"Let the cold wind destroy you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this sub-cycle (and every fifteen Rounds after), he will cast Katabatic Wind, Mass Ice Trap, Iceblade (+45%), and Abominable Weaver. This Spell will target the Wizard with the lowest current Health.
"I empower you!" - At the beginning of every Round in this sub-cycle, Yevgeny will cast Buff Minion on himself or one of his Minions.
"Crush my enemies!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this sub-cycle (and every fifteen Rounds after), he will cast Saga of Heroes, Mass Myth Trap, a Mythblade (+40%), and Mystic Colossus.
"I decimate you!" - At the beginning of every Round in this sub-cycle, Yevgeny will cast a Feint (+75%) on a random Wizard.

"Klaatu Barada Nikto!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this sub-cycle (and every fifteen Rounds after), he will will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%), a Deathblade (+45%), Mass Death Trap, and Call of Khrulhu.
"Fear the howl of the moon!" - (Round 3) At the end of the third Round in this cycle, Yevgeny will cast Shift Chaney (3 Pip), dealing 800 Life Damage.
"Fear the bite of Dragonspyre!" - (Round 6) At the end of the sixth Round, he will cast a Fire version of Shift Akhtang, which deals 1,000 Fire Damage.
"Fear the Blackwater!" - (Round 9) At the end of the ninth Round, he will cast a Moon version of Shift Blackwater, which deals 1,200 Moon Damage.

name: Ygor
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Ygor will cast a -100% Virulent Plague that also applies a -100% Black Mantle, and a -100% Infection on the Wizard. Summoned Minions don't trigger this cheat.
(Fanmade Spell to represent this cheat - Impair)
"Please don't hurt me!" - In the first Round, Ygor will cast Breeze Shield which applies a -70% Life and Ice Shield.
"Why are we fighting?" - Every six Rounds, Ygor will cast another Breeze Shield.
"It hurts! Book, please help." - Ygor's Grimoire Pet will cheat-cast Power Link when Ygor or his Minions are attacked.
spellNotes: Ultra Blaze and Ultra Torment are 3 Pip maximum.
His Pet, Ygor's Grimoire, will cast Poison, Deer Knight, Rain of Fire, and Burning Rampage.
